name: Maple Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar with tight-fitting lid; shake well.
Serve over salad greens.

name: Peppy Salad Dressing
directions: Combine pectin, sugar, mustard and pepper.
Stir in water, vinegar and garlic.  Cover and chill for 1 hour.
Chill to store two or three days.
Variations:
Herbed Salad Dressing: Prepare as above, except add 1/8 teaspoon  dried basil, crushed and 1/8 teaspoon paprika to the dry pectin mixture.  Continue as directed.
Parmesan Salad Dressing: Prepare as above, except add 1  tablespoon grated Parmesan cheese and 1/4 tsp. dried oregano, crushed to the dry  pectin mixture. Continue as directed.
Creamy Onion Dressing: Prepare as above  except increase the sugar to 1 tablespoon and stir in 1/4 cup sliced green onion  and 1/4 cup plain Low-Fat yogurt with the water. Continue as directed.

name: Versatile Salad Dressing
directions: In a saucepan, combine sugar, flour, salt and mustard; stir in eggs.
Gradually stir in vinegar and water until smooth.
Bring to a boil over  medium heat, stirring constantly; cook and stir for 2 minutes.
Cover and  refrigerate. Just before serving, combine desired amount of base with an  equal amount of mayonnaise. Serve as a dressing for potato salad,  coleslaw or salad greens.
Refrigerate leftovers.

name: Jade Tree Salad Dressing
directions: Mix first seven ingredients together and refrigerate.
Immediately before serving, add 1/2  cup Parmesan cheese and 1/4 cup sesame seed.
Serve on lettuce with chopped water chestnuts.
Note: I usually add the cheese and sesame seed directly to the salad, so as not to spoil the dressing for later use.

name: Mountain Maple Poppy Dressing
directions: In a large container with a tight-fitting lid, combine all the ingredients, and mix thoroughly.

name: Cooked Salad Dressing
directions: Place sugar, flour, dry mustard, salt, and pepper in the top of double boiler.
Blend in 1 cup water until thoroughly mixed.
Place over direct heat and bring to  a boil, stirring constantly but gently.
Cook 1-2 minutes longer after mixture  comes to a boil.
Stir in vinegar.
Beat the egg yolks slightly then stir 3  tablespoons of the hot mixture into the eggs.
Beat vigorously.
Immediately  blend the egg mixture into the mixture in the top of the double boiler.
Place  over simmering water and cook 3-5 minutes.
Stir slowly to keep mixture cooking  evenly.
Remove from heat and stir in margarine. Cool and store in refrigerator  in tightly covered jar.
Before using, thin to desired consistency with cream,  fruit juice or vinegar.

similar.name: Cooked Potato or Pasta Salad Dressing

name: Mayfair Salad Dressing
directions: A St. Louis tradition. Use a food processor to make dressing.
Blanche onion and celery very briefly (5-10 seconds) in boiling water and drain.
Combine everything except eggs and oil in processor and blend for 2 or 3 seconds.
Add egg and blend again.
Then, with processor on, slowly dribble in oil to make creamy mayonnaise type dressing.
run processor until dressing is very smooth.
Correct salt and pepper seasoning.
This will keep for a week or so if covered tightly and refrigerated.
If Parmesan cheese is used in salad, do not add until the salad is tossed with dressing.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all together and serve over mixed greens.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing II
directions: Mix garlic powder, 1/4 cup salad oil and croutons; let  stand 15 minutes.
Mix remaining ingredients; blend well.
Mix  all together.
Serve over romaine salad.

name: Salad Dressing Dry Mix
directions: Mix salt, garlic powder, seasoning, mustard, paprika, pectin and sugar substitute in jar with tight-fitting lid.  Store in a cool, dry place. 20 cal per tsp.
SALAD DRESSING Whisk dry mix, vinegar, water and oil in a bowl. Refrigerate up to 3 days.
VEGETABLE SALAD  Combine onion, celery, broccoli, tomato, squash, zucchini and cucumbers in large bowl.
Toss with 1/2 cup dressing.
Marinate in refrigerator for 30 minutes.
Spread lettuce over platter.
Spoon vegetables over lettuce.

name: Sweet Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients well.
Drop a whole clove of garlic in dressing (don't cut up).

name: Honey Mustard Salad Dressing II
directions: Mix well.
Delicious on tossed raw vegetables.

similar.name: Better Homes and Gardens Honey Mustard Salad Dressing

name: Honey Mustard Salad Dressing III
directions: Mix all ingredients in bowl. Serve.
Note: Dressing gets tart after refrigeration. Simply add more honey if desired.

name: Buttermilk Salad Dressing Mix
directions: For Salad Dressing: Mix with buttermilk and mayonnaise.  For Dip: Combine the mix with sour cream and mayonnaise.

name: Sadie's Shrimp Salad Dressing
directions: Cook macaroni, drain, and cool.
In a large bowl, mix macaroni, shrimp, eggs, onions, celery, olives, and pickles and toss well.
Make dressing out of mayonnaise, olive oil, hot sauce, lemon juice Worcestershire sauce, and mustard, and ketchup.
Pour over other ingredients and mix well.
Refrigerate 1 hour before serving.
You may have to make more dressing if the salad takes it up.

name: Hidden Valley Mix for Dressing(copycat)
directions: Mix instant onion mix, salt, garlic powder, parsley flakes and M.S.G together  well.
Fill 1/2 cup containers with mixture, putting 3 tablespoons of the mixture into each container.
One container equal 1 envelope of dressing.
Keeps up to 6  months.
To make dressing, combine mayonnaise, buttermilk and 1 container of the  mix.
Stir well wit h a spoon.
Substitute sour cream or yogurt for buttermilk to  make different kinds of dressing.

name: Low Sodium Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a container and shake well pour over your salad.

name: Low Fat Mayonnaise
directions: Whisk together water and cornstarch in a small saucepan.
Stir continously over a medium heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
Continue stirring and boiling for 1-2 minutes or until the mixture is clear.
Pour mixture into a bowl and whisk the remaining ingredients, in order listed.
Whisk.
Refrigerate in a tightly sealed container.
Should keep about 2 weeks.

name: Mustard Pepper Dressing
directions: Whisk first five ingredients together.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Taste and adjust.

name: Tomato-Basil Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients.

similar.name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Shake and serve.
Does not refrigerate well, so make just before serving.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: just mix ingredients together and serve!
So simple.
Add more or less of each ingredient to accomodate individual tastes.
Great for dips, salads, on sandwichs, anything!

name: Easy Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients except poppy seeds into a jar with a lid and shake it until thick and smooth.
Add seeds, shake again and serve.
Store in the fridge and re-shake as it will settle.

name: Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Into a food processor it goes-whirl until smooth.

similar.name: Low Fat Green Goddess Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Italian Garlic Dressing
directions: Into a food processor all ingredients go-whirl till smooth.

similar.name: Creamy Italian Dressing With Parmesan Cheese, Lower Fat
Creamy Parmesan Italian Dressing (Weight Watcher Friendly)

name: Buttermilk Salad Dressing
directions: Mix everything (I put it in a quart canning jar) let stand overnight.
Crumbled blue cheese may be added (Sometimes I add a little cottage cheese, just for texture).

similar.name: Creamy Low Fat Garlic Salad Dressing

name: Del Rancho Garlic Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients until smooth.
Thin mixture with a little milk to desired consistency.
*Remember,if you don't use all within a day or two, the longer the dressing sets, the stronger the garlic tastes.

name: Honey Mustard
directions: Whisk together and chill.

name: My Mom's Salad Dressing
directions: Mix it all together and chill.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: combine all, mix thoroughly.
chill.

name: Zippy Dressing
directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in saucepan gradually add the cold water.
Cook and stir over med heat til thickens.
Remove from heat, Cover with paper towel, to keep skin from forming.
Cool to lukewarm, about 10 minutes.
Beat in remaining ingredients with whisk.
Transfer to container with lid.
Chill.

name: Catalina Dressing
directions: Mix all together and refrigerate.
I freshly grind all spices, makes for better flavor.

name: Easy Red Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a sauce pan.
Simmer 15 min.
then cool, bottle and refrigerate.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Five Star House Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well with wisk.
Refrigerate 1 hour before use.

name: Anything Goes Red Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender combine all ingredients except oil.
Slowly add oil and combine.
Bottle and serve.

name: Pixy Dressing
directions: Blend in a blender until well mixed.
Store this in a refrigerator.

name: Salad Dressing with a Punch
directions: Mix the ingredients together.
Serve over lettuce or thin sliced cabbage.

name: Hidden Valley Original Ranch Dressing Clone
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl and whisk until smooth.
Cover and chill for several hours before using.

similar.name: Homemade Ranch Dressing Mix - Kitchen Shortcut

name: Creamy French Dressing
directions: In a blender, place the mayonnaise, ketchup, vinegar, sugar, onion, salt and pepper; cover and process until smooth.
While processing, gradually add oil in a steady stream.
Let chill in fridge for about an hour before serving to let onion flavor mellow.
Serve over salad.
Refrigerate leftover dressing.

name: Vinegar and Oil Salad Dressing
directions: Mix well and refrigerate for atleast 2 hours.
Shake well before using.

name: Amazing Roquefort Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients, crumbling cheese into small bits.

name: Creamy Dill Dressing
directions: Whisk mayonnaise and sour cream in a medium bowl until smooth.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Season with salt and pepper.
Cover; chill 1 hour to blend flavors.

similar.name: Heavy on the Dill Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Romaine Dressing
directions: In a food processor or blender, add mayonnaise, anchovy paste, garlic cloves, lemon juice, red wine vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and Tabasco sauce.
Process until smooth.
Add grated Parmesan cheese and black pepper and process until well blended.
With processor running on low speed, add olive oil in a steady stream and process until creamy smooth.
Refrigerate in a glass container with lid.
In a large mixing bowl, break Romaine lettuce into bite size pieces.
Use enough creamy romaine dressing to suit your taste and toss to coat lettuce.
Sprinkle each serving with croutons as desired.
Sprinkle each serving with additional grated Parmesan cheese.
Grind some cracked black pepper over salad if desired.

name: Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Combine the sugar, mustard, salt and onion.
Add the vinegar and olive oil and beat well till combined.
Add celery seeds and whisk all together to blend.

name: Dry Ranch Dressing Mix
directions: Put crackers through blender on high speed until powdered.
Add parsley, minced onions, and dill weed.
Blend again until powdered.
Dump into bowl.
Stir in onion salt, garlic salt, onion powder, and garlic powder.
Put into container with tight-fitting lid.

name: Island Lime Dressing
directions: whisk until well blended.
refidgerate until serving.

name: Honey Cumin Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingrediets together well.

name: Citrus Shallot Vinaigrette
directions: If using a vegetable peeler or cocktail zester blanch lemon and lime peel for 30 seconds and then shock in cold water.
Dry in a salad spinner.
If using a rasp zester, combine all of the ingredients-- juice, zest, vinegar, shallots, sugar, oil and red pepper.
If using within a few hours keep covered at room temperature, otherwise refrigerate up to 5 days.
Bring to room temperature before using.

name: Masters Acapulco Salad Dressing
directions: Mince the onion and place in a non-reactive bowl (glass or plastic).
Add lime juice, Worcestershire, soy sauce, Tabasco, mustard and sugar.
Add mayonnaise and olive oil and wisk to combine.
Cover and let sit in refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Note: I put everything in the Vita-Mix and blend- I don't know if a regular blender will work, but perhaps.

name: Hot Bacon Dressing
directions: In heavy skillet over medium heat fry bacon pieces til crispy, stirring around to brown equally.
Add onion, brown sugar, mustard, basil Remove some of the grease if you like.
Stir cornstarch into vinegar add to hot water; add hot water vinegar to bacon mixture, stirring til hot and thickened.
Serve over fresh spinach.

name: Low - Fat ,Spicy Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a large glass jar, shake to mix.
Chill for at least 1 hour, but not more than 3 days.
Shake well before using.

name: French Dressing
directions: Place first 5 ingredients in a bowl.
Beat with rotary beater until blended.
Add oil slowly;continue beating until well blended.
Add garlic clove.
Let stand for one hour, then remove garlic clove.

name: Italian Seasoning Red Wine Vinaigrette
directions: Pour all ingredients in a jar.
Cover and shake well.
Store in the refrigerator up to 2 weeks.
Shake before serving.

name: Chipotle Caesar Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients except oil in blender, pulse to puree, stream in oil slowly to form an emulsion.

name: Caesar Dressing
directions: Put lemon juice, anchovies, garlic, Dijon and vinegar in blender on high.
Slowly drizzle olive oil in while machine is running.
Add remaining ingredients and just blend.

name: Russian Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients, refrigerate.

name: Pennsylvania Dutch Hot Bacon Dressing
directions: Cook bacon until crisp; remove and chop.
Add to greens.
Add all other ingredients to the beaten egg and pour into the hot bacon fat and cook until thickened.
Serve over greens, and add a little bit of chopped onion to salad, if desired.

name: Papaya Seed Dressing
directions: In a food processor or blender, add sugar, dry mustard, salt, papaya seeds, green onions and white vinegar.
Process until smooth.
On low speed, drizzle olive oil and process until well blended.
This dressing is excellent on almost any green salad.
Keep refrigerated in a glass jar with a lid.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Mix Splenda and balsamic vinegar together.
Add olive oil and shake to mix.

name: Parmesan / Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Whisk first 3 ingredients in medium bowl til smooth.
Add Parmesan cheese, worcestershire sauce, vinegar tabasco sauce and garlic.
Fold in blue cheese.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Chill at least 1 hour.

name: Lime Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients,chill and shake well.

name: Ginger Dressing
directions: Soak ginger root in cold water for a few minutes to make it easier to remove outer skin.
Remove skin and cut into quarters.
Combine all ingredients in a food processor with a steel blade and blend until smooth.
(Divide ingredients in half and blend each half separately if food processor will not handle entire recipe.) Refrigerate.

name: Nutty Dressing
directions: Puree ingredients in processor or blender.

name: Italian Salad Dressing Mix
directions: Combine all in a container with a tight fitting lid and shake.
For salad dressing,combine 1 1/2 tbsp mix with1/4 cup vinegar,2 1/2 tbsp water and 3/4 cup oil in a bottle with a lid and shake.
Let stand 1 hour to blend.

name: Honey Miso Salad Dressing
directions: Mix the miso, honey, and mustard together.
Add the remaining ingredients, mix until smooth.
Let sit 15 minutes before using.

name: Ranch Salad Dressing
directions: Put mayonnaise in a mixing bowl.
Gradually whisk buttermilk into mayonnaise and whisk until smooth.
When mixture is creamy add rest of ingredients and mix until well blended; chill.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients except oil in blender and blend until smooth.
With blender running slowly pour in olive oil.
Best if made 1-2 days in advance.
Makes 1-1/2 cups.

name: Cornstarch Mayonnaise
directions: Place in a bowl the unbeaten egg, sugar, salt, dry mustard, paprika, lemon juice and salad oil Prepare the mayonnaise base by blending together the cornstarch and water in a saucepan Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until boiling.
Boil for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat and pour this cream over the egg mixture.
Beat with a beater until smooth and creamy.
Keep refrigerated in a closed jar.

name: Thousand Island Dressing
directions: In a bowl combine yogurt, vegetable oil, tomato sauce, lemon juice, mustard powder, sugar, salt, and pepper.
Whisk together until blended.
Add onion, sweet relish, olives, and jalapeño and whisk to combine evenly.
Chill 1 hour.

name: Citrus Cilantro Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Stir well.
Serve over salad.
Makes 3/4 cup.

name: Green Onion Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Chill one hour befor serving.

name: Del Rancho Garlic Dressing
directions: Mix together all ingredients until smooth then thin with a milk a little at a time until salad dressing consistency.
Can use this dressing on salads, as a vegetable dip, for fried mushrooms/batter fried vegetables.

name: Mild Red Chile Dressing
directions: Soak the dried tomatoes in the hot water for 20 to 30 minutes, or until they become soft and plump.
With a slotted spoon, transfer the tomatoes to a food processor and add the garlic; chop finely.
Pour tomato-soaking water into the processor and blend well.
Add the olive oil or apple juice, orange juice concentrate, 3 tablespoons vinegar, soy sauce, chile powder, pepper and salt if using.
Process until the dressing is creamy.
Taste and add another tablespoon vinegar if needed.
Pour into a jar, cover and refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Ginger Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend on high speed for about 30 seconds or until all of the ginger is well pureed.

name: Beet Vinaigrette
directions: Mix ingredients in a blender until smooth.
Serve over your favourite salades or as a sauce/ dip for filled lettuce wraps.

name: Feta and Olive Oil Salad Dressing
directions: Grate cheeses and set aside.
Mix remaining ingredients together.
Add cheeses and mix well.
Best if prepared the day before serving.

name: Dijon Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients except the wine, vinegar, and oil in a medium bowl.
Beat in wine and vinegar.
Whisk in oil in a slow steady stream.
Cover vinaigrette and refrigerate until ready to use.
Enjoy!

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together and chill.

name: Veronica's 1000 Island Dressing
directions: Blend all the ingredients together thoroughly- hand held blender would work best Refrigerate.
Use as required.

name: Creamy Ranch Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine the cottage cheese, Parmesan, buttermilk, lemon juice, water, shallot, garlic, basil, oregano, and pepper.
Seal the jar and shake well.
Makes enough to coat about 6 cups of greens.

name: Tahini Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine the oil, tahini, vinegar, mustard, salt and pepper.
Seal the jar and shake well.
Makes enough to coat about 6 cups of greens.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Sensational Salad Dressing
directions: Mix well and serve chilled with a salad.

name: Caesar Dressing With Flax Seed
directions: Place the flax seed in a clean coffee grinder or blender and process until finely ground.
Place flax seed meal in a medium bowl; add water, stirring with a whisk.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Add vinegar and remaining ingredients; stir with a whisk.
Store dressing in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
Store remaining flax seed in the refrigerator since it's high in fat, it can go rancid easily.

name: Preserved Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: in a mixing bowl, stir together all the ingredients.

name: Vinegar Dijon Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake.
Store in refridgerator.

name: Lemon Sesame Dressing
directions: In covered container, combine all ingredients.
Chill.
Shake well before using, after bringing to room temp.

name: Yoghurt and Mint Dressing
directions: Mix together all the ingredients so that they are well combined.
Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 hours before serving.

name: Low Cal Sesame Dressing
directions: mix all together.
Use more or less of any ingredient according to taste.
Allow the flavors to blend for an hour.
Apply to salad and garnish with flat leaf parsely.

name: Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients well.
Refrigerate till cold.
Keep in refrigerator to store.
Use on coleslaw, egg salad, deviled eggs, etc.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Sweet and Sour Salad Dressing
directions: Combind ingredients in a jar.
Place lid on jar and shake well.
Chill.
Shake dressing before use.

name: Earl Grey Salad Dressing
directions: Bring vinegar to a boil, add tea bags, cover, and let steep 30 minutes; discard tea bags when done.
Using a blender, combine vinegar mixture with mustard, salt, and pepper in a blender until smooth.
Turn blender to high speed and add the oil in a slow, steady stream.
Hand stir in the minced basil.
Taste for seasonings- adjust to suit your tastes.

name: Betty's Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.

name: Raspberry Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: In electric blender, combine onion, Splenda, mustard, salt and raspberry vinegar.
Whirl in blender for 15 seconds.
While blender is running, slowly add olive oil through the center opening in the lid.
On lowest speed, add the poppy seeds.
Place in a covered container and refrigerate.

name: Elderberry-mustard Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk mustard, vinegar, salt and pepper.
Slowly add to oil while whisking.
Stir in onion and parsley.
Drizzle over salad greens, toss to coat.

similar.name: Almost-Empty Dijon Mustard Jar Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Elderberry-basil Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together, toss with salad greens and halved cherry tomatoes.

similar.name: Raspberry [or Other Fruit] Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Buttermilk Dressing
directions: In a medium bowl, combine all ingredients and whisk until well blended.
Taste and adjust seasonings, if desired.
Refrigerate and chill for several hours before using.

name: Fresh Tomato Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Place everything but oil in food processor.
Pulse while adding oil slowly until well blended.

name: White Goddess Salad Dressing
directions: Blend together all ingredients, with a couple of dashes of worcestershire sauce.
Serve over a crisp mixture of lettuce and spinach.

name: Best Creamy Blue Cheese Dressing/Dip
directions: In a bowl mash the blue cheese with the buttermilk until the mixture resembles large-curd cottage cheese.
Add in sour cream, mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar, Worcestershire sauce, garlic and salt; mix until well blended.
Season with black pepper to taste.

similar.name: Palmer House Blue Cheese Dressing/Chip Dip
The Best Blue Cheese Dressing You'll Ever Make

name: Honey Dijon Dressing
directions: Combine the first 4 ingredients in a bowl and gradually whisk in oil, and continue whisking until dressing is slightly thickened and smooth.

name: Sweet Chipotle Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients, except the oil, in a blender or food processor and blend until very smooth.
Slowly add oil in a thin stream while blender/processor is running and blend until all of the oil is incorporated and emulsified.

name: Sweet Salad Dressing Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl until well mixed.
Put mixture in a foil packet or a 1-pint glass jar.
Label.
Store in a cool, dry place.
Use within 6 months.
To make dressing: Combine dressing mix with 3/4 cup oil and 1/4 vinegar.
Stir until well blended.
Chill before serving.
Makes about 1 1/4 cups Sweet Salad Dressing.
May be used on tossed crisp greens or with fresh fruit!

name: Super Salad Seasoning Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Mix well.
Put into a 1-qt.
airtight container.
Label.
Store in a cool, dry place.
Use within 3 to 4 months.
This mix can be sprinkled over tossed green salads, baked potatoes, and various breads before toasting.
It can also be a garnish for potato salads, macaroni/pasta salads, or egg salads.
You can also make a dip with 2 tablespoons.
mix and add 1 cup sour cream.
NOTE: You could probably add dried red or green pepper for extra color and flavor too, if desired!

similar.name: Ranch Dressing and Dip Spice Mix
Copycat Dry Italian Seasoning and Dressing Mix

name: Cooked Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients except for cheese in the work bowl of a food processor or blender.
Process or blend until smooth Transfer mixture to a small saucepan and cook over very low heat until thickened--about 10 minutes.
Do not boil.
Transfer to a bowl--Cover surface with plastic wrap and chill for at least 2 hours.
Toss chilled dressing and cheese with salad.

name: Cooked Potato or Pasta Salad Dressing
directions: In a medium saucepan mix all ingredients together.
Bring to a light boil, whisking constantly until thickened (about 2 minutes).
Remove from heat, cool slightly then whisk in mayo.
Season with salt and pepper.
Cool completely before adding to you potato or pasta salad.

name: Mediterranean-Style Salad Dressing
directions: Pour all ingredients in a large glass jar, and whisk or mix with a spoon until well combined or you can also blend in a processor or a blender.
Adjust all seasonings to suit taste, including the sugar and salt.
Let sit in fridge for a minimum of 2 hours.
Shake very well before using.

name: The Best Eggless Caesar Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake vigorously until smooth.
Makes 1 cup.

name: Yogurt Tahini Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients until smooth, with a spoon or in a food processor or blender.
Use as a salad dressing, as a vegetable dip, or in a pita sandwich stuffed with veggies.

name: Anchovy Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a covered jar and shake well.
Chill in refrigerator.
Before serving, shake thoroughly.

name: Italian Sweet & Sour Dressing
directions: combine all ingredients and shake well.

name: Lemon-y Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a food processor, or just wisk together.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: In small bowl, blend all ingredients.
Cover; store in refrigerator for up to 1 week.
Serve as a dressing for salad greens.

name: The Best Basic Vinaigrette
directions: In a clean jar or small bowl, add the vinegar, garlic, mustard and mix well.
Slowly add the olive oil while either whisking or stirring rapidly with a fork.
Add the parsley, thyme, salt and pepper taste.

name: Raspberry Vinaigrette
directions: Beat all ingredients with a wire whisk until well blended.
Store covered in refrigerator.

name: Green Goddess Dressing
directions: In a blender or processor, combine all ingredients and blend until smooth.
Cover & refrigerate until needed (up to one week). Dressing thickens slightly with chilling.

name: Italian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender on high until combined, about 30 seconds.
Pour into small bowl and cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Stir before serving.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Stir or shake all ingredients together and chill until ready to serve.

name: Mom's Green Dressing
directions: In a blender puree watercress, parsley, shallot, green pepper, capers, chives and tarragon.
With motor running add the 2 tsp.
wine vinegar and pour in the 3 Tbsp.
olive oil in a slow stream.
Transfer the dressing to a small bowl and add salt and pepper to taste.
Cool.
Pour over mixed green lettuce.

name: Creamy Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender and mix on high for about 30 seconds.

name: Berry Vinegar
directions: Fill sterlized quart jar a little over half full with pierced berries.
(berries are pierced to release juice) cover and fill to top with cider vinegar.
Cover top with plastic wrap and secure lid.
Let age 4-6 weeks out of direct of sunlight.
Strain out berries and pour vinegar into, sterlized bottles, adding a few berries for presentation if desired.
Seal with plastic lid or cork.
Enjoy using in salad dressing or marinades.

name: Honey Berry Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together vinegar, honey and mustard.
Add onion.
Whisk while adding oil.
Season with salt and pepper.
Chill at least one hour.
Store in refrigerator.
Shake before using.

name: Nat's Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk everything together in a bowl.
Let stand in refrigerator for a couple hours.
Notes: Will thicken even more when refrigerated.
Do not keep this too long because of the raw egg (I throw mine out if not used the next day).
I have used storebought "packaged" parmesean, and it still turns out good, but adjust salt accordingly.
I have used low fat mayo, and even Miracle Whip and it still turns out OK.

name: Creamy Tomato-Herb Salad Dressing
directions: Combine thoroughly and refrigerate for at least one hour.

name: Homemade French Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender and blend until mixed.
Chill and keep in the refrigerator.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Brittany Salad Dressing
directions: A half-hour before serving, combine sugar, vinegar& sour cream until smooth.
Refrigerate (I prefer to refrigerate overnight).
Fry bacon until crispy, saving 1/4 of the fat.
Dry bacon on paper towels and chop into small pieces.
To serve, heat 1/4 cup bacon fat until piping hot and pour over crisp, chilled lettuce pieces.
Toss to coat.
Place individual servings on chilled plates& sprinkle with bacon pieces.
Top with sour cream mixture& garnish with cherry tomatoes.

name: Home Fresh French Dressing - Homemade
directions: mix in a blender.
Keep refrigerated.
Shake before serving.

name: Ranch-Style Buttermilk Dressing
directions: In a bowl, whisk all ingredients together.
Transfer to a glass jar.
Refrigerate 2 or more hours to blend flavors.
Enjoy!

name: Sweet and Sour Bacon Dressing
directions: Fry bacon until crisp and brown.
Drain bacon and crumble into small pieces once it is cool.
Saute onion in bacon drippings until tender.
Add sugar, vinegar and water; bring to a boil.
Cool until room temperature.
Combine vinegar mixture with Miracle Whip, beating until smooth.
Add reserved bacon.
Chill thoroughly.

name: Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Mix sugar, onion, salt and dry mustard Stir in the remaining ingredients except the oils.
In another bowl mix the salad oil and olive oil Slowly added the oils, while stirring Refrigerate for at leadt 2 hours before serving.

name: Marinade for Vegetables
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Pour over mixed florets of brocolli and/or cauliflower, purple onion rings, sliced stuffed olives, sliced carrots, cucumbers, radishes-- etc.
Chill marinated vegetables at least 3 hours.

similar.name: Red Wine Vinegar and Oil Dressing/ Marinade

name: Garlic & Caper Mayonnaise
directions: Place egg yolks, lemon juice, mustard, anchovies, garlic and paprika in a food processor.
Process until combined.
With motor running, gradually add about 150ml olive oil, 1 teaspoon at a time, processing well after each addition, until mixture starts to thicken.
Add remaining oil in a thin stream until incorporated and mayonnaise is thick and glossy.
Transfer to a bowl; add capers, season to taste and stir well to combine.

name: Roasted Capsicum Mayonnaise
directions: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Line a baking tray with non-stick baking paper Coat capsicum and garlic with olive oil and place on prepared tray.
Cook in oven for 30 mins, until both are tender when tested with a skewer.
Transfer to a heatproof bowl, cover and stand 5 minutes.
Peel skin from capsicum and discard.
Place flesh in a food processor.
Cut garlic bulb in half horizontally and squeeze cloves into processor.
Process until smooth.
Add egg yolks and vinegar and process to combine.
With motor running gradually add about 150ml of extra olive oil, 1 teaspoon at a time, processing well after each addition, until mixture starts to thicken.
Add remaining oil in a thin steady stream until incorporated.
Add tomato paste and process until combined.
Season to taste.

name: Chilli, Lime & Coriander Mayonnaise
directions: Place egg yolks and lime juice in a food processor.
Process until combined.
With motor running, gradually add about 150ml of the oil 1 teaspoon at a time, processing well after each addition, until mixture starts to thicken.
Add remaining oil in a thin steady stream until incorporated and mayonnaise is thick and glossy.
Transfer to a bowl and add the chilli and coriander.
Season to taste and combine.

name: Honey Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together or use blender to mix all ingredients.
Allow flavors to blend in refrigerator.
Keep chilled.

name: Million $$ Salad Dressing
directions: Blend in blender.
Keeps for months in the refrigerator.

name: Lemon Dressing
directions: In covered container combine all ingredients.
Chill.
Shake well before serving.

name: Creamy Garlic Dressing
directions: In a small saucepan, combine vinegar, water, onion and garlic; simmer until onion is tender (about 5 minutes).
Add in sugar and simmer until dissolved. Cool to room temperature.
Stir in mayonnaise, sour cream, salt and pepper.
Refrigerate dressing for 2 hours or more.
Delicious!

name: Ranch Dressing (Light)
directions: In small saucepan, combine water and cornstarch.
Mix well.
Bring mixture to a boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
Simmer 1 minute, remove from heat and cool.
In small jar, combine all ingredients except pepper.
Place tightly fitting lid on jar and shake well.
Add pepper to taste.

name: Sweet Russian Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients by shaking in a tightly covered jar.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: Cheesecake Factory Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except greens and whisk until smooth or blend at low speed.
Chill.
When ready to serve, put 2 cups greens on each salad dish and top with dressing.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: In a large wooden bowl, using a wooden spoon, mash chopped garlic cloves to a fine paste with salt.
Stir in egg yolks and Dijon mustard.
Add anchovies, and grind to a paste.
Add lemon juice, Worcestershire, hot sauce and season with pepper.
Slowly whisk in olive/canola oil until dressing is creamy.
Add lettuce to bowl, and toss to coat leaves thoroughly with dressing and grated Parmesan.
Sprinkle croutons over salad.

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Oil & Tarragon Vinegar Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a jar or bowl.
Refrigerate before serving for about an hour.
Shake well before serving.

name: Curry Dressing
directions: Process all ingredients in a food processor or blender until smooth, stopping to scrape down sides.

name: Homemade Ranch Dressing/Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Pour in jar and chill.

name: Spinach Salad Dressing
directions: Put first 6 ingredients in blender and blend on high about a minute.
Add the Worchestershire and hot sauces and blend again.
Set blender on high and add Canola Oil pouring very slowly.

similar.name: Onion and Mustard Spinach Salad Dressing

name: Maple Dijon Salad Dressing
directions: Very simple.
Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake well.
Shake again before serving.

name: Tomato-Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Blend first 6 ingredients and 1/2 c oil in processor or blender.
With machine running, add remaining 1/2 c.
oil; process until well blended.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
(Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover& refrigerate. Bring to room temperature before using.).

name: Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Combine first 5 ingredients (vinegar- salt& pepper) in small jar, shake well.
Whisk in oil.

name: Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: Mix lemon juice and garlic in small bowl.
Pour in olive oil, whisking as you pour it in.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Pour over salad or green beans and toss well.

name: Angie's Just-Right Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients.
I like to let this sit for about a day in the refrigerator to let the flavors really blend.
Rewhisk before serving.

name: Hot Bacon Dressing (For Spinach Salad)
directions: In skillet fry bacon until crisp then remove bacon and reserve bacon grease.
Crumble bacon over fresh spinach.
Add minced onion to bacon grease and saute over medium heat about 3-4 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients and saute for about 1 minute.
Pour hot dressing over spinach and toss, mixing well.
Serve immediately.

similar.name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad

name: Orange Pineapple Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk all together.
Allow 15 minutes for flavors to meld.
Whisk before serving.

name: Best Green Goddess Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender and blend until smooth.
Put into container, cover and chill.
Makes 1 1/4 cups.
Good over crisp green salad.

similar.name: Low Fat Green Goddess Salad Dressing

name: Zero Dressing
directions: Shake all ingredients in a jar with a screw top.
Store in refrigerator.
Shake before using.

name: Fancy Lady Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients.
except the sesame seeds.
Cover and refrigerate mixture for 15 to 20 minutes.
Add sesame seeds.(If desired).
mix well.
Serve over salad.

name: Diane's Easy Thousand Island Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients together by hand mixing in a bowl.

name: Canadian Salad Dressing, Eh?
directions: Beat the first 4 ingredients for a few minutes.
Add vinegar and stir well and set aside for a few hours.
The dressing will keep for weeks in the fridge.

name: Carole's Coleslaw
directions: Mix vinegar with other ingredients in cup.
and pour over solids.

name: Perfect Vinaigrette Base
directions: Combine all ingredients in a microwave-safe measuring cup.
Microwave until warm, 30-45 seconds.
Stir to dissolve salt.
Pour liquid into glass jar, discarding garlic solids, and refrigerate.
To Make a Salad: In salad bowl, combine 1 tablespoon Perfect Vinaigrette Base, 1 tablespoon water, 3 tablespoons olive oil and 1/8 teaspoon black pepper.
Add 6 cups torn salad greens (and a small chopped tomato, if desired).
Toss together and taste.
Now, here's the big secret: If dressing seems too strong, add another 1-2 tablespoons water (mild greens will need the extra water).
And another hint -- use a mild olive oil with mild greens, and a more assertive olive oil with robust greens.

name: Copycat of Milani's Buccaneer Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Keep refrigerated.

similar.name: Jan's Beg for the Recipe Salad Dressing

name: Tangy Tomato Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Bring to a boil, whisking often, then reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
Cover the dressing until cool, then refrigerate it until well chilled.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Rainwater Dressing
directions: In a mixing bowl, combine first 6 ingredients and beat with electric mixer for about 4 minutes to dissolve sugar.
Add the oil and poppy seeds and continue beating for about 10 minutes more.
Pour dressing into a pitcher or large jar.
Will keep for several days in refrigerator.
Just stir well to serve.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Lower-fat Ranch Dressing or Dip
directions: Mix the yogurt, mayonnaise and buttermilk together.
Stir in the onion and garlic.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then fold in the chives.
Keep refrigerated, covered, until ready to use (up to 8 hours).

name: Jalapeno Lime Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Puree all of the dressing ingredients in a blender or processor, except the oil.
Emulsify slowly with the oil. Supposedly you can sub the oil with half buttermilk and half mayo, but I haven't tried this yet.
Put together the coleslaw of your choice, add dressing and refrigerate for a few hours.
I usually make half of this- depends on how many you're serving!

name: French Salad Dressing
directions: put in a jar and shake and let stand in refridgerator till it cools threw before using.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine ingredients and shake well.

name: Dressing for a Superb Salad
directions: Whisk or shake together all ingredients.
Serve over salad.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Add all the ingredient to a glass jar/cruet.
Seal jar opening and shake jar to mix up ingredients.
The dressing can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
Take the dressing out of the refrigerator 1 hour before ready to serve.

name: Lemon Pepper Vinaigrette
directions: Process first 5 ingredients in a blender.
With blender running, add oil in a slow steady stream until smooth.
Chill until ready to use.

name: Different Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except olive oil in blender; blend until smooth.
Add olive oil and blend until creamy and combined.
Transfer to serving container and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Stir before serving, and serve over your favorite salad greens.

name: Burnt Honey and Orange Vinaigrette
directions: Pour the honey into a 2½ cup saucepan or a small nonstick skillet.
Cook on medium heat until it begins to foam up, then reduce heat slightly and cook 4 to 5 minutes, swirling the pan occasionally, or until the honey begins to caramelize and turn a light brown.
Remove pan from heat and pour in the orange juice.
Set back on burner and stir to dissolve the honey.
Stir in orange zest.
Pour into a bowl and cool.
Whisk in vinegar, salt, pepper and oils until thickened and blended.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Honey Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk garlic, vinegar, mustard, honey, salt and pepper in a small bowl.
Slowly whisk in oil.

name: Raspberry Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk vinegar, salt and pepper in a small bowl.
Slowly whisk in oil.

name: French Vinaigrette (a.k.a. Grandma Itte's, Pronounced Ee-tay)
directions: Pour vinegar and oil in container of your choice.
Peel garlic and cut into slivers (if you are using an empty vinegar bottle like I do, you will want the pieces to go in and come out easily).
Put the top on the jar and give it a shake.
Pour a small amount into a measuring cup.
In this cup measure your tarragon, salt and pepper.
Stir and use a funnel to pour the mixture into your vinegar bottle.
Give the bottle a good shake or two and let sit for several hours for the flavors to blend.
If you are using a mayonnaise jar simply add all ingredients, shake and let sit.

name: Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar and shake or whisk to combine.
Shake again before using.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Simply Salad Dressing
directions: Wash and dry the leaf lettuce, break into pieces and if chosen, wash the vegetables.
Chop your vegetables to desired size.
Finely chop the garlic cloves and the fresh parsley, set aside.
Pour the oil and the cider vinegar into a container that can be sealed.
I use a small empty peanut butter container.
Add the sugar, parsley, garlic, salt& pepper.
Put lid on and shake and shake until it is all mix up well.
Pour over your salad.

name: World's Easiest Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix together mayonnaise, sour cream with chives, and bleu cheese crumbles.
Add Lawry's seasoning salt to taste.
Refrigerate well.

similar.name: The Best Blue Cheese Dressing You'll Ever Make

name: Lime and Ginger Dressing
directions: Whisk first 5 ingredients in small bowl to blend.
Gradually whisk in olive oil and sesame oil.
Season with salt and pepper.

name: Ciro and Sal's Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients or blend in a food processor or blender.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Dutch Lettuce Dressing
directions: Combine salad dressing, mustard, sugar, salt, pepper and celery salt.
Warm in microwave oven or in the top of a double boiler.
Dressing may be refrigerated and reheated as needed.
Mix desired amount with a tossed salad.

name: Margarita Salad Dressing & Chicken Marinade
directions: Beat all ingredients together with a wire whisk.
Chill.
Serve over a mixed greens salad.

similar.name: Red Wine Vinegar and Oil Dressing/ Marinade
Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Peanut Butter Salad Dressing
directions: You will need 1 used-up peanut butter jar - DO NOT RINSE - be sure to leave a little around the sides for flavor.  Place all ingredients in jar and then fill with oil almost to the top.
Replace the lid and shake until no peanut butter clings to the sides of the jar.
FAT-FREE VERSION:
Use left-over tea (any kind, even ordinary pekoe), or fruit juice such as lemonade, limeade or orange juice in place of some or all of the oil.
NOTE:
To suit my family's tastes I add more fresh ginger, garlic and pepper than called for.

name: Zippy Avocado Dressing
directions: Mix all of the ingredients together in a mixing bowl.
Cover and refrigerate any leftovers.

name: Basic Lemon Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients together.
Enjoy.

name: Plantation Dressing
directions: Beat together all ingredients in mixer.
Store in tightly closed jar and keep refrigerated.

name: Pink Tarragon Cream Dressing
directions: Mix mayo, sour cream, sherry, tarragon, garlic powder, and Tabasco until blended well.
Add a few drops of food coloring to tint it slightly pink.
If the dressing is too thick, you may thin it with a small amount of milk.
Chill well before serving.

name: 2 Minute Mayonnaise
directions: combine all ingredients mixing well.
stand to thicken (I put mine in refrigerator overnight).

name: Green Jacket Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in the bowl of a food processor or a blender and puree until blended.
Salt and pepper to taste.

name: Moosewood House Dressing
directions: Puree all ingredients in blender, except milk.
While the blender is still running, slowly add the milk in a stream until dressing thickens.
Stop the blender when the dressing thickens or the oil may seperate and the dressing will become thin.
This will store for about a week in the frige; shake before use if it looks like it is seperating.

name: Blue Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: Place first eight ingredients in a blender.
Cover and process until smooth.
Pour into container.
Stir in Blue Cheese.
Refrigerate.

name: Creamy Dill Dressing
directions: Whisk mayonnaise and sour cream in medium bowl until smooth; stir in remaining ingredients; season to taste.
cover and chill 1 hour to blend flavours.

similar.name: Heavy on the Dill Salad Dressing

name: Pesto Vinaigrette
directions: Combine first 7 ingredients in mini-processor and process until finely chopped; with machine running, gradually add oil and blend well.
Transfer to small bowl; season to taste with salt and pepper.

name: German Yogurt Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in mixing bowl.
Use whisk to incorporate all ingredients.
Chill and serve.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing (Frenso's)
directions: Place mayonnaise in a mixing bowl with paddle attachment.
Mix on low.
Add honey, cayenne pepper, onion salt, and mustard.
Mix until incorporated.
Add cider vinegar.
Add oil slowly until oil is absorbed.

name: Lovely Tzatziki Sauce
directions: Thinly slice the cucumber, sprinkle with salt.
Let sit for 20 minutes.
Take cucumber slices in hand and squeeze out all of the juice into a bowl.
Then chop up the cucumber in little bits and add to bowl.
Mix in the yogurt, lemon, salt and pepper.

name: Watercress-ranch Salad Dressing
directions: Combine salad dressing mix, buttermilk, mayonnaise and watercress in a blender.
Pulse to combine.
Refrigerate until using.

similar.name: Red Leaf Lettuce and Watercress Salad W/ Buttermilk Dressing

name: Vanilla Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients.
mix well.

name: Oriental Sesame Dressing
directions: Place sesame seeds, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, sugar mixture, and water (everything except the two oils) in a blender.
Start blending and add the vegetable and sesame oil slowly, allowing the mixture to emulsify.
Pour over a big salad of mixed greens.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: Creamy Pesto Dressing
directions: In a small bowl stir together mayonnaise or salad dressing and pesto (add more pesto to accommodate personal taste); add enough buttermilk until mixture is desired consistency; cover and store dressing in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
Note: excellent with baby greens, or as a dip with raw veggies.

name: Homemade Miracle Whip
directions: Put all ingredients except the oil to a blender.
Blend until mixed.
While the blender is running, slowly add the oil (1 tablespoons at a time) until thick and creamy.
Spoon mixture into a clean jar with a lid and chill.
Keep in the fridge.

name: Incredible Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Cover and process until blended.
Refrigerate any leftovers.

name: Sesame Ginger Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Toast sesame seeds in a dry, hot skillet just until they turn brown and begin to pop.
Be careful not to burn them.
In a food processor blend vinegar, ginger, soy sauce and sesame seeds.
Slowly blend in sesame oil until emulfisified.
Refrigerate and Enjoy!

name: Almond Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
shake well.

name: Pampered Chef Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients well in the order listed.
Enjoy!

name: Creamy Ginger Miso Dressing
directions: Combine all of the ingredients in a blender or food processor and puree until smooth.
Coverd and refrigerated, this dressing will keep for about a week.
Enjoy!

name: Dynamite Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients until smooth.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Old Spaghetti Factory Creamy Pesto Dressing Recipe
directions: Whisk together oil and mayonnaise.
Add buttermilk, cheese, basil, salt, garlic, and hot pepper sauce to taste.
Mix thoroughly.
Cover and let flavors meld overnight in refrigerator.
This also will make an excellent chicken marinade.

name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing
directions: To prepare dressing, warm wine vinegar slightly.
Add sugar and stir to dissolve.
If you don't warm the vinegar, you will have trouble getting the sugar to dissolve.
Allow to cool.
Add onion, paprika and salt.
Stir well.
Add olive oil and blend.
I prefer to put it in a bottle and shake it.
Pour over salad and enjoy!

name: Hot Spinach Dressing and Salad
directions: Mix garlic, vinegar, catsup, oil and sugar together in a microwave safe bowl.
Heat for 30 seconds, then stir.
Continue to heat the dressing until it is very warm to touch.
Assemble the salad: spinach, eggs and bacon pieces, pour the dressing over the salad and serve immediately as the hot dressing will wilt the spinach.

similar.name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad

name: Mariel's Amazing Salad Dressing
directions: combine all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator until ready to use.
Mixture can be used as salad dressing or marinade.

similar.name: Diane's Easy Thousand Island Salad Dressing

name: Fat Free Sugar Free Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together.
There are many variations, sometimes I'll saute green onions and stick the whole thing in a blender, but if you are really watching your calories this is pretty good.
If you like it, make it on a larger scale it keeps for about two weeks in the refrigerator.

similar.name: Low Cal Garlic Mustard Vinaigrette Dressing
Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Peel and chop the hard boiled eggs.
Place all of the ingredients in a blender and puree until smooth.
Covered and refrigerated this dressing will keep for about 5 days.

name: Anita's Homemade Ranch
directions: Stir all together until smooth.
Enjoy!
If you want dip, leave as is, if you want dressing add 1 tbs milk or buttermilk at a time until you reach the desired consistency (aprox 2 tbs is how I like it).
As the flavors blend, it gets better.

name: Sweet Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: Combine sour cream, mustard, vinegar and sweetener.
Mix well.
Serve!

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!

name: Fennel Salad Dressing
directions: Measure the oils, vinegars, lemon juice, sugar, mustard, fennel and dried parsley into a glass jar.
Cover, and shake to combine.
Add salt and pepper (and fresh parsley if using) to taste.
Serve over your favorite combination of greens and other salad ingredients such as chopped vegetables, meats, eggs, etc.
Store the unused portion in the refrigerator.
It should last at least one week (it is used quickly in our home).

name: Oregano Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, combine vinegar and seasonings.
Gradually whisk in olive oil to blend.
Serve over your favorite salad greens.
Especially nice garnished with shaved parmesan cheese.
Refrigerate any unused vinaigrette.

name: My Version of Red Robin's Honey Mustard
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
(use a food processor).
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.

name: The Brigantine's Not Your Usual Blue Cheese Dressing!
directions: Stir, but don't beat- all ingredients with spoon until blended.
Add sugar while blending.
Serve well chilled; the dressing will thicken when chilled.
(Note: cook time is chill time).

name: Amish Cooked Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, beat the egg with 1 tbsp of the water;set aside.

In a saucepan, whisk together the flour, mustard, and sugar, then whisk in the remaining 1 cup water, vinegar, oil and lemon juice; heat over medium heat for 1 minute, whisking continuously (the dressing will be thick).
Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and continue to cook for about 1 minute, stirring until the dressing has a mashed potato consistency and is off-white in color.
Remove from heat, cover, and cool completely.
Store in a covered jar in the refrigerator (will keep for several weeks).

name: Ginger's Sweet Onion Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender and process until smooth.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: The Brigantine's Spinach Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Keep refrigerated.
Serve over spinach salad.

similar.name: Onion and Mustard Spinach Salad Dressing

name: My Use Only Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: To food Processor add everything except oil.
Blend these until smooth.
While motor is running, slowly add oil.
Create emulsion.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

name: Outback Blue Cheese Salad Dressing - Copycat
directions: Mix all ingredients together by hand in a small bowl until smooth.
Cover and chill for 30 minutes before serving.

name: Lorna's Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender.
Blend till thickened.
Toss with fresh romaine.
Top with fresh croutons and bacon bits.
Dust with a bit more parmesan If chilling dressing before use, take out of the fridge about 1/2 hour before use.
One recipe will do a large head of romaine.
If you need to double the recipe-- mix 2 batches separately.
It doesn't double well!

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Flax Seed Raspberry & Coriander Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients in jar and shake or in blender and mix.
Variations; try using mint, basil or parsley in place of coriander and experiment with other fruit jams.

name: Copycat Pancho's Dressing Recipe
directions: Place all ingredients in a blender (or use immersion hand blender) and liquefy.
Enjoy.

name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Boil egg for 1 minute exactly.
Remove from water with a slotted spoon and cool until you can handle it.
Crack directly into food processor, blender, or tall jar (if using a hand blender).
Add garlic, anchovy paste, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Pulse to combine.
With blender or food processor running, begin adding the olive oil a few drops at a time.
It should take about 3 minutes to add all of the olive oil.
Begin adding the vegetable oil in a thin stream.
When all oil is incorporated, continue blending until dressing is at the thickness you prefer.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to blend flavors.
Serve over romaine lettuce with shredded Parmesan on top.

name: Avocado Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a food processor and blend until smooth.
Chill.

name: Dressing for Spinach Salad
directions: Whisk all ingredients together until thoroughly incorporated.
Pour over prepared spinach salad.
Toss.
***note***maywant to prepare a little in advance of use in order to allow time for the sugar to dissolve.

similar.name: Onion and Mustard Spinach Salad Dressing
Spinach Salad Dressing - Spicy and Fabulous

name: Lou Lou’s Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: Dorothy Lynch Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a blender until creamy.
Pour into canning jar or container and refrigerate overnight.
Here is an update on this dressing. To thicken the dressing add a little cornstarch.

name: Pomegranate Molasses Dressing
directions: Mix the garlic, cumin, sugar, pomegranate molasses and freshly squeezed lemon juice in a bowl.
Whisk in the olive oil. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Add a little extra sugar if you think it’s a bit too sharp.

name: Dijon-herb Dressing
directions: In a small bowl whisk together the mustard, vinegar, oil, basil and parsley.
In a separate bowl mix the powdered milk and water; it will not be the consistency of milk, instead creamier and thicker like half-n-half.
Combine milk with the dressing and whisk until fully incorporated.
This will keep in the fridge for 2 or 3 days; if you are using dried herbs its tastes better if you let the dressing sit in the ice box an hour or so before serving.

name: The Black Swan House Dressing
directions: In a food processor place the garlic, sugar, mustard, salt, and sour cream.
Add the vinegar.
Blend on continuous speed.
In a thin stream, slowly add the oil.
When blended, fold in the parsley and pepper.

name: Basil Garlic Citrus Vinaigrette
directions: Combine orange juice concentrate, vinegar, water, black pepper and basil in a small bowl.
Whisk in olive oil slowly, then add clove of garlic.
Pour into a small jar and refrigerate.
Bring to room temperature before using.
Shake well before pouring on salad.
Can keep in refrigerator for up to one week.

name: Smoky Green Chili Ranch Dressing
directions: Whisk all the ingredients together in a bowl. Refrigerate until needed.

name: Honey and Lemon Salad Dressing
directions: Combine lemon juice with olive oil in a small jar and shake well.
Combine honey and vinegar in small bowl or cup and stir until honey is dissolved and then add to lemon juice/oil mixture in jar.
Add remainder of ingredients (salt - sherry) and shake well (or whisk) to combine.
Refrigerate.
Shake well before using.

name: Barbara's Kitchen Counter Vinaigrette
directions: Shake ingredients in a jar with a tight fitting lid.
No need to refrigerate the left over dressing; you can leave it on the counter for a couple of days, just shake and serve.
Number of servings is relative - this usually is enough for two or three bowls of salad ~ or 3 dinners.

name: Granny's Red French Dressing
directions: Put all the ingredients into a blender and process until smooth.

name: Citrus-cumin Salad Dressing
directions: Mince garlic and mash to a paste with 1/4 tsp salt, then whisk together with orange and lime juices, shallot, honey cumin and remaining 1/4 tsp salt.  Add oil in a slow stream, whisking until well blended.  Whisk in pepper.

name: Creamy Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Place onions and garlic into a small bowl(I use a fruit nappie.).
Add just enough white vinegar to cover the garlic and onions in the bowl.
Salt and pepper to your desire.
Add a few drops of olive oil and mix everything together.
Let sit until you are ready to eat.
Mix in Hellman's Mayonaise a tablespoon or so at a time until you get the consistency that you like.
Pour over 1/2 head of chopped lettuce(chilled)
Toss and croutons and enjoy!

name: Tea Vinaigrette
directions: Place tea leaves, if using, in a tea ball. Place tea ball or bag and vinegar in a small saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat. Lower heat and simmer, covered, 1 minute. Remove pan from heat and set aside, allowing tea to steep in the vinegar until cooled. Remove the tea ball or tea bag and gently squeeze the bag or leaves to extract as much vinegar as possible into the pot.

Transfer vinegar to a medium glass or stainless steel bowl. Add honey, thyme, shallots, and salt and pepper to taste; whisk until blended and smooth. Continuing to whisk, gradually add oil in a slow, steady stream until blended. The mixture will not emulsify. Transfer to a jar, cover tightly, and revrigerate for at least 4 hours. Store covered in refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Shake well before serving. Makes about 1 1/3 cups.

name: Favorite Salad Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients in a bowl and whisk well.
Drizzle dressing over salad of choice and toss gently to coat.
Serve immediately.

name: Spiced Orange Cream Dressing
directions: Mix mayonnaise, orange rind, orange juice, icing sugar, cinnamon and nutmeg.
Whip whipping cream til stiff.
Fold into mayonnaise mixture.
Chill.

name: Parmesan-Dijon Vinaigrette
directions: Blend all ingredients in a processor, or shake in a glass jar with a tight fitting lid.
Store in the fridge.

name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing
directions: Peel the onion and garlic cloves then mince together with the fresh basil leaves. Put in a jar and add remaining ingredients. Shake up and season as needed with salt and pepper. Store in a cool place. Each night  simply shake it up and pour over a salad.
Variations:

For Thai-style dressing that is good over rice noodles, grains or vegetable salads, blend 1/2 cup dressing with 1 tbls. minced fresh chile, 1/4 cup minced fresh cilantro, 1 tbls. brown sugar, 1/2 cup crushed peanuts.
For ranch dressing: blend 1/3 cup of dressing with 1/3 to 1/2 cup buttermilk.

name: Warm Garlic Balsamic Dressing
directions: In a medium skillet combine garlic, oil and some salt and pepper. Cook over medium low until garlic is soft and very light. (If it darkens, it will turn bitter. Throw everything away and start again.) Add the vinegars and sugar. Simmer 2 to 3 minutes.
Taste by cooling a sample first (it will be very hot), and seeing if there's soft acidity and good sweet/tart balance. If too, acid, continue simmering. If acid is lacking, add a little more vinegar.
Remove from heat, cover and hold until shortly before serving (up to 3 hours). Taste again, adjusting seasonings if necessary.
Heat up and spoon over salad.

name: Onion and Mustard Spinach Salad Dressing
directions: Cut onion into large chunks and place in blender with remaining ingredients.  Blend well.
I usually use this on a salad made with baby spinach, hard bolied eggs, bacon bits, mushrooms, and a bag of stuffing mix.

name: Oil Dressing
directions: Beat egg yolk into lemon juice, when well mixed add small quantities of oil until it forms a thick, creamy dressing.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Place first 7 ingredients in a bowl and very slowly whisk in olive oil.
Add garlic and place dressing in a covered jar in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours to let flavors develop.

name: Soy-mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients except for the oil.
When well blended add the oil slowly.
Chill before using.

name: Tarragon Vinegar
directions: Fill a wide mouth quart jar 3/4 full of tarragon sprigs.
Heat the white wine vinegar to the boiling point and pour into jar.
Cover and keep in a cool place, out of the sun, for 10 days to 2 weeks.
Strain into bottles in which you've put 1 to 2 fresh tarragon sprigs for decoration.
Cork tightly.

name: Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Mix together as would any dressing, shake, and refrigerate.

name: Warm and Creamy Bacon Dressing
directions: Cook bacon until crisp. Drain, reserving 1 tablespoon drippings.
Heat reserved drippings, bacon and garlic over low heat 1 minute.
Stir in salad dressing and milk. Cook, stirring occasionally, until thoroughly heated.

similar.name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad

name: Roasted Red Pepper Dressing
directions: In a blender or food processor puree the red peppers, cayenne, and vinegar until the mixture is smooth and with the motor running add the oil in a stream. Turn off the motor, scrape down the sides, and blend in the yogurt and salt/pepper to taste.

name: Basic Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all the ingredients together and shake well.  I use a jar with a screw top.

name: Sweet 'n' Sour Salad Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine the first seven ingredients; shake well.
Serve over salad greens.

name: French Dressing
directions: Into mayonniase beat in oil -- add remaining ingredients and stir -- chill.

name: White Spot Dressing
directions: Cream cheese until soft and add mayo gradually.  Mash eggs as smooth as possible and mix all ingredients.

name: Lemon Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a cruet and shake well.
For best results, chill about 1-2 hours to combine flavors.
Serve.

name: Hot Bacon Salad Dressing
directions: Mix sugar, salt and cornstarch and mustard powder in a blender.add beaten egg and mix.
Add milk and vinegar.
Pour into a pan and cook till thick --
Fry bacon and crumble and add to dressing.
May add a little of bacon grease to thin.
Very good on spinach or salads.
Serve warm.

name: Garlic and Blue Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: In food processor, combine onion, garlic, lemon juice and process till smooth.
Add miracle whip. blue cheese, pepper and tabasco sauce.
Process till smooth.
Makes 2 2/3 cups of dressing.

name: Lincoln Highway Salad Dressing..
directions: Blend all ingredients in a blender or food processor till smooth .
Chill.
Will keep in refrigerator for several months.

name: Honey Poppy Seed Salad Dressing..
directions: In a screw top jar, combine all ingredients, cover and shake well.
Makes 3/4 cup of salad dressing.serve chilled.
Will keep for several weeks in refrigerator.but it won't last that long.

name: Raisin Orange Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a screw top jar and shake to mix well.
Serve chilled.

name: Sesame Seed Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl or a jar with a tight fitting lid.
Beat briskly or shake till well combined.

name: Ritz Ranch Salad Dressing Mix
directions: Place Ritz crackers in your blender (I pulse them) until they are powdered.
Add all other ingredients to the powdered crackers and blend. I don't blend it all until powdered, just mixed really well.
Store this mixture in an airtight container.
To make salad dressing: Mix 1 1/2 cups mayo (use the good stuff); 1 cup milk (whatever you use at home); and 1 Tbsp of the dry mix. Shake well and store in the refrigerator.

This makes a great dip too!  Try any combination you like of sour cream, mayo, cottage cheese or cream cheese, and 1 Tbsp or more if desired of the dry mix.  I like 1/2 cup mayo, 1 package softened cream cheese, and 1/2 cup IMO, with at least 1 Tbsp dry mix.

name: Honey-Mustard Dressing (Another One)
directions: Combine all ingredients, except oil.
Add oil slowly, whisking it constantly.
Refrigerate for at least an hour to let the flavors blend.  This dressing just keeps getting better the longer it sits.

name: My Best Salad Dressing
directions: Combine Ingredients.
Mix Well.
Enjoy over your favorite salad.

name: Cranberry Vinaigrette/Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender, whisk egg white, mustard, lemon juice and red wine vinegar until frothy.
While blender is still running, slowly pour in the cranberry concentrate, allowing it to infuse gradually into the egg white mixture.
Mix the two oils together in a small bowl, and let it gradually stream into the blender, continuing to whisk until the dressing has emulsified, and has thickened.

Turn off the blender at this point, and pour dressing into a large bowl. Stir in the dill and add the salt, pepper and sugar to your liking. You might like yours sweeter or more tart than mine, so I'll leave the amounts up to you.
If using it on broiled Salmon, you can marinate the fish for 20-30 minutes before broiling or brush it on the fish in the final minutes of grilling.

name: Cranberry Vinaigrette/Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender, whisk egg white, mustard, lemon juice, and red wine vinegar.
While blender is still running, slowly infuse the cranberry concentrate into the mix by pouring it in a thin stream.
Keep the blender running as you add both the olive and canola oils the same way you added the cranberry, until the oils are completely emulsified and the dressing starts to thicken.

At this point, turn off blender, and pour the dressing into a bowl. Stir in the dill, and season the dressing with salt, pepper and sugar to your liking. I did not specify the amounts, because some like it sweet, and some like it tart.
Use it to marinade salmon before broiling, or brush it over grilled salmon. Or use it over your favorite tossed greens--especially good during the holiday season.
Note: To make it really bright in color, add a couple of drops of red food coloring--WOW!

name: Low-Fat French Dressing
directions: In a bowl, whisk the first 10 ingredients until blended.
Serve with a tossed salad.
Refrigerate leftover dressing.

name: Creamy Low Fat Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: In a medium glass bowl, whisk together all the ingredients till well mixed.
Pour into medium jar, cover and refrigerate.
Serve on salad cold.

name: Low-Cal Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a large non-aluminum container.
Chill and mix well before tossing with lettuce.
Makes 100 servings.

name: Avocado Dressing
directions: Mix all the ingredients, cover and refrigerate till use.
Whisk well and use on green salad or fruit.

name: Ranch Dressing
directions: I use a rasp to puree the garlic, but you can use a garlic press.
Mix all ingredients and chill in the refrigerator before use.
The flavour improves with time.

name: Tarragon Vinaigrette
directions: Mix together all ingredients.
Taste and adjust seasonings.

name: Nancy's Boiled Salad Dressing
directions: Stir Butter and flour together.
Add milk and let cook in top of double boile, stirring constantly, until quite thick.
Separate eggs and beat the egg whites until stiff.
Beat egg yolks slightly, and add the salt, cayenne pepper, sugar, mustard, and vinegar then stir into the thickened milk mixture.
Cook until thick like cream.
Remove from heat and pour into the egg whites, stirring constantly.

name: Soy Sesame Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar shaking well to combine.

name: Honey Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: In a small sauce pan, combine balsamic vinegar, water cranberries and apricots.  Set pan over med high heat and bring to a boil.   Simmer for 5 minutes.  Strain and reserve liquid and fruit.  Combine liquid with mustard, honey and oil.
When using the dressing.
Place 2 cups of shredded lettuces of your choice  (Spinach leaves would work also!)  On a plate per serving.  Top each plate with 2 tbls vinaigrette and 1 tbls of fruit.

name: Catalina Dressing
directions: Food process half a small onion with the white vinegar and then combine all ingredients in a medium sized pale.
Mix together with a hand or wand mixer.  For home use I would quarter the recipe.

name: Charlotte Annes Caesar Dressing
directions: Using a hand processor or wand mixer mice garlic and egg together.  Slowly add the oil.  While blending add the remaining ingredients.  Voila!  You are done, for more zing add two more cloves of garlic!

name: Tangy Catalina Dressing
directions: I recommend that you use a hand mixer with the whisk attachment to ensure that the dressing is thoroughly incorporated.
Place in an airtight container and store in the refrigerator. It will take a few minutes and perhaps some additional stirring to get all of the sugar dissolved, but it will happen!

name: Balsamic & Roasted Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: Roast garlic at 350 degrees until soft, golden brown and fragrant - about 30 minutes.
Peel cloves and mash coarsely in a mixing bowl.
Add the vinegar and mustard, whisking to incorporate.
Whisk in the olive oil.
Season with salt and pepper.
Refrigerate at least 1 hour.

name: Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients.
Mix with shredded cabbage and carrots.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Marie's Coleslaw Lemon Dressing
directions: Mix altogether well in a bowl.
Add dressing to coleslaw just before serving.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients in food processor except oil.  Blend while slowly drizzling oil into the processor.

name: Cilantro-Pumpkin Seed Dressing
directions: NOTE: I have also used a combination of vegan mayonnaise, sour cream and/or plain yogurt with this recipe, using up what I have on hand.
Add all ingredients to blender and blend until smooth. (Or use an hand held immersion blender to blend. ).
For thinner salad dressing, add more water. Adjust seasonings if necessary.
The thicker version of this dressing makes a delicious topping for fish tacos, tostadas; or as a dip for tortilla chips, etc.
Best if prepared at least an hour in advance.
Yield is estimated.

name: Caesar French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender except the pepper and mix well. Blend again before combining with lettuce. Sprinkle more parmesan cheese on after mixing with lettuce.
Top with pepper, parmesan, and croutons.
This recipe makes about 3/4 cup dressing.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Run tomatoes and garlic through a grinder, using a fine blade.
Place pepper, salt and mustard in a container, mix well.  Add tomato mixture, vinegar, cheese and capers.
Stir until well-mixed and mustard is dissolved.  Add in oil and stir well.  Store in the refrigerator.  Makes 2 cups.

name: Kim's Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingrredients together and shake well. Let marinate over night for best results.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Ranch Dressing (Copycat)
directions: Thoroughly blend Miracle Whip and buttermilk.
Add spices, blending well.
Store covered in refrigerator.
HINT: Using the low speed of a blender lessens prep time!

name: Hot Bacon Salad Dressing
directions: Brown bacon, remove from drippings.
Combine sugar and flour, add beaten egg, salt, water and vinegar and stir till smooth.
Pour into bacon drippings and heat stirring till mixture thickens -- add crumbled crisp bacon.
Serve immediately.on leaf lettuce mixture.

name: Penny's French Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar(make sure you have a little mixing room about 3 inches, seal lid tight and shake well. Its done. chill for best results, mixture will thicken in refrigerator. Shake well before using, enjoy. In my house i double sometime triple the recipe. It  last long in refrigerator.

name: Greek Salad Dressing
directions: combine all ingredients and refridgerate at least 30 minutes prior to serving.

name: Low Sodium Citrus Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients.

name: Honey Poppy Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients, and whisk to combine.
Yield 1cup dressing- enough for 4-6 side salads.
For those of you ,that watch the sugar intake, see if you can find the sugar-free honey made by:HoneyTree. They are located in Onsted, Mi 49265.
It is great.

name: Cilantro-Chipotle Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients, except the mayonnaise, to the container of a blender.
Blend on low speed, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon until blended.
Add mayonnaise.
Blend on low speed stirring occasionally until mixture is smooth and creamy, about 3 minutes.
Serve immediately or refrigerate until served.

similar.name: Cafe Rio Copycat Cilantro Ranch Dressing

name: The Best Blue Cheese Dressing You'll Ever Make
directions: Combine all in a mixing bowl. Chill for at least 1 hour to bring out the flavor.

name: Tgi Friday's Mandarin Orange Sesame Dressing(Copycat)
directions: Measure the marmalade, cayenne pepper, ginger, garlic powder, vinegar, oil, soy sauce, sesame oil and honey into a blender container.
Cover and blend on medium speed for 30 to 45 seconds.  Then transfer to a small bowl and add the chopped orange sections. Stir to mix, cover and refrigerate for up to 3 days.
Serve on a bed of mixed greens, topped by sliced grilled chicken breasts.  Enjoy!

name: Light Mustard Pepper Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk together the oil, vinegar, mustards, mustard seeds, salt and pepper.
If desired, add spicy mustard.
Add the hot water, and whisk well to combine.
If desired, add egg yolk, and whisk again.
Let sit for at least 1 hour for flavours to meld.

name: Bacon and Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Slice bacon into 1/2 inch pieces and fry until just crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Remove bacon from pan, and all but 2 Tbsp of the bacon fat.
Gently saute the onion until just soft, making sure not to brown.
Add the brown sugar, blend in and then add the vinegar. Reduce for a minute or two.
Add the olive oil, as much as to your taste and the salt and pepper to taste.
Cool, but do not refrigerate. Dress your salad just before serving.

Serve this warm or at room temperature over a salad of hearty greens like romaine. I usually add some very finely sliced red onion rings, mushrooms and homemade croutons, but let your imagination work here. I've thought of trying it in the summer with just onions and fresh sliced strawberries.

name: Creamy  Orange Dressing
directions: In a bowl combine all ingredients.
Beat til smooth.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Spicy Sour Cream Dressing
directions: Mix well and drizzle over salad.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix everything but vegetable oil in blender until foamy and thick.
Slowly add in vegetable oil.Continue mixing for about 30 seconds.
Refridgerate for about 1/2 hour before serving to blend flavors.

name: Spinach Salad Dressing - Spicy and Fabulous
directions: Mix all ingredients except for oil in blender.
When well blended, add oil in a slow stream while blender is running to create a thick dressing.
Blend in a tbsp or two of water until the dressing is thin enough to pour out of the blender (but the dressing should still stay quite thick).
Will keep for a long time in refridgerator.

similar.name: Onion and Mustard Spinach Salad Dressing

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: In a blender or food processor:.
Add first seven ingredients.
Blend well.
Slowly add the oils.
Blend until well emulsified.
Keeps well in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

name: Faith58's Potato Salad
directions: Cook Potatoes until done and cube. Usually 20 minutes. Pour french dressing over warm potatoes and chill for 2 hours. Boil eggs and cut up.
Add celery and onion, eggs and salt to potato mixture and add mayonnaise and mix carefully. Chill 4 hours.

name: A Dijon Dressing
