directions: Whisk together and wal-la!

name: Wasabi Vinaigrette
directions: In a jar, combine wasabi powder and water. Let stand 5 minutes.
Add vinegars and whisk to combine.
Stir in oil and shake well to combine.
Add parsley and salt (if desired) and shake well before serving.

name: Two-Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk or whirl the ingredients in blender. Store in glass container. Will stay fresh up to 4 days.
Adjust seasoning before serving.

similar.name: Almost-Empty Dijon Mustard Jar Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Spicy and Creamy Peppercorn Dressing
directions: Cut the cream cheese up into small pieces and add it along with the other ingredients to blender.
Process until smooth. Season to taste and add salt if necessary. (The parmesan should add enough salt.)
Chill until ready to serve. Best made 1 hour in advance.

name: Cuban Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients throughly with wire whisk.  Serve at room temperature.

name: Salad Dressing - Creamy Citrus Ginger
directions: Combine all.
Shake it up.
Serve.
Refrigerate.

name: Emerils Herb Vinaigrette
directions: Combine the vinegar, shallots, garlic, herbs,salt and pepper in a bowl.
slowly whisk in the olive oil until emulsified.
Can be stored in an airtight container in a refrigerator for 3 days.
Whisk before serving.

name: Garden Dew Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar.  Cover tightly, and shake vigorously.
Chill several hours.
Serve over salad greens.

name: Basic Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together the oil and vinegar until emulsified.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Use to dress 1 serving of salad.

name: Uncle Bill's Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients in a food processor or blender until smooth.
Store in refrigerator in a glass jar with lid.

name: Cranberry Walnut Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, combine vinegar, shallots(or scallions) and brown sugar; set aside for at least 1/2 hour. In a medium bowl, slowly whisk together cranberry sauce, olive oil, walnut oil and salt and pepper. Add vinegar mixture to cranberry mixture and whisk until combined. Serve over a pretty salad. Enjoy!
Makes about 3/4 cup vinaigrette.

name: Poppy Seed Honey Dressing
directions: Whisk together or process in a food processor until smooth.

name: Low Fat Celery Seed Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk the mayonnaise, sugar, onion, vinegar and celery seed. Cover and chill until serving. You can double this recipe and keep extra on hand.

name: Jicama Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together all except cilantro.  Just before serving over jicama/vegetable salads, add cilantro.  Toss salad and allow to sit in fridge for at least 1/2 hour.

name: Low Fat Buttermilk Basil Salad Dressing
directions: In a bowl, combine the buttermilk and sour cream until smooth.
Stir in the Parmesan cheese, basil, onion salt, and garlic.
Cover and refrigerate for at least an hour.

name: Parmesan Creamy Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, mix together the mayonnaise, Parmesan, vinegar, sour cream, parsley, mustard, herbs, and salt and pepper to taste.
Whisk well.
Slowly add the oil, whisking constantly, until the dressing is emulsified.
Cover and chill the dressing until you are ready to use it.
Let stand at room temperature for 5 minutes before serving.
This dressing will keep for up to 5 days in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

name: English Room Salad Dressing
directions: Mix together sugar, dry mustard, paprika, celery seed, and salt.
Add onion and grated orange peel.
Blend in honey.
Combine lemon juice, orange juice and red wine vinegar in seperate bowl; blend well; add to mixture.
Add salad oil in a slow stream, beating constantly with a hand mixer or whisk.

name: Avocado Ranch Dressing
directions: Blend the ranch dressing and the peeled and pitted  avocado until smooth.
Serve over salad.

name: Low Fat Ranch Dressing
directions: Blend well and chill for a few hours.

similar.name: Fat-Free Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing (Mix  It Yourself )

name: Italian Ranch Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.  Serve on salads or mix with cucumbers, onions, tomatoes, or any cold vegetable.

similar.name: Creamy Parmesan Italian Dressing (Weight Watcher Friendly)

name: Tasty Chicken Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender for about 1 minute.
Put chicken breasts and marinade in zip lock bag.
Seal air tight over night.

name: Champagne Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: In bowl, whisk vinegar, honey and mustard.  Gradually add oil and whisk until emulsified.  Season with salt & pepper.

name: Spicy Sour Cream Dressing
directions: Mix all together, drizzle over salad.

name: Mock Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: In food processor or blender, combine garlic, salt, lemon juice, Worcestershire, sour cream.
Add olive oil in a stream.
Pour over torn Romaine lettuce right before serving.
Sprinkle with Parmesan and toss in croutons, if desired.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: In blender combine everything but oil and poppy seeds.
Blend at high speed for 30 seconds.
Go to lowest speed. SLOWLY add oil (it will get creamy if you don't add oil to fast); add poppy seeds.

name: Benihana Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.  Process until almost smooth.
Any unused portions may be stored in a covered container in the refrigerator.

name: Martha Stewart's Add-On Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk together vinegar, mustard, salt, pepper, and a pinch of sugar.
Slowly add olive oil, whisking until emulsified. Or shake the ingredients in a jar, or whirl them in a blender.

Variations. For Garlic: Add 1 teaspoon minced garlic or 1/2 clove crushed. For Balsamic: Substitute balsamic vinegar for the wine vinegar. For Lemon Parmesan: Use fresh lemon juice instead of vinegar; add 1/4 cup finely grated Parmesan. For Scallion: Add 3 chopped whole scallions (about 1/4 cup). For Herb: Add 2 tablespoons chopped fresh herbs, such as thyme, parsley, or tarragon. For Blue Cheese: Add 1/2 cup crumbled blue cheese, such as Roquefort.

name: Caesar Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients and chill.

name: Balsamic Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Blend honey and mustard in a small bowl with a fork until emulsified.
Drizzle in the balsamic vinegar until dressing is just thin enough to pour over salad.
Taste and add more vinegar if desired.
Crush the peppercorns with a mortar and pestle or in a spice grinder.
Mix in dressing and drizzle on salad.

name: Mom's Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients togther well, until combined.
Store in a container in the fridge.

name: Caesar Dressing
directions: .Use on Romaine lettuce and garnish with plenty of fresh Parmesan cheese.

name: Blackberry Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients except the oil into a blender.  Blend on high for one minute until mixed well, then turn blender off and taste.  Adjust with more Splenda if you want a little sweeter vinaigrette.
Turn blender back on and with blender running, slowly add oil.  Pour into jar with tight-fitting lid and store in the refrigerator.

name: Basic Vinaigrette With Variations
directions: Whisk the garlic cloves, mustard, vinegar, kosher salt, and pepper together in a bowl.
While whisking, slowly drizzle in the olive oil to emulsify.
Taste for salt and pepper.
Variations:
Use any other vinegar you have, including red wine vinegar and balsamic.
Leave out the garlic or add herbs as you like.
Add 1 teaspoon dried oregano and/or substitute lemon juice for the vinegar for a Greek-like dressing. I would leave out the mustard for this but the dressing will not emulsify as well without it.

similar.name: Everyday Vinaigrette With Lots of Variations

name: Bacon Buttermilk Dressing for Spinach Salad
directions: Saute the bacon until crisp and drain on paper towels. Set aside.
Mix together the remaining ingredients, then add the bacon.
Refrigerate at least an hour or up to 8 hours, stirring before serving.

similar.name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad

name: Pomegranate Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients and pour over you favorite salad.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Lori’s Horseradish Maple Vinaigrette
directions: Puree all.

name: Cilantro-Lime Vinaigrette
directions: Puree all ingredients.

name: Apricot Curry Dressing
directions: Finely chop or crush the garlic.
Mix all ingredients together in a shaker or mix with a whisk.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Lemon Parsley Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar.
Cover and shake until blended.
Chill before serving.

name: Creole Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, whisk together mustard and vinegar.
Pour oil into mustard/vinegar mixture in a steady stream; whisking, until it is emulsified.
Serve over simple salad greens and/or your choice of chopped raw vegetables.
Refrigerate leftover dressing for up to one week.

name: Blue Cheese  Dressing
directions: In a small mixing bowl combine the vinegar and milk.
Allow to sit for a few minutes to curdle.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Mix well.
Serve over your favorite salad greens.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to one week.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients. Chill.

name: Lovely Lavender Salad Dressing
directions: NOTE: Use regular honey if you cannot find lavender honey. To make it easier to blend, zap the honey a few seconds in the microwave.

Combine all ingredients except for salt and pepper in a non-reactive bowl and whisk well or blend with immersion blender. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour prior to serving to allow ingredients to marinate. Bring to room temperature. Whisk before serving.
Adjust seasoning if necessary and season with salt and pepper if desired.
Keeps for 1 week in refrigerator.

name: Outback Ranch
directions: Add buttermilk and spices.
Mix well.
Add mayo and then mix on low 15 minutes.
Stays fresh 3-5 days.

similar.name: Outback Steakhouse Tangy Tomato Salad Dressing

name: Dublin's Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: mix oil and vinegar well then add spices.

name: Chili's Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Mix well and chill for two hours before serving.  Stir again before serving.
Enjoy!

name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing
directions: In food processor or with grater,chop the onion so fine it is almost liquefied.
Mix in other ingredients with the ground up onion and using a food processor emulsify the dressing. Dressing, can, however, simply be mixed using a wire whisk in the traditional way.
Add to your favorite cole slaw recipe.

name: Soy Mayonnaise
directions: Add all ingredients except the oil in a blender and blend until well mixed.
Add oil slowly until mixture thickens.

name: Chef Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a dressing bottle or jar.
Chill several hours to blend flavors.
Serve with tossed greens.

name: Red-Wine Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together everything except olive oil.
Slowly drizzle in oil, whisking until thickened.
Add the garlic cloves, if desired, and let them flavor the vinaigrette overnight.
Bring to room temperature before using.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Combine and chill for an hour or longer.

name: Romano's Macaroni Grill Roasted Garlic Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: Place vinegar, honey, salt and roasted garlic in a food processor.  Puree until the garlic is chopped very fine.  With the food processor still running, add olive oil and lemon juice.  Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Beat together all ingrediants with an electic mixer for 1 minute.
Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours before serving. If you let it set over night, it is better.

name: Louis Dressing
directions: Stir ingredients together.
Cover and chill.

name: Favorite French Dressing
directions: In a bowl or jar with a tight-fitting lid, mix vinegar, sugar and onion. Combine salt, mustard, paprika and 2 tablespoons chili sauce to form a paste. Add remaining chili sauce and mix well. Pour into vinegar mixture; add oil and mix or shake well. Store in the refrigerator.

name: Creamy Dressing for Slaw and Macaroni and Everything Else!
directions: Blend.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Lazy Lavender Dressing
directions: In small non-reactive bowl using an immersion blender, puree ingredients until smooth. (Salad dressing can also be prepared using a standard blender.) For best results, prepare at least 1 hour in advance to allow dressing to marinate.
Refrigerate until ready to serve. Adjust seasoning if necessary. Whisk well before pouring.

name: La Becasse Celery Seed Dressing
directions: In a medium bowl, mix together the sugar, celery seeds, salt, mayonnaise, garlic and dijon mustard.
Whisk in the vinegar, mixing well.
Slowly add oil, whisking constantly, until emulsified.
(For the smoothest results, Kristi uses the whisk attachment on her Kitchen Aid.).

name: Tomato Soup Dressing
directions: Shake together in 1 quart jar.
Refrigerate.

name: Herbed Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar and cover tightly.
Shake vigorously to blend.
Chill thoroughly.

name: French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a quart mason jar and shake!

name: Eyes Wide Open Ginger-Horseradish Dressing
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a non-reactive bowl (or use a blender) and blend until smooth.
Adjust seasoning if necessary and refrigerate until ready to serve. Best prepared at least 1 hour in advance.
NOTE: for a less thick version of this dressing, add a little bit of water to desired consistency.

name: Makeover Creamy French Dressing
directions: In a small saucepan, combine the cornstarch and water. Bring to a boil; cook and stir for 1-2 minutes or until thickened.

In a blender or food processor, combine the lemon juice, ketchup, sugar, onion, Worcestershire sauce, salt, mustard, onion powder and garlic powder; cover and process until smooth. While processing, gradually add oil in a steady stream. Process until thickened. Transfer to a bowl; stir in cornstarch mixture. Cover and store in the refrigerator.

name: Creamy Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender or food processor.
Blend until very smooth.
May be refrigerated but.
Shake up thoroughly again or return to blender before serving.
Makes 1 3/4 cups.

name: Ruby Tuesday's Creamy Raspberry Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until thoroughly mixed and creamy.
Refrigerate until serving time.

name: Blue Cheese Bacon Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk the mayonnaise, sour cream, milk, vinegar, sugar, salt, garlic powder and pepper until blended.
Stir in the blue cheese, bacon and chives.
Cover and refrigerate.

name: Strawberry Vinaigrette
directions: Place the strawberries in a blender.
Cover and process until smooth.
Add lemon juice and sugar.
While processing, gradually add vinegar and oil in a steady stream and process until thickened.
Stir in poppy seeds.
Place in a jar and refrigerate until serving time.

name: Lemon Pepper Dressing
directions: Whisk together zest, juice, mustard, pepper, sugar, and salt together until sugar and salt have dissovled.
Add oil in a slow stream, whisking until emulsified, 2-3 minute.

name: Poppy Seed Salad Dressing
directions: In a lar, with a tight fitting lid, combine the first 6 ingrediants;shake until sugar is completeley dissolved.
Shake again before serving.
Drizzle over tossed salad.

name: Balsamic Salad Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine all ingredients; shake well.
Store in a refrigerator.

name: Strawberry Vinaigrette
directions: In a mixing bowl, place the mustard and honey, Slowly whisk in the olive oil.
Whisk in the vinegar.
Add the strawberry puree and mix well.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Will hold refrigerated for three weeks in a sealed container.

name: Spicy Ranch Dressing
directions: Combine above ingredients in a medium bowl, mix well.
Cover and chill for at least 30 minutes prior to serving.

name: Dilled Yogurt Dressing
directions: Process yogurt, onion, dill, dried mustard, and garlic in a blender or food processor until smooth, stopping to scrape down sides.
Wisk in mayonnaise.
Cover and chill 1 hour.

name: Come Back Sauce
directions: All ingredients go into a jar with a tight lid. Cover and shake well.
Refrigerate overnight before using.
This is also good on seafood. The recipe makes about 2 cups.
ENJOY!

name: Taco Salad
directions: Scramble fry ground beef until cooked and no pink is showing. In a sieve, run under hot water to remove any excess fat.
Add all ingredients together (except coleslaw dressing and crushed taco chips) Refrigerate until ready to serve.
When ready to serve, toss salad well with just enough coleslaw dressing to coat. Top with crushed taco chips.

similar.name: Taco Salad Dressing - Fast and Easy

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine first 6 ingredients in a small bowl.
Whisk in olive oil slowly.
Transfer to a covered jar and refrigerate to let flavors develop.
Remove garlic before serving.

name: Bacon Salad Dressing from the Biltmore Estate
directions: In a large sauté pan over medium-high heat, brown the bacon and cook until crisp.
Drain off two-thirds of the fat. Add the celery and onions to the bacon and sauté until lightly browned, approximately 9 minutes.
Add the vinegar and cook, uncovered, until the mixture has been reduced by half, about 3 minutes. While cooking, stir in the Dijon mustard and seasonings.
When the mixture has been reduced by half, add the sugar and cook until dissolved. Stir in the brownings and seasoning sauce.
Slowly pour in the cornstarch mixture, stirring until the dressing is thickened.
Cool, then transfer to a storage container and refrigerate.
To serve, dip out the amount of dressing you need (allow 2 tablespoon per person) and reheat the dressing in the microwave until it is very hot but not boiling.
Drizzle over the greens, toss and serve.
It keeps indefinitely in the refrigerator.

name: Lemony Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender.
Cover and process until creamy.
Serve over torn lettuce with Parmesan cheese and croutons.

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Italian Dressing With Parmesan Cheese, Lower Fat
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Refrigerate for at least an hour before using.
Store in an airtight container in refrigerator if waiting longer.
Shake well before using.
Keeps 3 to 4 days.

similar.name: Creamy Parmesan Italian Dressing (Weight Watcher Friendly)

name: Balsamic Dressing
directions: Mix garlic into the oil and rest for several hours to remove the harsh garlic edge.
Add the other ingredients and shake well in a jar.
The dressing should be used within several days or refrigerated, as garlic will go rancid.

name: " Chef Salad" Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients until smooth and well blended.
Store in refrigerator.
Will keep up to 2 weeks.

name: Sweet Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients and allow to set for a few minutes so sugar can dissolve.
Be sure to mix or shake before serving over salad or favorite steamed veggies.

name: Mandarin Orange Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Whip together balsamic vinegar and honey.
Add all ingredients to salad contents and toss gently.
Mandarin orange may be replaced with a tangerine, if you wish.

name: Lori's Soup Dressing
directions: Puree all.  Serve on any salad!

name: My Caper Vinaigrette
directions: Puree the capers, mustard, sugar, vinegar, salt and pepper.
With the machine running, add oil slowly until emulsified.

name: Cumin Vinaigrette
directions: In a bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, salt, cumin, and Dijon mustard.
Whisk in the olive oil; add in pepper to taste.

**Low-Fat Cumin Vinaigrette—replace the 1/3 cup olive oil with 1 tablespoon olive oil and 4 tablespoons plain nonfat yogurt or 5 tablespoons low-fat buttermilk; add 1 tablespoon water, or more to taste; combine well.

name: Quick, Low Fat Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Pour all ingredients together in a glass jar with tight fitting lid or a salad dressing cruet.
Shake vigorously and serve.

name: Fresh Herb Vinaigrette
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together vinegar, Dijon, salt and pepper until mixed.
In a steady stream, whisk in oil until blended.
Whisk in herbs.
Refrigerate up to 1 day.

name: Red Wine Vinaigrette
directions: Pour each ingredient into a small bowl.
With a small whisk, whisk the ingredients until well blended and slightly thickened (it's always going to be slightly thickened, so don't worry about getting that desired consistency).
Add to your favourite vegetable salad, and referigerate until ready to serve (or have right away, if desired).

name: Creamy Pesto Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together oil and mayonnaise until smooth and creamy.  Add buttermilk, cheese, basil, salt and garlic; mix thoroughly.  Chill for several hours before servings in an airtight container.

name: Louis Dressing... (So Don't Peek!) :-)
directions: Mix mayo, whipping cream, and chili sauce together.
Add all remaining ingredients.
Refrigerate.

name: Citrus Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all of the ingredients in a blender and process until thoroughly blended.
Vinaigrette can be made a day in advance and kept refrigerated.

name: Salad Dressing Mix
directions: Blend all the ingredients until a fine powder, in a blender.
Store in an air tight container.

name: Simple Sweet Red Pepper Salad Dressing
directions: Finely chop 2 cloves of garlic.  Wash pepper, remove seeds, Slice and dice red pepper, not too big, not too small.
Mix with olive oil and vinegar.  I like to use immediately.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Lemon-Nut Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well. Makes 2/3 cup.

Tip: To marinate with the dressing, place the meat, poultry, or fish in a plastic bag set in a deep bowl. Pour about 1/3 cup dressing into the bag. Close bag; turn to coat well. Marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes or in the refrigerator for 3 hours, turning bag occasionally. Drain, reserving dressing, If desired, brush the reserved dressing on the meat, poultry, or fish during cooking.

name: Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Blend in blender until smooth.

name: Mom's Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and season with some salt and pepper, to taste.
Pour over prepared shredded, cabbage and apples.

name: Basil-Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: In a blender, combine the basil, vinegar, and Parmesan cheese until the basil is pureed.
With the motor running, pour the oil through the opening and blend until smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste, pour into a small bowl, and set aside.
Whisk before using.

name: Creamy Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl or a jar with a lid so you can shake it!
Combine well.
Refrigerate for at least one hour before using so the flavours can blend.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Louisiana Avocado Dressing
directions: Put lemon, orange juice,, salt and mayonnaise in blender and blend at low speed until well blended.
Add avocado and blend until smooth.
Refrigerate until ready to serve.

name: Brown Derby's Special French Dressing
directions: Combine water, vinegar, sugar, lemon juice, salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, garlic, and oils. Chill. Shake well before serving.

name: Creamy Italian Dressing
directions: Crumble the oregano, basil, thyme and rosemary into a medium bowl. Stir in the salt, pepper, vinegar and lemon juice.
Whisk in the mayonnaise and then the gradually whisk in the olive oil. Stir in the Parmesan cheese.
Transfer to a jar with a tight-fitting lid and store in the refrigerator. Shake well before using.

name: Zesty  Diet Tomato Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl use a wire whisk to mix all ingredients until well blended.  Store in clean tightly covered container in refrigerator.  Keeps up to one week.
Makes about 2 1/4 Cups.

name: Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together until completely blended and serve.
This will make 32 servings, 2 T each.

similar.name: Quick and Easy Gourmet Vinaigrette Dressing

name: Flaming Spinach Salad
directions: make a bed of the baby spinach in a flame resitant bowl or platter (not plastic).
sprinkle celery and onions over the spinach.
fry bacon until crisp and place in between paper towels to drain.
remove all but 1-2 tablespoons of the bacon fat from the pan.
stir in sugar, vinegar and spices and bring to a boil.
Add bacon and cook until slightly carmelized
pour the hot mixture over the greens.
at the table warm the brandy and ignite pour over the salad
toss and serve.

name: The Palm Restaurant Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Crumble the blue cheese into a bowl and, using a fork, whisk in the olive oil.
Let stand for about 40 minutes.
Whisk in the mayonnaise and vinegar.  Whisk again just before serving.

name: Red Grape Vinaigrette
directions: I CANNOT REMEMBER RECIPE EXACTLY, I MAKE MY ADJUSTMENTS TO TASTE PLEASE FEEL FREE TO TWEAK.
In blender or food processor, blend grapes until well broken down.
Add cilantro and pulse until chopped and blended; if you use stems pulse longer, you want a fairly smooth consistency.
Add lemon juice,honey and oil pulse until blended.
add salt,pepper and cumin to taste.
Everyone likes it a bit different.

Note: this dressing must be refrigerated, so that is why i don't use an oil that will solidify, can use olive oil but you will have to remember to take out to warm up. Or for a healthy version reduce or leave oil out.

name: Bea's Favorite Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix first 6 ingredients together in small bowl.
Mix remaining ingredients in large bowl.
Mix both mixtures together and refrigerate at least 1 hour before using.
I usually pour the contents in 4 empty bottles of dressing. Keeps for 7 days.

name: Minty  Blue Spinach Salad
directions: Add all dressing ingredients into food processor and pulse until blended.
Taste and adjust if needed.
Drizzle dressing over salad just before service and toss.
Serve with crusty garlic bread.
Store remainder dressing in fridge. When using again, process quickly and let warm slightly.

name: The Palm Restaurant Basic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine the ingredients in a jar that will seal tightly and shake vigorously to emulsify.

The dressing can be kept, refrigerated, for up to 10 days.  Once it has been refrigerated, let stand at room temperature for 10 minutes before shaking so the olive oil will flow.  Always shake well again just before tossing with a salad.

name: Dads Dressing
directions: Mix garlic, sugar and mustard together in a small jug.
Add the vinegar, and then slowly add the olive oil while whisking with a fork, until emulsified.
Serve at room temperature .

name: Easy Honey French Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients, except canola oil, together.  With wire whisk, slowly whisk in canola oil until well blended.
Chill, serve & enjoy.

name: Nanny's Salad Dressing
directions: Combine and shake.

name: Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Pack all ingredients into a food processer and pulse until smooth.

similar.name: Low Fat Green Goddess Salad Dressing

name: Robyn's Salad With Pecans
directions: The above ingredients can be put in a bowl ahead of time, but do not mix with dressing until you're ready to serve.

In a blender, mix all salad dressing ingredients, except vinegar and celery seed first. Add vinegar and celery seed and mix for 30 seconds. Add dressing to salad and toss to coat just before you serve.

similar.name: Mandarin Orange and Pear Salad With Toasted Pecan Vinaigrette
Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad
Bleu Cheese and Roasted Pecan Salad

name: Brown & Wild Rice Salad
directions: Mix the dressing ingredients together and * pour half of it over the rice while still warm.
Allow to cool completely.
Mix chopped vegetables into the now cold rice.
Drizzle over the rest of the dressing.
Simple but delicious.

similar.name: Black Bean and Wild Rice Salad
Cold Sesame Noodle Salad With Spicy Peanut Dressing

name: German Potato Salad
directions: To prepare Salad:.
Fry bacon until crisp.  Remove to paper towels, reserving fat to use in dressing.  Crumble bacon; set aside.
Steam or boil potatoes until tender.  Peel and slice thinly while they're still warm.  You should have 6 cups of sliced potatoes.
In a bowl, gently combine potatoes, onion, pimento, egg, parsley, bacon, cider vinegar, and seasoned vinegar.  Let sit 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare Dressing:.
Bring water, vinegar and bacon fat to a boil.
Combine flour, sugar, salt and pepper in a small bowl.  Add to the boiling mixture, stirring vigorously; cook until clear.
Add dressing to potato mixture; stir gently.  Serve hot or warm.

similar.name: Dilled Potato and Grilled Corn Salad

name: Western Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender until smooth. Makes about 2-1/2 cups.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Ramen Chinese Salad
directions: Saute slivered almonds in 1 Tbl butter, set aside.
chop cabbage and onions - mix together. break up Top Ramen into small pieces and add to mixture.
Make Dressing: mix together dressing ingredients plus 2 seasoning packets and pour over salad and toss!

TIP: (You can pour this over the salad just before serving or --  I made the dressing ahead of time recently for a party out of town and put in the refrigerator overnight and went to pour it over the salad and it had become a glaze. The ingredients separated in the jar so I microwaved it for about a minute and it was the best I ever made - - I will make it like this from now on.
TIP: This salad is better the next day, the Ramen noodles soak up the dressing and its yummy.
ENJOY ~V.

name: Crunchy Orange-Chicken Salad
directions: Combine sugar, flour and salt in medium, heavy-duty saucepan; gradually stir in evaporated milk, orange juice and egg.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until dressing just comes to a boil and begins to thicken.
Remove from heat. Cover; refrigerate until cool.
Combine chicken, celery, nuts, dressing and green onion in large bowl.
Gently stir in oranges.
Cover; refrigerate for 2 hours.
Serve over lettuce.

similar.name: Mixed Greens With Orange Juice Vinaigrette
Maple Grilled Corn, Blueberry and Apple Salad

name: Fresh Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Process first 6 ingredients in a blender until smooth.
With blender running, gradually add oil in a slow, steady stream.
Process until smooth.

name: Lute'ce Dressing
directions: Make a paste of, pepper, mustard, garlic and a small amout of the vinegar.
Blend in egg yolk until smooth.
Start addin oil veryslowly until dressing is the consistency of mayonnaise.
Continue add in oil alternately with remaining vinegar.
Beat in sugar and Worcestershire sauce.
Fold in chili sauce and chill thououghly.

name: Exotic Chicken and Pineapple Salad
directions: In a bowl, marinade chicken with lime juice and tabasco for 20 minutes.  Drain.
In a non-stick pan, heat oil.  Sauté chicken on medium heat.  If it's the case, remove the juice that accumulates during cooking.  Salt.  Reserve.
In a big bowl, whip the dressing ingredients.
Add remaining ingredients to salad.
Top with dressing.  Serve cold.

similar.name: Thai-Style Chicken Salad With Spicy Peanut Dressing
Abby's Grilled Prawn Mango Avocado Salad

name: Genie Baby's Basque Salad Dressing
directions: Mince boiled eggs using all egg whites and 2 egg yolks in this recipe.
Mix oil, vinegar, garlic and eggs marinate 2-3 days.
Add mayo – chill add salt and pepper to taste.

name: Creamy Sesame Dressing
directions: Mix mayonnaise, sour cream, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, rice vinegar and cilantro into a blender.
Mix until combined.
To liquidify, add water.

name: Super Easy Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Combine the ingredients and use over salads.  I usually increase the recipe by 4:  1/2 cup mustard, 1/2 cup vinegar, 1 tsp sweetener.
Options:  Use balsamic vinegar instead of the white vinegar.  Use a combination or different mustards for a unique taste.
This dressing was especially nice on steamed cabbage and garlic.  I was eating the leftovers for breakfast!

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!

name: Jan's Beg for the Recipe Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in salad dressing bottle, and shake well, until well mixed.
If using a bowl, add all ingredients, except oil.
Mix with spoon, then whisk in oil.
Keep chilled in fridge.

name: Raspberry Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Mix well together.  Refrigerate over night.

name: Thousand Island BBQ Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.
Stir together until well blended.
Store leftovers covered in the refrigerator.

name: Italian Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients. Chill.

name: Italian Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients in a jar with a tight filling lid.
Shake well.
Pour over greens and toss gently.

name: Cathy's Caesar Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients, except olive oil in food processor.
Pulse until well blended.
Drizzle in olive oil to taste.
Add dressing to romaine lettuce; toss to cover.
Top salad with croûtons, bacon bits, and Parmesan cheese.

name: Wild Rice Salad With Raisins
directions: Preheat oven to gas 4 / 350°F / 180°C.
Cook the rice as per packet instruction. When cooked but still warm mix with 3-4 tbsp of the dressing, toss the rice around in the dressing and then leave to cool completely.
Make the dressing: Pound the garlic with the salt into a creamy paste.
Next work in the mustard powder.
Whisk in the vinegar and olive oil.
Season with plenty of black pepper.
Now toast the walnuts, spread them out on a baking tray and place them in the oven for 8 minutes. (Put a timer on, they burn easily).
Mix rice with cucumber, tomatoes, diced pepper, apple, raisins, spring onions and walnuts.
Drizzle with the remaining dressing and mix again so that everything is throughly combined and coated in dressing.

similar.name: Black Bean and Wild Rice Salad
Cold Sesame Noodle Salad With Spicy Peanut Dressing

name: Light Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Whisk all the ingredients into a bowl.
Chill for 30 mins, serve with a salad or veggies.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: whisk together everything except the olive oil in a medium bowl.
Drizzle in olive oil, whisking to incorporate.  Or just combine everything in a jar and shake until thoroughly blended.
This will keep up to 1 week! enjoy.

name: Yummy Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Store in covered container in fridge.
Lasts up to 3 months.

name: Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender and blend just until thick.
Add seeds last.
Serve on fruit or vegetable salads.

name: Raspberry Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: In mixing bowl place Dijon and raspberry vinegar.
Whisk until blended.
Slowly pour in olive oil until incorporated, whisking continually.
Add scallions and juice of 1 lemon.
Add salt and white pepper.
Whisk in raspberries.

similar.name: Raspberry [or Other Fruit] Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Easy Low Fat Mayonnaise
directions: Put everything into microsafe jug and mix.  Put lid on jug with pourer open.
Micro on  high for 2 minutes  Whisk, then micro again 2 minutes  Whisk again, and then finally micro for yet another 2 minutes
The mix will have thickened.  Now whisk to give a smooth custard-like texture.  Stir occasionally as it cools.
Transfer to a sealed container and chill.  Before using, stir each time.

name: Hearts of Romaine With Roquefort and Toasted Pecans
directions: Blend 6 ounces cheese, mayonnaise, and yogurt in processor.
Add 1/4 cup water and hot pepper sauce; blend until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper. (Can be made 1 day ahead.
Cover; chill. Whisk before using.).
Arrange lettuce on large platter.
Drizzle dressing over lettuce.
Sprinkle with pecans and remaining cheese and serve.

similar.name: Bleu Cheese and Roasted Pecan Salad
Greens W/ Beets, Haricots Verts & Goat Cheese Croutons

name: Tomato Herb Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a blender; cover and process until smooth.
Chill for at least 4 hours before serving.

name: 5 Star Balsamic Strawberry Salad Dressing
directions: Boil balsamic and red wine vinegar and strawberries.
Reduce by 3/4 (to 1/2 cup).
Add sugar, diced onion and parsley.
Simmer 10-15 minutes and cool.
When cooled, gradually whisk in the oil.

name: Cucumber Dill Dip or Salad Dressing
directions: Peel, seed and finely dice the cucumber.
Using a colander, squeeze out any excess water from the cucumber chunks.
Place in a bowl and blend well with the dill.
Blend in the remaining ingredients and mix thoroughly.
Cover and chill.

name: Soured Cream Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Season to taste.

name: Sarah's Hot Bacon Dressing
directions: Mix the clear jel with enough water to make a glue like consistency.
In a sauce pan add vinegar, water, onions, brown sugar, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil - slowly adding to the hot mixture the clearjel - stirring constantly until it all is stirred though out (we use a wire whip).
When thickened add bacon and grease and remove from heat.

name: Lemon-Olive Oil
directions: Peel the lemons with a potato peeler being very careful not to remove the white of the lemon.
Place the peel only in a morter with 1/4 cup of the obove olive oil.
Pound and rub for one minute or more.
Remove to a sterile glass jar.
Add the rest of the oil.
Marinate for 4 days at room temperature.
Strain and discard peel.
FOR THE DRESSING:
Mix the Lemon-Olive Oil, mustard, and mayonnaise whisking all the time, and put onto your favorite salad or antipasto.

name: Persian Dressing
directions: Put all the ingredients in a blender.
Blend well.
Pour into a sterile jar or bottle and refrigerate.

name: Spinach Salad With Cranberry Dressing
directions: To make salad:  Stem spinach, tear into bite-sized pieces and place into salad bowl.
Place avocado and onion over spinach.
To make dressing:  Combine cranberry juice concentrate, vinegar mustard, pepper and oil in a jar.
Shake until well blended.
Drizzle over salad.
Sprinkle dried cranberries over salad; toss gently and serve.

name: Light & Tasty Caesar Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk the mayonnaise, sour cream, buttermilk, vinegar, mustard, lemon juice and wrcestershire sauce.
Stir in the cheese, anchovies, garlic and pepper.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

name: Tammy's Best Salad Dressing/Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender ( I use the magic bullet) till well mixed.  This dressing goes well on your favourite salad, grilled veggies or as a marinade for chicken or fish.

name: Calorie Free Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar. Stir or shake.
Makes enough for a couple of servings, so store leftovers in refrigerator. Shake before using.
Note: Feel free to experiment --  red wine vinegar, balsamic, dill weed, etc etc.

name: Sweet Salad Dressing
directions: Wisk ingredients together and chill.

name: Caesar Pasta Salad
directions: Mix together well.
add pasta and lettuce.
Top with Parmesan cheese.
May substitute chicken and bacon for shrimp.

name: Chili's Honey-Lime Dressing
directions: Stir ginger into Dijon mustard.
Mix into remaining ingredients.
Serve chilled.

name: French Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together; shake well.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Balsamic Maple Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together in a bowl, or shake vigorously in a well sealed bottle.
Add water to thin (and shake), if desired.

name: Mum's Red Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and process (I use the "liquify" button), till smooth.
Store unused dressing in the refrigerator up to a month.

name: Strawberry Spinach Salad
directions: Combine salad ingredients in a bowl.
Put remaining ingredients in a small bowl and whisk vigorously. Toss dressing with salad.
LAWL count - 1 veg, 1 fruit, 1 fat.

similar.name: Spinach Salad With Berries and Curry Dressing

name: Roasted Shallots
directions: Place shallots and oil in a bread loaf pan. You may need to add more oil to cover all the shallots.  Make sure they are all covered.
Cook in a 250F oven until the shallots are soft and have turned a little brown.
Remove shallots from oil and set aside, uncovered in the fridge to cool completely before you cover them.
Pour oil through a coffee filter and let cool in the fridge before you bottle it.
Keep chilled.  I don't know how long it will actually last before going bad, because it only lasts about 2 weeks in my house.
BE ADVISED!  SOME LOCAL HEATH DEPARTMENTS CLAIM THAT THERE IS A RISK WHEN MAKING HOMEMADE FLAVORED OILS.
To help avoid any risk of illness I suggest that you treat this entire procedure like you are doing home canning.  SANITIZE EVERYTHING THAT WILL COME INCONTACT WITH FOOD!

I can't stress this enough.  Botulism is nothing to mess around with.  I have never had a problem with my flavored oils.  Always smell them before you use them.  If there is any doubt about the quality, TOSS IT!  You can't be too careful.
Be careful and enjoy.

similar.name: Garden Herb Salad With Roasted Shallot Vinaigrette

name: Roast Garlic Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: In a small sauce pan, mix the balsamic and the sugar.  Heat on low and reduce by half.
Pour balsamic reduction into a blender and add the mustard and the salt and pepper.
Cover and start blending on low.
If your blender lid has the small cap, remove it and slowly pour in the oil.
Once all the oil is poured in, put the small cap back on and blend on high for about a minute.

name: Strawberry Spinach Salad
directions: In a small skillet ver medium heat, stir sesame seeds until golden; set asie.
Wash spinach thoroughly; dry on paper towels and tear into bite size pieces.
Chill.  Blend oil, vinegar, sugar, onion, paprika and worcestershire sauce.
In a large bowl combine spinach, strawberries and sesame seeds.
Pour dressing over and toss gently.  Serve immediately.

name: Mixed Greens With Berry Dressing and Toasted Walnuts
directions: Place all dressing ingredients in blender; blend until smooth. (Dressing can be made up to 4 days ahead.  Cover and refrigerate.).
Just before serving, in large bowl, toss greens, dried cherries and berries, and onion.
Add enough dressing to lightly coat greens; toss to coat well.
Divide among salad plates; sprinkle with walnuts.
Pass remaining dressing.
To toast walnuts, spread on baking sheet; bake at 375°F for 7 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned.  Cool.

similar.name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing
Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad

name: Tim's Louis Salad
directions: Put 6 salad plates in the freezer to chill.
Dressing: Whisk together the mayonnaise, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco sauce, mustard, vinegar, lemon juice, pepper, garlic powder, and cayenne. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.

Beets: Put the beets in a small saucepan with enough water to cover them. Bring to a boil and cook until tender, about 30 minutes. Drain in a colander and let cool. Peel the beets with your fingers under cold running water. Cut into 1/4-inch-thick slices and set aside.

Asparagus: Prepare an ice bath by filling a large bowl with water and ice. Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the asparagus and cook until crisp-tender, about 2 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the asparagus to the ice bath. When the asparagus spears have cooled, drain them in a colander, then lay them on paper towels to absorb any excess moisture. Set aside until ready to use.

To Assemble the Salad: Tear the lettuce into bite-sized pieces. In a large salad bowl, toss the lettuce with the tomatoes, green onions, and half of the dressing. Divide the salad among the 6 chilled plates and arrange the beets, asparagus, shrimp, avocado, and  eggs on top of each serving. Drizzle some of the remaining dressing over each salad. Garnish with the lemon wedges.

name: Honey-Dill Vinaigrette
directions: Put the vinegar, honey, garlic, and dill in the container of a blender or food processor and process until smooth.
With the machine running, add the olive oil in a thin, steady stream until it is completely emulsified.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad
directions: Combine spinach and mushrooms in salad bowl.
Cook onion until translucent, in olive oil.
Combine cornstarch, apple juice and cider vinegar together until well mixed.
Add to skillet with onion and heat until boiling.
While boiling continue to stir until mixture thickens slightly.
Stir in honey.
Pour hot dressing over spinach mixture and mix in well.
Add boiled egg and bacon and toss thoroughly.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

similar.name: Spinach Salad With Bacon and Mushrooms

name: Tangy Russian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients until well blended. Refrigerate.

name: Brown Derby House Salad With Citrus Vinaigrette Recipe
directions: To make salad, combine all ingredients in bowl.
Toss with citrus vinaigrette before serving.
To make dressing, in measuring cup, combine all juices and balsamic vinegar.
Add enough apple cider vinegar to make 1/2 cup, which should be about 7 Tablespoons.
Pour into mixing bowl or blender, with mustard.
Slowly incorporate oils while mixing.
Add the zests, and season with salt and pepper.

similar.name: Salad With Walnut Vinaigrette and Warm Chevre
Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing
Summer Fruits and Field Greens Salad With Citrus Dressing

name: My Tangy French Dressing
directions: Put all in a blender and blend well.  Refrigerate.

name: Herb Vinegar
directions: You will need a sprig of rosemary to fit the bottle of vinegar; depending on the size of rosemary, you might neet two.
Usually the thyme sprigs are not big so you will need two.
At any rate, you can't have too many sprigs for your vinegar.
Peel about a 5" strip from the lemon with a potato peeler being careful not to get too much of the white.
Thread the lemon peel here and there on the skewer and add the whole garlic bud.
Add the skewer and then the sprigs of thyme and rosemary.
Put a cork in it and let it sit for at least 7 days; but better if it is about 2 weeks.

name: Fabulous Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Whisk everything together except blue cheese.*
Then crumble blue cheese [into which ever size you prefer] and hand stir it into the other ingredients.
Put into the fridge for about an hour so the flavors can blend.
*To make this recipe diet friendly you can use low fat sour cream, low fat mayonnaise and low fat buttermilk.

name: Outback Steakhouse Dressing (Mustard Vinaigrette)
directions: Shake all ingredients in tightly covered container.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Shake well before serving.

name: Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar with a tight fitting lid.
Shake well.
Serve over salad greens.

name: Spinach Salad With Bacon and Mushrooms
directions: Stem the spinach and place in a large bowl.
Cover with a paper towel and refrigerate while preparing the rest of the salad.
In a 9-inch skillet, cook the bacon until crisp.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Pour off the fat.
Put the skillet back over medium-high heat and add a tablespoon olive oil.
Put the mushrooms into the skillet and saute until they are soft and have given up their liquid.
Remove with a slotted spoon to a plate.
Put the remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil, lemon juice, mustard and salt into the pan and mix with a spatula to dissolve the mustard.
Put the bacon and  mushrooms into the pan and cook 30 seconds to thicken the juices.
Pour over the spinach, season with pepper and add the Parmesan.
Toss and serve.

similar.name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad
Spinach, Mushroom and Red Onion Salad

name: Victory's Twisted French Dressing
directions: combine all ingredients in large bowl whisk till well blended and smooth.
chill in fridge at least 2 hours before serving enjoy!

name: Appletree Sesame Tamari
directions: Place all ingredients in a bowl and beat well until mixed.

name: Oriental Coleslaw
directions: Put first five ingredients in a large bowl. To make dressing put remaining ingredients in a bowl and stir. Pour dressing over slaw mixture.  (Over the years we've changed a few things.  We don't add the dressing until right before we serve.  This way everything is still crispy!)  Also, I omit the sunflower seeds altogether because they keep getting stuck in my teeth. LOL.

name: Basic Olive Oil and Vinegar Vinaigrette (Weight Watchers)
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Shake and refrigerate.
Variations:  Choose one of the following or mix, to you taste.
4 cloves garlic, crushed.
Squirt of lemon or lime juice.
2 teaspoons of one of the following:
fresh chopped basil.
minced scallion.
fresh thyme.
fresh marjoram.

name: Roasted Garlic and Buttermilk Salad Dressing
directions: Preheat oven to 400°F
Cut top 1/2 inch off heads of garlic, exposing cloves.
Place each on square of foil.
Drizzle with oil, then enclose in foil.
Place on oven rack; bake until garlic is tender, about 45 minutes.
Open foil; cool garlic slightly.
Hold 1 head of garlic, cut side down, over medium bowl and squeeze, releasing roasted garlic pulp into bowl.
Repeat with remaining garlic.
Mash until smooth.
Whisk in mayonnaise, buttermilk, Parmesan cheese, lemon juice, and Worcestershire sauce.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Cover and chill until ready to use, up to 2 days.

name: Everyday Vinaigrette With Lots of Variations
directions: In a small bowl, combine oil, water, garlic, mustard and salt and pepper.
Mix well.
Variations:  Add ONE of the following to the dressing:  1 tsp sesame oil.  1/4 tsp cumin.  1 fresh Parmesan cheese, grated.
Herb Dressing:  Add 2 T chopped fresh herbs (basil, dill or parsley or a combination).
Poppyseed Dressing:  Omit mustard and garlic.  Add 1 T poppy seeds and 1 tsp sugar.
Ginger Garlic Dressing:  use 1 T each of vegetable oil and sesame oil; add 1 tsp finely minced gingerroot, omit mustard.

name: Broccoli Salad
directions: Mix all the ingredients of the dressing together and set aside.
toss together the slaw, pineapple, raisins, and dressing, enjoy.

name: Sweet Adeline's Homemade Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a salad dressing container and shake well.  Use as you would any other dressing.  Store unused dressing in the refrigerator.

name: Linda's Asian Coleslaw
directions: There are 2 options for preparing this dressing.

You can either mix ingredients in a blender, which makes it a little frothy, OR you can finely mince the garlic and ginger, and whip the rest of the ingredients together with a wire whisk. Either way, it's fabulous!
You can easily double this recipe. Just keep the 2 to 1 ratio for the garlic and ginger, and the soy sauce and vinegar.

name: Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Mix Vinegar, Juice, salt, pepper together.  Add Oil and whisk till blended.
Pour over your favorite salad.

name: Creamy Basil Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients in a blender until smooth.
Gourmet Magazine's  notes:
• Use dressing on mixed greens or sliced tomatoes or for chicken salad.
• Dressing keeps, covered and chilled, 1 day.

name: Strawberry Spinach Salad W/Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Put salad ingredients in a bowl and set aside.
Dressing:
Combine first five ingredients and mix with hand mixer on medium for 30 seconds.
Add Vegetable Oil in a slow stream and continue to mix for another 30 seconds.
Stir in Poppy Seeds.
Add dressing to salad and toss.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to let flavors meld.

similar.name: Strawberry Salad With Poppy Seed Dressing

name: Citrus Cumin Vinaigrette
directions: Mince garlic and mash to a paste with salt.
Wisk together the mashed garlic with the juices and remaining ingredients except the oil.
Add oil in a very slow stream whisking into the dressing as to incorporate.

name: Balsamic Maple Vinaigrette
directions: In a large jar with a tight lid mix all ingredients.
Cover and shake well.
Refrigerate up to 1 month. Shake well before using.

name: Creamy Parmesan Italian Dressing (Weight Watcher Friendly)
directions: Whisk together all ingredients and store in an air-tight container in the fridge.
Mix well before using.
Can be used on steamed veggies.

similar.name: Creamy Italian Dressing With Parmesan Cheese, Lower Fat

name: Healthy Buttermilk Herb Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender or food processor, combine mayonnaise, cottage cheese, buttermilk, and onion.
Process until smooth.  Add remaining ingredients and process 10 seconds more.
Cover and refrigerate.  Chill at least 1 hour before serving.

name: Healthy Chef's Dressing 1
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar with a tight-fitting lid.  Shake vigorously until mixed thoroughly,.
Place in a covered condiment container and refrigerate.

name: Fat  Free Herbed Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar and cover tightly.
Shake vigorously.
Chill thoroughly.

name: Easy Cranberry Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Whisk syrup, mustard, vinegar, cranberry juice, and salt to taste.
Whisk in oil until emulsified.  Enjoy.

name: Lemon Garlic Dijon  Vinaigrette  (Low Fat)
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar; cover tightly, and shake vigorously.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Our Favourite Balsamic Dressing
directions: Whisk all of the ingredients together.
Enjoy!

name: Orange-Mustard Dressing
directions: In a saucepan, combine orange juice, rind, mustard, honey & mint, simmer for 2-3 mins until reduced to approx 2 tblsp.
Cool slightly & stir into sour cream.

similar.name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing

name: White French Dressing
directions: Heat vinegar and honey in small sauce pan until thoroughly blended.
Add remaining ingredients, except oil, and blend thoroughly in blender, food processor or with hand mixer. Gradually add vegetable oil and blend well.
Keeps well in refrigerator about a week.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth.

name: A Different Tomato Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together.
Toss with quartered tomatoes and fresh mozzarella cubes. Can also add celery and green onions.

name: Balsamic / Red Wine  Dressing
directions: Mix together all ingredients except Olive Oil.
Add Olive Oil and mix well.
Note:  Better if allow to sit for a few hours.

name: Citrus & Herbs Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk all ingredients together in a microwave safe bowl.
Microwave for 1 minute on high.
Remove from microwave and whisk well.
Cove a refrigiate for 1 hour before serving.

name: Strawberry Spinach Won Ton Salad
directions: Directions:
Set oil at 350* and deep fry the won tons until lightly brown and crisp.  Or spray with olive oil spray and bake 400* for 5 minutes.
Cook bacon and crumble, or buy pre-cooked bacon and cut into pieces.
Put the almonds in a frying pan over medium heat, and continue to move around until lightly browned, and set aside until cooled.
Slice strawberries.
For Dressing, mix red wine vinegar, mustard, salt, sugar and raspberry jam.  Then add oil and shake or mix well.  Refrigerate after serving.
Layer salad ingredients.  Serve dressing on the side.

name: Shallot Vinaigrette
directions: Combine first five ingredients in a bowl.
Whisk in olive oil until emulsified.
Toss with your favorite veggies and salads.

name: Goat Cheese and Spinach Salad With Warm Vinaigrette
directions: Several hours before serving, remove the Bucheron from the refrigerator (it should be very cold); pull off the skin that encircles the cheese.
With a sharp knife, cut the Bucheron into 6 thin slices and place on a large plate.
Cover with plastic wrap and let stand at room temperature until ready to assemble the salad. (If the edges crumble a bit, don't worry--put the crumbled cheese aside with the slices.).
Wash the spinach in a sink filled with cold water, pulling the stems off the leaves.
Drain in a colander and dry thoroughly with paper towels.
Tear the leaves into pieces and set aside in a salad bowl.
Combine the shallot, mustard, basil, sugar, salt, pepper to taste, vinegar, and oil in a small bowl and reserve.
When ready to serve, toss the spinach with 3 tablespoons of the vinaigrette--enough to barely coat the leaves.
Arrange the spinach on 6 salad plates and arrange a slice of cheese on top of each bed of spinach.
In a small saucepan, warm the remaining vinaigrette over moderate heat.
As it comes just to a simmer, drizzle it in equal portions over the cheese and spinach.
Serve immediately.

similar.name: Roasted Beet and Goat Cheese Salad

name: Caesar Salad
directions: Mix all sauce ingredients together and put in the fridge until ready to use (can be done ahead up to 24 hours).
Heat a couple tablespoons olive oil in a pan and add the white bread and toss until golden brown.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Wash and drain the salad, mix in the bowl with the croutons.
Toss in the desired amount of dressing and top with parmesan cheese.
Feel free to add grilled chicken to this too for a main course.

similar.name: Caesar Salad from Salt Fat Acid Heat

name: Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients in a food processor or blender.
Blend until creamy and garlic is shredded, about 60 seconds.
Keeps well in the refrigerator.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: For the Dried red bell pepper:
Take a bell pepper and slice it length wise into strips about 3/4 of an inch thick.
Place onto a pan and "bake" at around 150 - 200 degrees in the oven. This will dry the hell outta them. They will shrink big time and fill your house with a nice odor.
Put them in an empty spice container and you have about 20 dressings worth.

name: Benihana Superb Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in an electric blender.
Mix at medium speed for about 5-8 seconds or until celery, ginger and onion are finely grated.
Add dressing to the greens of your choice and toss.

name: Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad
directions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Preheat oven to 350°F
Place pecans, touching each other, on prepared baking sheet. Toast in preheated oven for 10 minutes. Remove from oven. Set pan aside, leaving pecans on pan.

Meanwhile, stir sugar and water in small saucepan over medium heat until sugar is dissolved. Boil gently, without stirring, for about 5 minutes or until mixture is a deep golden color. Drizzle syrup mixture over pecans. Let stand for about 20 minutes or until cooled completely and brittle; coarsely chop.
Maple Vinaigrette: Shake olive oil, red wine vinegar, maple syrup, mustard and salt in a jar until well combined.
Toss toffee pecan, spinach, cheese and cranberries in large serving bowl. Drizzle with Maple Vinaigrette and toss.

name: California Salad With Curry Dressing
directions: Prepare all salad ingredients as indicated and place in bowl.
Combine dressing ingredients and mix well.
Toss dressing with salad and eat up!

similar.name: Apple, Avocado Salad With Mandarin Dressing
Spinach Salad With Berries and Curry Dressing
Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing

name: Hot Bacon Dressing (For Spinach-Bacon Salad)
directions: Heat bacon drippings in skillet.
Add sugar and vinegar, stirring.
Add 1 cup water and bring to a boil.
Mix cornstarch with 2/3 cups water, and stir until dissolved. Pour into skillet.
Return to a boil; simmer 5 minutes.
Add to salad hot.

similar.name: Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad

name: Roquefort Salad
directions: Pull whole leaves from core of lettuce, tearing off and discarding any brown edges.
Wash carefully under the faucet to remove any dirt;let drain about 5 minutes in a colander.
Shake off excess water and wrap loosely in paper toweling and store in refrigerator to crisp.( Since these leaves are delicate, tear them just before serving).
To make the salad dressing, combine the crumbled cheese,salt, pepper, and dry mustard in a screw-top jar.
Gradually whisk in the vinegar to blend the other ingredients, then add the oil and shake until well combined.The salad can be prepared in advance to this point. Serve within a few hours.
When ready to serve, tear the lettuce into rather large pieces, leaving the smaller leaves whole; put into the salad bowl.
Shake the dressing to blend, then pour over the leaves.
Toss gently to coat the leaves, place on individual plates and serve immediately.

similar.name: Green Salad With Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Greek Salad Dressing by Champps
directions: Combine ingredients
Add to salad.

name: Tarragon Salad Dressing
directions: Combine and put over salad.

similar.name: Simple Market Salad With Tarragon Vinaigrette

name: Roasted Beet and Goat Cheese Salad
directions: Roast beets:  Wash beets and place in tin foil.  Bake in 400 oven for 45 - 1 hour (depending on size).  Test beets with a fork to see if cooked inside.  Remove and let cool slightly.  Peel and set aside. (can be done in advance).
Vinaigrette:  Combine shallots, balsamic vinegar, olive oil and grainy mustard.  Salt and pepper to taste.  (can be done in advance).
In a bowl, add just enough dressing to coat lettuce leaves.  Divide salad on plates, top with red onion and sprinkle with goat cheese.

similar.name: Pickled Beet Salad With Avocado & European Greens
Greens W/ Beets, Haricots Verts & Goat Cheese Croutons

name: Warm Mushroom Salad
directions: Toss mushrooms with lemon juice.
Melt butter and olive oil. When butter is brown add mushrooms. Season with salt & pepper.
Cover and cook 3 minutes.
Uncover & cook  until liquid has evaperated and mushrooms are deep brown. Remove mushrooms and keep warm.
Mix together remaining oil and vinegar, garlic tomato paste, sherry vinegar and sherry.
Meanwhile melt 1 T. olive oil and add shallots, cook till soft and add vinegarette and mushrooms. Toss and simmer together 2 minutes.
Plate salad greens and spoon on mushroom mixture.
Top with shredded Gouda.

similar.name: Spinach, Mushroom and Red Onion Salad
Spinach Salad With Bacon and Mushrooms
Warm Bacon Dressing for Spinach Salad

name: Fat-Free Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing (Mix  It Yourself )
directions: In a shakable container, combine the dressing mix, the mayonnaise and the milk.
Shake until well blended.
Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before serving.
Enjoy!

name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Chill.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Low Cal Garlic Mustard Vinaigrette Dressing
Guilt-Free Creamy Mustardy Dressing - Soooo Good!!!!!!

name: Margurite Ellefson Potato Salad
directions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add potatoes and cook  until tender but still firm, about 15 minutes. Drain, cool, peel and cube.
Place eggs in a saucepan and cover with cold water.
Bring water to a boil; cover, remove from heat, and let eggs stand in hot water for 10 to 12   minutes.
Remove from hot water, cool, peel and cube.
In a large bowl, combine the potatoes, eggs, celery, onion, radish,  garlic salt, celery salt,  and mayonnaise.
Mix together well  and refrigerate until chilled.

name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing
directions: Combine first 7 ingredients in a large bowl, stirring with a whisk.
Add lettuce, radicchio, and spinach; toss gently to coat.

similar.name: Leafy Green Salad With Honey-Dijon Vinaigrette for Two
Fruit Salad With Honey and Spice Dressing

name: Easy Russian Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a covered container. Shake well.
Let flavors meld and store in refrigerator at least 1 hour ahead of when you need it.
Mix with green salad ingredients.
(Also good on fruit salad).

name: Sour Cream-Blue Cheese Dip
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl and mix well to combine.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Guilt-Free Creamy Mustardy Dressing - Soooo Good!!!!!!
directions: Blend all ingredients together in mixing bowl.
Chill for 30 minutes before serving.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!

name: Soy Sauce Mayonnaise
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend till smooth.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Oil and Vinegar Dressing
directions: Combine all the ingredients except the garlic in a small saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Reduce heat and simmer for 1 minute.
Add garlic and stir.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Buttermilk Dressing
directions: In a blender, blend buttermilk and cottage cheese till smooth.
Add herbs and salt.
Store covered in the refrigerator.

name: Russian Style Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all and whisk together.
Shake well before serving.

name: Delicious Raspberry Salad Dressing OAMC
directions: Combine 1 jar of jam, vinegar, salt and poppy seeds in blender and blend until smooth.
Pour into 7 pint jars. Each jar should be half full.
Combine the remaining jar of jam and the canola oil in blender and blend until well mixed.
Fill the jars the rest of the way with the second mixture and stir. Makes 7 pints.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix first 4 ingredients in a jar.
Chop garlic add to the jar.
Shake well.
Will keep for 2 to 3 weeks in fridge.
Makes about 1.5 cups.

name: Garden Herb Salad With Roasted Shallot Vinaigrette
directions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Wrap shallot in foil. Bake at 400° for 35 minutes; cool for 10 minutes. Peel and mince.
Combine shallot, vinegar, oil, sugar, mustard, salt, and pepper in a large bowl; stir well with a whisk.
Add enough dressing just to coat salad mix; toss.

name: Island Shrimp Salad
directions: Arrange the shrimp and fruit attractively on a large platter or on individual plates.
Combine all the dressing ingredients except the mayonnaise in a food processor and blend until fluffy. Fold the mixture into the mayonnaise.
Place a dollop of dressing on each salad (or serve the dressing around the table when you serve from the platter).
Garnish the salad with the toasted coconut, macadamia nuts, the bunch of grapes dvided into several little bunches, and the mint.

similar.name: Abby's Grilled Prawn Mango Avocado Salad
Tiger Shrimp and Glass Noodle Salad

name: African Salad
directions: Combine ingredients and top with the dressing.

similar.name: Tomato, Artichoke and Feta Couscous Salad
Pickled Beet Salad With Avocado & European Greens
Black Bean and Wild Rice Salad

name: Low-Cal, Fat-Free Asian Dressing -Similar to Islands China Coast
directions: Using an electric mixer, combine all ingredients until well-blended.
Chill for at least 30 minutes and enjoy!

name: Dijon Mustard Dressing
directions: In a 1 quart measure, whisk together salt, sugar, pepper, tarragon, parsley, mustard, garlic, oil, vinegar, and cream.
Microwave on HIGH for 1 1/2 to 2 minutes.
Beat 2 to 3 tablespoons of hot oil mixture into beaten eggs, beating constantly.
Microwave at 50% power for 2 minutes, until thickened, stirring once, during cooking.
Chill dressing.

name: Creamy Chutney Dressing
directions: Combine the yogurt, sour creamn, chutney, vinegar, lemon juice, allspice, and pepper in a medium bowl.

name: Honey Poppy Seed Vinaigrette Low Sodium
directions: Whisk together the vinegars, honey, mustard, and salt.
While whisking pour in the olive oil in a steady stream until the dressing is thoroughly mixed.
Stir in the poppy seeds.
Store the dressing in a glass jar in the fridge for several months.

name: Grandma Ramsey's Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all of the ingredients in a bottle or jar. Mix well. Don't refrigerate.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Dijon Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: Combine mustard and vinegar. Add remaining ingredients except oil. Mix until well blended. Add oil and incorporate one tablespoon at a time. Chill. Keeps several weeks. Use on salad greens.

similar.name: Almost-Empty Dijon Mustard Jar Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Bacon Salad Dressing
directions: I put all the ingredients in my mini food processor.
If you want to mix by hand, the bacon should be minced fine.
This is a thick dressing to toss with a salad.  if you prefer a thin dressing to pour over the salad, substitute milk for the cream.

similar.name: Bacon Salad Dressing from the Biltmore Estate

name: Creamy Vinaigrette Salad  Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in jar with a tight fitting lid and shake well.
Refrigerate.
Shake again before using.

name: Fat-Free Ranch Dressing!
directions: Mix ingredients together with electric mixer (smooths out the yogurt better than hand mixing).  Add salt and pepper to taste.
Chill at least 30 minutes.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Fat-Free Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing (Mix  It Yourself )

name: Maple-Almond Salad Dressing
directions: Lightly roast the almonds in olive oil.
Take off burner, add maple syrup, and cool a few minutes.
Add lemon juice and salt.
Whisk ingredients together.
You may also add your favorite herbs.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all the ingredients well and store in the fridge in a jar for 4 weeks.

name: Strawberry Salad With Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Combine first 5 ingredients in a small bowl, stirring with a whisk.
Place lettuce in a large bowl; add strawberries and almonds, tossing with enough dressing to combine.

similar.name: Strawberry Spinach Salad W/Poppy Seed Dressing

name: Lemon Walnut Vinaigrette
directions: Measure all the ingredients into a container.  Shake the contents vigorously.
The vinaigrette keeps in the fridge for a week.
Bring to room temperature and shake vigorously again before using.

name: Grilled Salade Nicoise
directions: Dressing:  In a food processor, combine the ingredients for the dressing, adding the oil slowly until combined.  Season with salt and pepper. (dressing can be made ahead of time and chilled).
Grill the Salad:  Preheat the grill.  Lightly coat the potatoes, onion, tomatoes and beans with oil and sprinkle with salt.

Lightly brush the tuna with oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper.  Put the potatoes over medium low heat and cook until they are fork tender and roasted looking, turning occasionally to keep them from sticking, 30 to 35 minutes total.
Put the onion, tomatoes and green beans on a medium high part of the grill.  Grill the veggies until they are lightly charred and tender, moving the ingredients around so they don't overcook.
The onions will cook in 10 - 15 minutes; the tomatoes in 5 - 8 minutes; the beans in 4 - 6 minutes.
As the veggies come off the grill, transfer them to a tray and drizzle with the dressing, tossing lightly and cover with foil to keep warm.
Grill the tuna over medium high heat until it is slightly pink in the centre, about 4 - 6 minutes per side, (depending on preference).  Let cool for a few minutes and slice.
Line four salad plates with the lettuce and arrange the grilled veggies and tuna on top.  Garnish with anchovies, capers, eggs and olives.
Drizzle with a little more dressing, sprinkle with basil and serve.

name: Bok Choy Salad
directions: Combine all salad ingredients and set aside.
Make dressing in a jar so you can re-shake when ready to serve.
In a pan, saute almonds and the crushed noodles in butter until brown and toasted.
When ready to serve, toss all ingredients together and serve.

name: Homemade Mayonnaise
directions: In a blender, add eggs and vinegar.
In a small bowl, combine mustard, paprika and salt.
Add dry ingredients to the oil.
Turn on blender to lowest speed until egg mix is combined.
While blender is still on, add the oil mix in a very thin and steady stream until fully incorporated. About 2-3 minutes. Mixture will be VERY THICK.
Slowly incorporate the lemon juice.
Store in large mason jar in the refrigerator for about 4 weeks.

name: Dill Sour Cream Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients in blender.
Chill for 2 hour before serving.

similar.name: Cucumber Dill Dip or Salad Dressing
Heavy on the Dill Salad Dressing

name: Cowboy Ranch Dressing
directions: In a medium bowl, combine all ingredients.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Cabbage and Apple Slaw With Creamy Gorgonzola Dressing
directions: To Make Dressing:
In a small bowl combine the mayonnaise, sour cram, vinegar and pepper.
Stir in Gorgonzola.
To Make Salad:
In a large bowl, combine the green and red cabbage, apples, walnuts and green onions.
Add the dressing and vinegar and toss well.
Cover and refrigerate until chilled, at least 4 hours or overnight.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Additional vinegar can be added if needed.
Sprinkle with parsley just before serving.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Mom's Creamy Salad Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients.
Pour over salad and toss.

name: Honey Dijon Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk all ingredients together.
Taste for preference.
If too sour, add more Dijon mustard and honey.
*Note: I often add heaping teaspoons of honey and Dijon mustard to get the right flavor for the vinaigrette.

name: Easy Taco Salad Dressing
directions: Remove the lid and plastic portion contol top of your dressing bottle.
Pour 1/2-3/4 cup of dressing into a pyrex bowl with spout and set aside.
Using a funnel, add the entire seasoning pkt. to your ranch bottle.
Put the lid on and shake to completely mix together.
Tap bottom of bottle on counter to free up extra space at the top.
Add what you can of the remaining dressing to the bottle.  (it won't all fit).
Chill.

similar.name: Taco Salad Dressing - Fast and Easy

name: Jacques Pepin's Vinaigrette in a Jar
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar.
Shake well before serving.
Keeps refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a screw-top jar. Cover and shake.
Serve over whatever mixed salad greens you prefer.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Use a 2-3 cup jar, such as one from Classico pasta sauce.
Crush garlic cloves.
Add garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, parmesan cheese, worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper.  Shake.
Adjust  worcestershire sauce sauce, salt, and pepper.  (We tend to use a bit more Worcestershire sauce than listed in the recipe.).
Store in fridge, shake before using.

name: Warm Seafood Salad With Roasted Red Pepper Dressing
directions: Dressing:.
Combine all ingredients in a blender until smooth.
Salad and Seafood:.
Saute seafood in olive oil unti the fish is almost cooked through.
Add the dressing to finish cooking.
Make a bed with the greens and veggies and add seafood (I usually flake the fish while cooking) and the sauce it's cooked with on top of salad.

similar.name: Simple Sweet Red Pepper Salad Dressing
Lobster and Caviar Salad With Green Peppercorn Dressing

name: Buttermilk Cucumber Dressing
directions: Mix ingredients together and chill for several hours.
Shake or stir well before serving.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together first five ingredients.
Continue whisking, while drizzling the oil into the mixture.
Set aside the dressing. Allow 30 minutes for the flavors to meld.

name: Carmen Jackson's Salad Dressing
directions: Lacking a 50 year old Heinz glass ketchup bottle put all the ingredients in a jar, skewer the garlic cloves on a toothpick and shake.
Adust vinegar and oil to your taste.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Mom's Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Make paste of:
3/4 t. lemon pepper.
2 t. salt.
2 cloves garlic - crushed.
2 eggs.
Gently whisk into:
4 T. oil.
2 T. vinegar.

name: White Wine Vinaigrette
directions: In med bowl whisk together first 5 ingredients.
Continue whisking and whisk in oil slowly and continuously.
Store in fridge up to 1 week. Shake before using.

name: Balsamic Vinegar Dressing
directions: Mix & blend together on high in blender until smooth.
Store in air tight container in refrigerator.

name: Apple, Avocado Salad With Mandarin Dressing
directions: Roast the walnuts at 350'F for about 7 minutes.
FOR THE SALAD.
In a medium bowl mix the toasted walnuts, red onion, goat cheese and 2 teaspoons of lemon zest.  Form into a ball. Set aside on a plate.
Cut up the avocado and put it on the plate with the goat cheese ball.
Drain the liquid from the two cans of mandarin orange slices and save the liquid from one can. Put one can of the mandarin slices on the plate with the avocado and goat cheese ball.  Set aside.
Just before you serve the salad combine the spinach, apple, cabbage and English cucumber in a salad bowl.
FOR THE DRESSING.
Put one can of orange slices into the blender with the juice of 1/2 the lemon, the 1/2 teaspoons of lemon zest, agave nectar and the olive oil.
Blend well.  Put in a jar and set aside.
PUT THE SALAD TOGETHER.
Place the spinach, apple, cabbage cucumber mixture onto your plate.  Top with some mandarin slices, avocado and bits of the goat cheese ball.
Top with the Mandarin dressing.
Bon Apetit.

similar.name: Mandarin Orange and Pear Salad With Toasted Pecan Vinaigrette
Apple and Spinach Salad With Sweet Seed Butter Dressing
Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing

name: Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients well then refrigerate.

name: Tomato Salad With Crumbled Goat Cheese & Pine Nuts
directions: Dressing:  combine the ingredients for the dressing and mix well.
Salad:  cut the tomatoes and place on a decorative plate.  Cover with the onion and the chopped basil.
Sprinkle with the toasted pine nuts.

similar.name: Goat Cheese and Spinach Salad With Warm Vinaigrette
Roasted Beet and Goat Cheese Salad
Tomato, Basil, Pine Nut Salad Dressing

name: Spinach  Salad Dressing
directions: Shake ingredients together and chill.
Toss in salad just before serving.  A little goes a long way.

name: Fresh Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Heat 1/4 cup oil with 1 cup basil in medium saucepan over medium heat until basil turns bright green and small bubbles appear, 2 to 3 minutes.
Turn off heat and steep 5 minutes.
Process shallot, garlic, vinegar, water, salt, pepper, and mustard in blender until garlic and shallot are finely chopped, about 15 seconds.
With blender running, slowly add remaining oil and steeped basil oil and continue to process until dressing is smooth and emulsified, about 15 seconds.
Pack remaining basil into blender and process until dressing is smooth, about 15 seconds.
Dressing can be refrigerated in airtight container for up to 3 days.

name: Lemon Basil Salad Oil
directions: Place first 5 (or 6 if using garlic)ingredients in a mini-food processor and blend until smooth.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

I used this dressing over a simple salad mix of leftover corn on the cob kernals, fresh steamed green beans, garbanzo beans and diced red onion. It only tasted better after sitting in the fridge overnight!

name: Balsamic Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Combine all the ingredients.

name: Chinese Cabbage Salad
directions: In large bowl, combine first four ingredients.
In small bowl, combine remaining ingredients and blend well.
Just before serving, add dressing to salad; toss to mix and coat.

name: Tequila Lime Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together well in a small bowl.
Serve over crisp, fresh greens of your choice.

name: Lemon Balm Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Cover tightly and chill for 1 hour until flavors have blended.
Use as a dressing for green garden salads.

name: Jalapeno Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: Put all ingredients except oil in a food processor or blender.
Mix until uniform texture is reached.
