Drizzle the oil in last, very slowly.
Check salt and pepper seasonings.

name: Honeyed Lemon-Dijon Vinaigrette
directions: Combine first 12 ingredients in a blender; process until mixture is smooth.
Add water and olive oil; process until well combined.
Note: Refrigerate vinaigrette in an airtight container for up to five days, and stir well before using.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Mix together.

name: Raspberry Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine the oil, vinegar, sugar, mustard, oregano, and pepper.
Shake well.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, mustard, honey, and lemon juice.
Store covered in the refrigerator.

name: Val's Low Fat Veggie Dip
directions: Mix together. Stir well. Chill in refrigerator and serve with your choice of fresh vegetables. The dip may also be used as a salad dressing.

name: Ww Core or Points Perfect Caesar Dressing
directions: To make the dressing, puree the buttermilk, mayo, lemon juice, oil, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, anchovies, garlic, salt, and pepper in a blender.  Add 2 T of the cheese and blend until combined.
If you choose to make a traditional Caesar you will need: 1 (16-or18-ounce package romaine hearts, cut into bit-size pieces. 1/2 cup fat-free Caesar-seasoned croutons.

Place the romaine into a large bowl:add the dressing and toss to coat.  Add the remaining 2 T of cheese and toss to combine.  Divide the salad among 6 large plates and top with croutons.  Serve at once.
Per Serving: (2cups): 90 cal, 6g fat, 1g sat fat, 0g trans fat, 6mg chol, 325 mg sod, 7 g carb, 2g fiber, 4g prot, 102mg calc.   POINTS value:2.
Good Idea:  For a main-dish salad, add 3 cups cooked peeled shrimp or 11/2 cups diced cooked chicken breast with the dressing in step 2.  The per-serving POINTS value will increase by 1.

name: Nasturtium Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, mix lemon juice, chives, nasturtiums and mustard. Add the oil and whisk to blend.

name: Vinaigrette Dressing for a Crowd
directions: Mix first 7 ingredients and let stand for a few minutes.
Stir in oil of choice.
Store at room temperature if using soon.
If using fresh parsley refrigerate any leftovers.
Oil made with dried parsley may sit on counter top.
*BEST IF MADE A FEW DAYS BEFORE NEEDED TO ALLOW FLAVORS TO MELD.

name: Chipotle Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix in mixer on low speed until well mixed.
Refrigerate.

name: Honey Mustard Vinaigrette Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Use heaping tablespoons of the mustard (sometimes I use a combination of yellow and spicy mustard-whatever I have on hand).
Place all ingredients in a jar adding the salt and pepper to taste. Shake until well combined.
This will keep for weeks in the refrigerator, just shake well before using.

name: Super  Spinach Salad
directions: Puree first ingredients in blended or processor until smooth and emulsified . Set dressing aside.

Cook bacon in heavy medium skillet over medium heat until crisp. Drain bacon on paper towels. Crumbled. Combine spinach and remaining ingredients in a large bowl. Add enough dressing to season to taste. Toss well. Garnish salad with bacon and serve.

name: Ww 2 Points - Lime Cilantro Dressing
directions: Bring broth to boil in saucepan.
Slowly add arrowroot mixture, stirring constantly until thickened.
Remove from heat, stir in lime juice and sugar; let cool completely.
Gradually whisk in the peanut and sesame oils , cilantro, and salt.
Refridgerate the dressing in airtight container for up to 4 days.

name: Buena Vista Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients, store in airtight container (I wash the mayo and mustard jars and store dressing in those).  Refrigerate.
Serve over shrimp or crabmeat.
When edited by Zaar the anchovy paste states "package".  Use half of a 1.6 oz tube of anchovy paste.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing (The Best)
directions: Blend mayonnaise, sour cream and Blue cheese.
Gradually stir in vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, sugar, salt, pepper and garlic.
Chill.
Excellent on sliced tomatoes and lettuce.

name: Sweet & Tangy Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Combine in a pint size jar with a lid.
Cover and shake well.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Ultimate Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients and pour into bottle.  Allow 30 minutes for flavors to blend.  Shake again before serving.
Due to nature of olive oil, it will eventually solidify under refrigeration.  If this occurs bring to room temperature.  If you do not like olive oil, feel free to substitute any other suitable oil.

name: Creamy Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Chop up eggs and olives.
Mix with all other ingredients except mayonnaise.
Fold in Mayonnaise gently.
Keeps in refrigerator for 2-3 weeks.

name: Mexi Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients and blend until smooth.

name: Chocolate Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Place shallots and mustard in a stainless steel bowl and mix.
Alternately add oil and vinegar.
Add all other ingredients and mix well.

name: Allie's Simple Vinaigrette
directions: In the work bowl of a food processor or blender, place vinegar, sugar, garlic powder, basil, salt, and pepper; process slowly.
While the motor is running, add oil in thin stream until all the oil is added and the dressing is silky smooth.
Serve at once or store at room temp for up to a week OR in the frig for up to two weeks.

name: Middle Eastern Vinegar
directions: Pour vinegar over herbs. Cover tightly and steep 2-3 weeks.
Strain into decorative bottle. Add a cinnamon stick for decoration.

name: Mexican Vinegar
directions: Pour vinegar over bruised herbs. Cover tightly and steep 2-3 weeks.
Strain into decorative bottle. Add a whole red pepper for decoration.

name: Lemon Mint Vinegar
directions: Remove 1x3" strip peel with vegetable peeler from lemon making sure no pith is attached.
Pour vinegar into hot sterilized pint jar. Add rind and spearmint leaves.
Place in sunny window for 1-2 days. Taste.
Remove peel and leaves when desired strength is reached. Add mint leaves for decoration. Seal.
Store in cool dark place.

name: Onion or Shallot Vinegar
directions: Pour vinegar over the shallots/onions. Cover tightly and steep 2-3 weeks.
Strain into decorative bottle. Small pearl onions may be added for decorative purposes.
A few drops is sufficient flavor for most uses.

name: Oriental Vinegar
directions: Pour vinegar over herbs. Cover tightly and steep 2-3 weeks.
Strain into decorative bottle. A single lemongrass stem and a few garlic cloves may be added for decorative purposes.
This vinegar should be refrigerated because of the garlic in it.

name: Mint Vinegar
directions: Pour vinegar over mint. Cover tightly and steep 2-3 weeks.
Strain into decorative bottle. Press the mint leaves against the strainer to extract all the vinegar.
A few mint leaves may be added for decorative purposes.

name: Pepper Vinegar
directions: Steep cracked peppercorns in vinegar 3 days.
Strain into decorative bottle adding a few whole peppercorns to bottom.

name: Garlic Vinegar
directions: Pour vinegar over the garlic. Cover tightly and steep 2-3 weeks.
Strain into decorative bottle. Garlic cloves may be added for decorative purposes.
A few drops is sufficient flavor for most uses.

name: King's Inn Bombay Salad Dressing - Copycat
directions: Blend all ingredients together in a blender until smooth.
Enjoy!

name: Cranberry, Pear, Apple Tossed Salad
directions: Shake dressing ingredients in a jar.
Toss salad ingredients with dressing.

similar.name: Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing
Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad

name: Fresh Apple and Parsley Dressing With Greens
directions: Combine apple, vinegar, hot sauce, scallion, parsley, salt, and pepper in blender; pulse, scraping down blender jar and adding water as needed, until very finely chopped.
With machine running, gradually add oil, scraping down blender jar as needed. (Can be refrigerated up to 1 week.).
To serve four, toss greens with 4 tablespoons dressing; serve imediately.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Thyme and Company Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar, seal and shake vigorously for 10 seconds.
Use immediately or refrigerate.
Shake well again before serving over greens.
If kept refrigerated, the vinaigrette should keep up to 2 weeks.

name: White Balsamic Blackberry W/ Lemon Thyme Vinegar
directions: Heat vinegar until steamy but not boiling.
Remove from heat and add blackberries and lemon thyme and allow to cool to room temperature.
Pour all into a sterile, wide-mouth jar and seal tightly.  Place in a cool dark spot and shake gently twice a day for about 1 week.
Strain and decant into a sterile cruet and use as a substitute for regular vinegar.

name: Red Leaf Lettuce and Watercress Salad W/ Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Make dressing:
Whisk together all dressing ingredients in a small bowl with salt and pepper to taste.
Make salad:
Soak onion in 1 cup cold water 10 minutes, then drain well in a sieve.
Toss lettuce, watercress, and cucumber together in a bowl and divide among 6 plates. Spoon dressing over, then sprinkle salads with onion.

similar.name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing

name: Sweet Dijon Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients together in a bowl.

name: Creamy Caesar Dressing
directions: If this is made in the "Magic Bullet" blender, add all ingredients in order listed and process for 1 minute.

If being made in blender or food processor, add all ingredients except olive oil and process for about 1 minute.  When mixture is smooth, turn on blender/processor and slowly add olive oil until well blended.

Refrigerate for at least one hour before serving, as flavors will blend. (Keep in mind that the garlic flavor will get stronger as it sits so if you really like garlic and aren't satisfied at first, it's better to wait an hour first!). Also, if you like a thinner consistancy,increase milk to 1/2 cup.
Toss with 2 bags cut Romaine hearts and sliced, grilled chicken. (Make sure to use any remaining dressing within 8-10 days.) Enjoy.

name: Lemon Chive Dressing for 2
directions: In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine the first six ingredients.
Shake well.
Serve over salad greens.
Refrigerate leftover dressing.

name: Black Bean and Corn Salad
directions: Drain water from corn. Rinse black beans of their juice and mix with the corn, red pepper,  red onion, poblano pepper and chopped cilantro.
Chop vegetables. Blend together with corn,  black beans and cilantro.
In a bowl, combine olive oil, apple cider vinegar, cumin, coriander and salt and pepper.
Toss with the salad and serve cool.

similar.name: Black Bean and Wild Rice Salad

name: Tofu Cabbage Salad
directions: Heat oil in medium skilled over medium heat. Pat tofu strips dry. Saute until light brown - about four minutes per side. After cooking tofu, add the sesame seeds and quickly toast, they burn quickly, make sure to watch them.
Combine vegetables in a large bowl.

Mix salad dressing ingredients, adding 2 tablespoons water. Can puree in blender for smoother texture. Pour 3/4 dressing over vegetables and toss. Toss salad with tofu strips and drizzle on remaining dressing.

similar.name: Cold Sesame Noodle Salad With Spicy Peanut Dressing

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: IN a blender or Food Processor blend together the first 8 ingredients until well combined.
Now Remove the top, keeping the blender/processor going on its lowest speed and gradually add the oil in a thin stream.
Keep adding oil until your dressing gets to your desired consistency. I find that the more oil you add --  the thicker and creamier the dressing gets.
Cover and Refrigerate 2 hours - up to 4 days.
This recipe takes 10 minutes to make top to bottom.
I don't like my salad drenched in dressing so for me this makes about 5 Family Sized salads.

name: Spicy Honey Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Pour over salad.

name: Aunt Gena's Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a shaker.
It's good to make in advance so all the flavours can meld together.

name: Famous Caesar Salad
directions: Put egg and garlic into bowl, whisk together for 5 minutes or until very airy.
Slowly add oil, continuing to whisk.
Add ingredients in the order listed whisking well in between each.
Taste at the end and adjust any ingredients until desired flavor is reached.
Chill until ready to use. The longer you leave in the fridge the stronger the flavor will be.
Cut or rip washed lettuce into bite size pieces and put into large salad bowl.
Add croutons, bacon, and Parmesan.
Slowly pour dressing over salad while mixing, it is easiest if you get someone to help you with this part.(In my house this is my DH job!).
Serve as a side dish or the main course.

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Honey-Mustard Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender and blend to a creamy consistency.
Keeps in fridge for 2 weeks.

similar.name: Better Homes and Gardens Honey Mustard Salad Dressing

name: Morton's the Steakhouse Chopped Salad
directions: To prepare vinaigrette: In a medium mixing bowl, combine the vinegar and 1/3 cup cup of water.
Whisk the mustard and salad dressing mix until well mixed.
Add the olive oil and whisk until the dressing is emulsified.
Let the dressing rest for a few minutes before using so that the flavors can blend. Refrigerate in a tightly-lidded glass or rigid plastic container for up to 7 days and whisk before using.
Note: If you use Good Seasons brand mix, two packets work well.
To prepare salad: Cut the iceberg and romaine lettuces into ½-inch squares. Transfer to a large mixing bowl.
Cut the artichoke hearts into eighths and the hearts of palm into 1/4-inch-long pieces and add to the lettuce.
Peel the avocado and remove the pit. Cut the avocado into ½-inch cubes and add to the lettuce.
Crumble the blue cheese over the salad. Sprinkle the bacon bits, chopped egg, onion, and tomato over the salad.
Add the vinaigrette, toss well, and serve.

Note: To hard-cook eggs, put them in a saucepan with cold water to cover. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Cover, remove from the heat, and set aside for 15 minutes, during which time the eggs will harden. This is a better method than cooking the eggs in boiling water. There is less chance of the eggs cracking.

similar.name: Harvest Salad W/ Squash Croquettes and Maple Ranch Dressing #RSC

name: Rosa's Zesty Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Place the ingredients in a glass jar and shake until mixed.
Chill and shake again before serving.

name: Low-Fat Ranch Dressing
directions: In a bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, yogurt, sour cream, lemon juice, green onion and garlic.
Season to taste with salt, black pepper and cayenne pepper.
Whisk in a little water to thin to desired consistency.

name: Once-A-Week Vinaigrette - Sara's Secrets
directions: Combine the salt, mustard, and vinegar in a glass measuring cup and whisk with a fork until the salt is dissolved.
Slowly add the oil in a stream, whisking.
The vinaigrette keeps, covered, at room temperature for 1 week or in the refrigerator for a month.

similar.name: Everyday Vinaigrette With Lots of Variations

name: Creamy  Country Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Process until smooth.
Store in a covered jar and refrigerate until needed.
Shake well before using.

similar.name: Low-Fat Buttermilk and Parmesan Cheese Dressing

name: Lemon Dill Vinaigrette
directions: In bowl, whisk together vinegar, lemon juice, water, onion, garlic and dill.
Whisk in oil until thoroughly combined; allow to sit at room temperature for 30 to 60 minutes before serving to allow flavors to blend.
Refrigerate leftovers, allow to come to room temperature before serving.

name: Spinach Salad With Berries and Curry Dressing
directions: Wash and dry spinach.
Whip together ingredients.
Add to spinach and toss lightly.
Add berries onions and pecans.
Toss lightly and serve.

name: Luby's Clear French Dressing
directions: In small bowl, whisk together sugar, mustard, and salt until well blended.
Add oil, a small amount at a time, whisking constantly.
Add vinegar, a small amount at a time, whisking constantly.

name: Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Juice lemon to measure 2 Tablespoons juice; place in small bowl.  Add mustard, worcestershire sauce, garlic and black pepper; whisk until blended.  Add mayonnaise; whisk until smooth.

While continually whisking, add oil to mayonnaise in a thin, steady stream.  Continue whisking until blended; stir in parmesan cheese.  Cover; refrigerate until ready to serve.  Store dressing covered in refrigerator up to 2 weeks.

name: Hellman's Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in the refrigerator.
Serve over salad greens as desired.
Keeps well in the refrigerator for quite some time.

name: Martini Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, whisk the vinegar with the garlic and basil.
Add the oil gradually and whisk to combine with the other ingredients.
Fold in the cheese.
And whisk again briefly before adding to a salad.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients together.
Refrigerate.

name: Romaine With Garlic Anchovy Vinaigrette
directions: Pound garlic with salt to as smooth a paste as possible with mortar and pestle.
Add anchovies and pound to a paste.
Add lemon juice and stir vigorously with pestle to incorporate.
Add oil in a very slow trickle, stirring vigorously with pestle to incorporate, and continue to stir vigorously until dressing is smooth.
Toss romaine with dressing in a large bowl and grind pepper over it.

COOKS' NOTE: If you don't have a mortar and pestle, mince garlic, then mash to a paste with salt using flat side of a large heavy knife. Add anchovies and mash into paste with knife. Whisk together anchovy paste and lemon juice in a small bowl, then add oil in a very slow stream, whisking until combined well.

name: Low-Fat Buttermilk and Parmesan Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Serve over fresh salad.

name: Creamy Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk shallots, mustard, and vinegar together and season with salt and pepper.
Mix well and slowly incorporate sour cream. Whisking constantly.  Add paprika, mix and correct seasonings if desired.  Thin with vinegar if desired.
Serve over salad.

name: Mom's Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Chill.

name: Caraway Coleslaw
directions: In a large mixing bowl, add the vinegar, salt and sugar. Mix until sugar is dissolved.
Add caraway seeds and let sit for a few minutes.
Whisk in the mayo until smooth.
Add the cabbage and toss to coat evenly.
Let sit in the fridge for at least 1 hour before serving.

name: Creamy Parm Dressing
directions: whisk all ingrediants in mixing bowl.

name: Cranberry Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients in a shakeable container and let sit to chill for 3 hours.

name: Tarragon Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: In a jar, whisk together first 3 ingredients (lemon juice - salt).
Add oil in a slow steady stream, whisking or shaking jar until blended.
Stir in tarragon. Shaking to combine.

name: Light Buttermilk Ranch Dressing
directions: To make the dry mix, combine the dry dressing mix ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Store the mixture in an airtight container in a dry place.
To make the dressing, combine 1 T of the dry mix with the mayonnaise and buttermilk and mix well using a wire whisk.
Store the dressing in a tightly covered container in the refrigerator.
Makes 1 cup of dressing.
Each 2 T serving contains approximately: 16 calories, negligible fat; 1 mg cholesterol; 112 mg sodium; 3 grams carbohydrate; 1 gm protein; negligible fiber.

name: Raspberry Pecan Salad
directions: Blend jam and vinegar in a blender for 15-20 seconds.
With blender on high gradually add oil in a steady stream.
Stir in poppy seeds.
Chill well.
Mix with salad greens when ready to serve or leave in a pretty glass bowl on the side.

similar.name: Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing
Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad

name: Mom's Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together and chill for at least 4 hours.

name: Spinach Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.

name: Goat Cheese Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Using rubber spatula, mix goat cheese and sour cream in a medium bowl.
Puree buttermilk, avocados, tarragon, parsley, and chives in blender until smooth.
Gradually whisk buttermilk mixture into goat cheese mixture until smooth.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Can be made 2 days ahead. cover and refrigerate.

name: Dijon-Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar; cover tightly, and shake vigorously. Store in refrigerator.

name: Strawberry Spinach Salad
directions: Melt 3 T sugar over medium heat and stir in the almond pieces.  Remove and cool on a cookie sheet.
In a large salad bowl, mix together the spinach, bacon, red onion, strawberries, cranberries and almonds.
In a blender, combine the mayonnaise, milk, vinegar, sugar and jam.
Serve the dressing tossed with the salad or on the side.

similar.name: Spinach Salad With Berries and Curry Dressing

name: Sonoran Salad & Dressing
directions: Mix romaine, tomato, onion, avocado, cheese and bacon in salad bowl.
Mix together the olive oil, vinegar, garlic, salt, sugar and pepper.  Pour over salad and mix well.

similar.name: Diane's Easy Thousand Island Salad Dressing

name: Blue Cheese Salad With Chive Dressing
directions: Mix together the lettuce and tomatoes.  Top with the cheese, beets, parsley.
Process the salad oil, vinegar, olive oil, salt, mustard, pepper and chives until smooth.
If making the dressing early, shake well before using.  Makes about 1/2 C dressing.

similar.name: Green Salad With Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Old World Dressing
directions: Combine everything except oil in a small blender (like bullet). Blend until garlic is finely chopped. Slowly add the oil, processing until the mixture emulsified, stir in salt and pepper to taste.
Serve at once.

name: Wasabi Vinaigrette
directions: Blend vinegar, lemon juice, wasabi, garlic and sugar in a food processor or hand mixer. Slowly add the oil while blending. Stir in cilantro, thyme, salt and pepper to taste. Cover and chill.
Let stand at room temperature 5 minutes before mixing again and serving. This dressing will keep for up to 4 days in an airtight container in the fridge.

name: Buttermilk Dressing Dry Mix
directions: Put crackers in blender or food processor and mix until powdered.
Add parsley flakes, minced onion and dill weed, blend finely powdered.
Pour into 2-qt bowl, add rest of ingredients.
Your done!
Package in to tight containers. Store at room temperature, out of direct sunlight, away from heat and moisture.
Use within one year, or give it to friends & family !
To make Dressing: 1 tablespoon mix and 1 cup of buttermilk and 1 cup mayonnaise, mix and refrigerate 6-8 hours.
To make Dip: Mix 1 1/2 tablespoons mix and 16 oz sour cream, mix and refrigerate 6-8 hours.

name: Mousseline Mayonnaise
directions: Place egg yolks in bowl and add mustard, vinegar, curry, shallot and parsley and mix well.
Add oil in a thin stream, mixing constantly with a whisk.
Fold in whipped cream. Chill.

name: Low Fat Mousseline Mayonnaise
directions: Mix all ingredients until well blended.
Chill.

name: Papaya Avocado Salad
directions: Wash and dry lettuce. Tear into bite sized pieces and place in salad bowl.
Halve and peel the papaya. Scope out the seeds and save 2 tablespoons. Slice the papaya.
Combine dressing ingredients and papaya seeds in a blender until the papaya seeds have the appearance of ground papper.
Store dressing in refrigerator.
Just before serving, add papaya and red onion slices to lettuse.
Pour dressing over salad and toss.

similar.name: Fruit Salad With Honey and Spice Dressing
Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing
Apple, Avocado Salad With Mandarin Dressing

name: The Great Caesar Salad
directions: In a food processor combine the egg yolk, garlic, mustard, lemon juice, vinegar, and Worcestershire. Pulse the mixture together until garlic is finely chopped.
Then, while continuing to pulse, add the olive oil in a very patient, slow, steady stream to produce a smooth, creamy mayonnaise like emulsion.
Add the anchovy, pulse for 30 seconds, and season with salt and pepper to taste. (Can be stored covered in the refrigerator for up to 4 days).
Place lettuce into 6 individual salad bowls (if wooden, smear with additional clove of garlic).
Pour desired amount of dressing over top of each salad.
Sprinkle with croutons, Parmesan cheese, and optional bacon bits.

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Sesame Tofu Caesar Dressing
directions: Heat a sauté pan over medium heat. Add the 2 tablespoons of oil and swirl to coat the pan. Add the garlic, season with salt and pepper, and sauté until the garlic is lightly brown, 2 to 3 minutes.

In a food processor combine the garlic, anchovy fillets, olives, and mustard and purée until smooth. Add the tofu and lemon juice and purée. With the machine running, drizzle in the remaining 2 cups of olive oil and the sesame oil. Correct the seasoning with salt and pepper. Use or store. Lasts 2 weeks, refrigerated.

name: Nyte's Low-Fat Salad/Potato Salad Dressing
directions: Blend sour cream, cottage cheese, Tabasco, lemon juice until smooth.
Add parsley, salt and pepper and blend a few seconds longer.

similar.name: Diane's Easy Thousand Island Salad Dressing
Creamy Low Fat Garlic Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Garlic Dressing
directions: In a small mixing bowl stir together mayonnaise or salad dressing, sour cream, vinegar, ,mustard, basil, salts, and garlic.  Serve immediately or cover and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.  Before serving, if necessary, stir in milk to make dressing of desired consistency.

name: Asian Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients until blended.

name: Japanese Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl with a whisk or fork.
Bon Apetite!

name: Tomato Salad With Goat Cheese
directions: Cover each plate with a layer of mesclun or mixed greens.  Place sliced tomatoes on top.  Salt generously.
Mix the dressing ingredients into an emulsion.
Crumble the goat cheese onto the tomato slices.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.  Pour the dressing on top.  Add the chopped parsley.  Serve with a slice of country bread.

similar.name: Roasted Beet and Goat Cheese Salad
Goat Cheese and Spinach Salad With Warm Vinaigrette
Tomato Salad With Crumbled Goat Cheese & Pine Nuts
Arugula, Pear and Goat Cheese Salad With Pomegranate Vinaigrette

name: Apple-Walnut Salad With Cranberry Vinaigrette
directions: Puree cranberries in processor until smooth. Add vinegar, onion, sugar and mustard and process until well blended.
With processor running, gradually add oil and process until well blended.
Transfer to medium bowl. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Dressing can be made one day ahead of time and refrigerated. Bring to room temperature and whisk before using.
Combine greens and apples in a large bowl. Toss with enough dressing to coat and sprinkle with walnuts.
Serve, passing remaining dressing separately.
Note1: I like Granny Smiths or Jonagold apples in this, although the original recipe calls for red delicious. I sometimes dice the apples, rather than slicing.

Note2: This is an older recipe, and I use much less oil in it these days - maybe 2/3 cup? I just pour it in and taste until it tastes right. I use about 2/3 grapeseed or other neutral oil and 1/3 olive oil.

similar.name: Pear & Blue Cheese Walnut Salad W/ Pear Vinaigrette
Salad With Walnut Vinaigrette and Warm Chevre
Apple Spinach Salad With Warm Maple Dressing
Apple and Spinach Salad With Sweet Seed Butter Dressing
Apple, Avocado Salad With Mandarin Dressing

name: Thai Cole Slaw Salad and Dressing
directions: In a bowl mix red pepper flakes, garlic, rice vinegar, sugar, tamari, and olive oil until combined.  Cover and refrigerate for at least fifteen minutes.
Meanwhile, combine coleslaw mix, cucumber, green onions, and basil.
Just before serving cover salad with dressing and toss to combine.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a jar, shake and serve with chicken nuggets or over greens.

name: Egg Yolk Vinaigrette
directions: Lightly whisk egg yolk and mustard.
Add parsley, shallots and vinegar.
Season with salt and pepper, mix well with whisk.
Slowly add oil, while whisking constantly.  Add lemon juice.
Note this vinaigrette should be quite thick.

name: Shallot Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk vinegar, shallot, mustard, sugar, salt and pepper together in a small bowl.
Slowly drizzle in the olive oil, whisking constantly until thickened.
Store covered in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
Bring to room temperature before serving.

name: Fancy Vinaigrette
directions: Combine the olive oil, cream, egg, vinegar, garlic, salt, pepper, Dijon, dry mustard, and sugar in a jar with a tight fitting lid.
Shake to mix.
Add: hard cooked egg, olives, chives, parsley and capers and shake again.
Store leftovers in refrigerator.

name: Herbes De Provence Vinaigrette Recipe
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar and shake until the dressing is well blended. Alternately, you can vigorously whisk the ingredients in a small bowl until they are completed incorporated together.

name: Creamy Gorgonzola Dressing
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the vinegars, yogurt, and sour cream until smooth. Stir in the crumbled cheese, salt, and pepper. Chill for several hours before serving.

name: Mom's Caesar Salad
directions: Lightly beat Egg Yolks with Salt.
Add reamining dressing ingredients and shake well in a tightly closed container.
Let sit in the fridge for at least an hour for flavors to mingle.
Toss into ripped Romaine (Romaine to be washed and well dried) and until all lettuce id lightly coated.
Sprinkle with Parmesan, Gruyere and Croutons.
Light toss and serve!

name: Chive-Yoghurt Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients until smooth and thickened.

name: Dill and Cucumber Dressing
directions: Cut cucumber into 1-inch chunks, leaving skin on.
Trim tops of dill from stems and discard the stems.
Place garlic into the bowl of a food processor, add the cucumbers and dill tops, and process until finely chopped.
Add salt and yogurt, and process for 30 seconds.
Chill until ready to serve.

similar.name: Cucumber Dill Dip or Salad Dressing

name: Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender.

name: Pumpkin Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients except the olive oil into a
small bowl.  Whisk well.  Add olive oil and whisk
until incorporated.  Makes 1 cup.

name: Northwoods Inn Creamy Buttermilk Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender or food processor.
Blend until smooth.
Chill at least 3-4 hours to let flavors meld.
Best served tossed onto chopped iceberg lettuce.

name: Italian Balsamic Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a small bowl or blender.
Blend well using a blender or hand blender.
Serve immediately or refrigerate.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: In a bowl, combine the first seven ingredients. Stir in the blue cheese.
Cover and chill at least 2 hours. Store in the refrigerator.
Yield: 2 cups.

name: Honey Mustard Dressing
directions: In a small bowl or measuring cup, whick together honey, mustard and vinegar.
Slowly add the oil in a steady stream, whisking continuously until all the oil is added and the mixture is emulsified.
Add the salt and whisk, adjust pepper and other seasoning to taste.

name: Sammy's Balsamic Basil Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients in blender and blend until smooth.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Toss all ingredients for salad.
combine sugar, lemon juice,  minced onion, mustard, and salt in a blender.
In a steady stream with the blender on low, add olive oil until thick and smooth.
When finished add poppy seed.

similar.name: Spinach and Pear Salad With Pomegranate Dressing
Pear & Blue Cheese Walnut Salad W/ Pear Vinaigrette

name: Spinach & Cranberry Salad
directions: Put the spinach and mushrooms in a large bowl.
Crumble the cheese over the spinach.
Cook the bacon in a medium skillet over medium heat until crispy, 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Drain the bacon in a sieve set over a heatproof measuring cup, reserving the rendered fat.
You will need 1/4 cup for the dressing. Pour the fat back into the pan and add the shallot and cook until light brown, about 30 seconds, stirring occasionally.
Add the thyme and let it crackle in the fat for about 10 seconds.
Add the vinegar, and season with salt and pepper and sugar to taste. Cook for about 30 seconds to lower the acidity of the vinegar. You should see tiny drops of vinegar dispersed throughout the fat.
Add the dried cranberries and stir.
Add Bacon back into dressing.
Pour the dressing over the spinach and cheese. Toss to coat the leaves evenly and melt the cheese a little.
Serve immediately.

name: Green Salad With Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Place the cheese, mayonnaise, heavy cream, vinegar, salt and pepper in the bowl of a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Process until smooth.
Place a wedge of lettuce on each serving plate with 2 slices of tomato. Pour enough dressing over the lettuce to moisten. Serve immediately.

similar.name: Blue Cheese Salad With Chive Dressing

name: Maggie's House Dressing
directions: VERSION ONE:
Right before serving:
Sprinkle the garlic salt over the salad.
Drizzle the oil over the garlic salt.
Toss.
Taste.
Adjust by adding more of either ingredient.
VERSION TWO:
Right before serving:
Sprinkle the garlic salt over the salad.
Drizzle the oil over the garlic salt.
Drizzle the balsamic vinegar over the oil.
Toss.
Taste.
Adjust by adding more of any one of the ingredients that you feel the salad needs.
Hint:  Do not do this ahead of time.  The salad will become limp and it won't taste as good.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar with a tight-fitting lid; shake well.
Refrigerate overnight. Yield: 2/3 cup.

name: K.k. Sbd Chicken Salad
directions: Mix diced chicken with desired amount of salad dressing and set aside while you prep other ingredients. Combine lettuce, red pepper and cilantro in large mixing bowl (not the bowl you're serving salad in.) Stir in chicken with dressing and mix lightly, until dressing is distributed throughout salad. Sprinkle with cheese and stir gently a few more times, then serve.

Put salsa in food processor and puree about one minute with steel blade. (Could use a blender or immersion blender.) Add Ranch Dressing and ground Chipotle and process 30 seconds more, or until well combined.

similar.name: Pickled Beet Salad With Avocado & European Greens

name: Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Beat together with egg beater and serve.

name: Mango Curry Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl purée a medium sized mango, that has been peeled, pitted, & chopped with coconut milk.
Pour into a larger bowl an add remaining ingredients (mayo, cream, yogurt, curry, mustard, ginger, honey, salt & pepper).
Purée everything together until smooth & creamy.
Cover and chill for ½ hour or over night.
Serve over fresh baby greens or a European salad mix with shoestrings of fresh pair, sea salt & pepper croutons and add cocktail shrimp & serve with fresh sour dough or similar bread.

name: " Russian" Dressing (Ww)
directions: In small bowl or jar with tight-fitting cover, combine juice, mayonnaise, green bell pepper, red bell pepper, tomato paste, yogurt, horseradish, onion, mustard, chili powder and pepper; whisk or cover and shake to mix well.
Refrigerate, covered, 2–3 hours.
Whisk or shake before serving.
EACH SERVING (3 tablespoons) PROVIDES: 1 Fat, 1/2 Vegetable, 10 Optional Calories.
PER SERVING: 49 Calories, 1 g Protein, 3 g Fat, 5 g Carbohydrate, 148 mg Sodium, 3 mg Cholesterol, 1 g Dietary Fiber; 1 points.

name: 360 Rocket Salad (Arugula)
directions: For the Apple Cider Dressing:
Combine oil, vinegar, shallots, garlic and salt and pepper to taste in jar or bowl.
Add Calvados and shake in jar or whisk well in bowl. (Makes 1 1/4 cups).
For the Salad:
Toast walnuts on baking sheet at 350 degrees until lightly browned, about 10 minutes. Combine walnuts, arugula, cheese and apple slices in large salad bowl. Toss with dressing and serve.

similar.name: Apple Gorgonzola Salad With Balsamic Vinaigrette
Arugula, Pear and Goat Cheese Salad With Pomegranate Vinaigrette
Fresh Apple and Parsley Dressing With Greens

name: Fruity Spinach Salad
directions: Cut melon half in 1/2 inch rings, then into 1 inch pieces;place in a bowl.
Add spinach, mushrooms, onion and grapes.
Chill for at least 2 hours.
Place sugar, orange juice, vinegar, onion, orange peel and pepper in a blender, cover and blend until smooth.
With blender running, gradually add oil until slightly thickened.
Stir in poppy seeds.
Cover and chill.
Just before serving, pour dressing over salad and toss.
Top with pecans.

name: Marra's Light Citrus Buttermilk Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all dry base ingredients together and store in an air tight container.
Whisk all wet ingredients together except the lime juice.
Add 1 to 2 tbs of dry base to wet. Start with one and taste as you go.
Add the lime juice a tsp at a time until you have the balance you like.
Let dressing sit chilled in the fridge for at least an hour to let flavors meld.

name: Julie's Creamy Raspberry Vinaigrette
directions: Put all the ingredients in the blender and blend until smooth.
Allow to refrigerate for at least 1 hour or longer as the flavors marry and it becomes even better.
**Note** if you really want to kick this recipe up a notch or two use heavy cream instead of half and half.

similar.name: Ruby Tuesday's Creamy Raspberry Vinaigrette Dressing
Creamy Raspberry Vinaigrette -  4 WW SmartPoints Per 3 Oz. Servi

name: Scrumptious Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients except olive oil to blender.  Pulse to combine.
Add oil very slowly while blending to emulsify.
All you need is the romaine lettuce, croutons and freshly grated Parmesan Cheese and you have a winner.

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Tomato Vinaigrette
directions: Pulse all ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth.

name: Magic Bullet Fat Free Cranberry Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Put all ingredients in Magic
bullet and pulse until desired cinsistency.

name: Creamy Garlic Dressing
directions: In a small bowl stir together all ingredients.
Use at once or cover and store in refrigerator.
Use within 3 days.
Stir or shake before using.

similar.name: Creamy Low Fat Garlic Salad Dressing

name: Old  San Francisco Dressing
directions: Mix salt, paprika and sugar in a quart jar. Add ketchup, mix well.
Add oil, vinegar and lemon juice.
Shake well.
Add onion and shake again.

name: Russian Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in the order listed.
Toss with a mixture of mixed salad greens.

name: Creamy Vinaigrette
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the vinegar, salt and pepper, and sour cream.
Slowly whisk in the oil until combined.

name: Cilantro Lime Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, stir all ingredients together with a fork.

name: Lemon Honey Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, mix the lemon juice with the olive oil and the flax oil. Whisk in the honey. Add more if you like your dressing to be sweeter.

name: Low-Fat Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Whisk all indgredients in medium bowl until smooth.

name: Honey Garlic Dressing
directions: Mix all the ingredients except the oil.
Slowly whisk in oil.

name: Citrus Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients.

name: Low Calorie Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: Cranberry Vinaigrette
directions: Stir and cook shallots and 1 Tbls. olive oil in a small saucepan over medium heat until softened; set aside.
Bring cranberries, cranberry juice and orange juice to a boil.
Boil until reduced to 1 cup, about 10 minute.
Puree mixture in a blender with remaining olive oil, shallots, vinegar, honey, salt and pepper until smooth.
Pour vinaigrette into a glass container.
Refrigerate.

name: Poppy Seed Salad Dressing
directions: In a blender, combine 1/2 of an onion and just enough water necessary to make smooth.
Measure 2 TBL of onion puree (or slightly more) and discard any excess.
Return onion to blender and combine with sugar and oil.  Mix well.
Add dry mustard and salt.  Add vinegar.  Blend all ingredients well.
Add poppy seeds and slowly combine with other ingredients.  Do not blend.
Refrigerate overnight for best flavor.

name: Raspberry Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk everything together and top with almonds.

I sometimes use all raspberry vinegar instead of half raspberry and half rice wine vinegar, but it can be very tart and it will also seem sweeter. If you do that, decrease the sugar, to taste. I use either the almonds or the poppy seeds - sometimes neither and it's still great tasting!

name: Buttermilk Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate for at least four hours to let flavors blend.

name: Julia Child's Lemon Oil Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients with a whisk in a measuring cup.
Use immediately.

name: Low-Fat Ranch Dressing
directions: Using a food processor or blender, puree all ingredients together until smooth.
Chill before serving.
The serving size is approximately 2 tablespoons and the recipe yields about 1 cup.

name: Low Fat Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Using blender or food processor, puree cottage cheese.  Add remaining ingredients and puree until smooth.
If dressing is too thick for your taste use non-fat milk or red wine vinegar to thin.
This recipe yields approximately 1 1/2 cups of dressing and the serving size is 2 tablespoons of dressing.

name: Peanut Thai Dressing
directions: Whisk all ingredients in a small bowl until well blended.
Serve!

name: Roquefort Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, stirring with a wire whisk until blended.
Cover and chill.
Serve over salad greens.

name: Chipotle Cilantro Lime Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients except oil in a quart container.
With a burr mixer, slowly add oil.  Extra oil may be needed depending on consistency desired.
If a burr mixer is unavailable, you can use a blender or a robot-coup.  Both have a hole on the lid, so you can still gradually add the oil.
Salt and pepper are added only to taste.

name: Light Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: Put yogurt in cheese cloth in a small strainer over a bowl.  Let drain in fridge 20 minutes.
Combine buttermilk, yogurt, vinegar, sugar and mayo til well blended.  Season to taste.  Fold in bleu cheese.

name: Garlic-Herb Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Wisk together all ingredients and thoroughly chill.
I like to use powdered garlic instead of a crushed clove for consistency. Passive time listed is chilling time.

name: Low Fat Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: In medium bowl, whisk together blue cheese, sour cream, and mayo. Carefully stir in remaining ingredients.

name: Italian Salad Dressing
directions: Combine everything together in container.
Shake well.
Serve.

name: The Best Salad Dressing Ever!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
directions: Combine first four ingredients with whisk. (We love garlic so we always use more.).
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Add a few drops of water if necessary to reach desired taste and consistency.

name: Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Mash blue cheese with some of the mayonnaise until well blended.
Transfer to a food processor or blender.
Add remaining mayonnaise with sour cream, milk, lemon juice, mustard, cayenne,  garlic powder and pepper.
Blend until smooth.
Transfer to a bowl or jar, cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.

name: Blood Orange Almond Vinaigrette
directions: Bring blood orange juice to a boil in medium saucepan. Lower heat to a simmer and reduce until you have 1/3 cup of blood orange juice.
In a medium bowl, combine reduced blood orange juice, vinegar, shallot, thyme and blood orange zest.
Slowly drizzle oils into mixture while whisking until combined and thick. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Refrigerate until needed.

name: Strawberry Salad Dressing
directions: Wash & dry lettuce.
Crumble Blue Cheese.
Add walnuts & dried cranberries.

name: Greek Salad and Dressing
directions: Add the salad ingredients in a large salad bowl.
Whisk together the dressing ingredients and  over salad, toss well to coat all vegetables.

name: Claim Jumper's Citrus Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients well and refrigerate to meld flavors.
Before serving, whisk well to combine again.
From: John Casey, Claim Jumper Restaurant.
Source: Daily Press, Cook's Exchange by Veronica Hill.

name: Krakas Untimate French Dressing
directions: Mix together in a large container and store in the refrigerator.

name: Ann's Famous Salad
directions: Dressing is best made the day before.
Mix all dressing ingredients in bowl and let stand at room temperature.
Wash and tear Romaine lettuce place in large bowl.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and set aside.
Layer Swiss cheese, Parmesan Cheese, Bacon and Croutons.
Put dressing on salad and toss.  Serve immediately.

name: Tomato, Basil, Pine Nut Salad Dressing
directions: In a bowl, mix the olive oil, balsamic vinegar, sun-dried tomatoes, basil, pine nuts, water, and sugar.

similar.name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing

name: Warm Quinoa & Broccoli Salad With Carrot-Ginger Dressing
directions: In sieve, rinse quinoa with cold running water. In 3-quart saucepan, combine quinoa, 3 cups water, and 1/2 teaspoon salt; heat to boiling over high heat. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer 20 minutes or until water is absorbed. Transfer quinoa to large bowl.
Meanwhile, place broccoli and 1/4 cup water in microwave-safe medium bowl; cover and cook in microwave oven on High 4 to 5 minutes or until tender-crisp. Drain; add to quinoa in bowl.

In blender, combine carrot, ginger, vegetable oil, vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, remaining 3 tablespoons water, and 1/4 teaspoon salt; blend until pureed. Add to quinoa and broccoli and toss to combine. Serve salad warm or at room temperature.

similar.name: Cold Sesame Noodle Salad With Spicy Peanut Dressing

name: Easy Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all together an shake well.
Serve over salad with mandarin oranges and slivered almonds.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Cranberry Salad Dressing
directions: In a small saucepan, place berries, sugar and vinegars.  Bring to a boil, turn down to a constant simmer and cook about 5 minutes- until berries start to pop.  (If using frozen or dried berries, just time this for 5-6 minutes as berries will not pop.)
Cool to room temperature.
Puree in food processor.  Add mustard, orange juice, salt and pepper.
With processor running, add oil through drip tube.
Keeps 1-2 weeks in refrigerator.  I have made half batches very successfully, or this can be frozen.

name: Guilt-Free Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine cottage cheese, mayonnaise, anchovies, lemon juice, Worcestershire, garlic and olive oil in a food processor and puree until smooth.
Stir in Parmesan and season with salt and pepper to taste.
If dressing is too thick, thin down with a little warm water.

name: Bleu Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix cream cheese and bleu cheese.
Add in mayonnaise until creamy.
Add rest of indgredients and mix well.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients with a hand blender until smooth.
Store in refrigerator for up to 2 days.

name: Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Combine first 4 ingredients in a bowl.
Whisk in oil; stir in salt and pepper.

name: Tarragon Vinaigrette
directions: Process first 5 ingredients in a blender until smooth, stopping to scrape down sides.
Turn blender on high, and add oil in a slow, steady stream. Stir in tarragon.

name: Balsamic-Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar; cover tightly, and shake vigorously.

name: Ginger-Lime Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, stirring until blended.
Cover and chill at least 1 hour.

name: Peach Dressing
directions: Process all ingredients in a blender until smooth, stopping to scrape down sides.

name: Smoky Tomato Balsamic Dressing
directions: Process ingredients in a blender until well blended.

name: Sesame Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients; refrigerate until serving time.
Mix again before serving.

name: Mustard Seed Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients together; refrigerate until serving time.
Mix again before serving.

name: Honey Lime Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Mix again before serving.

name: Napa Cabbage Salad
directions: The Salad.
Saute broken up noodles, sunflower seeds, and almonds in olive oil until brown.
let cool and mix with cabbage, red pepper and onions.
Just before serving mix dressing in with salad or it gets soggy.
I saute the crunchy stuff and keep it in a closed container until ready to use.

name: Gramma G's Salad Dressing
directions: Add all to a jar or container with a lid; shake well.
Refrigerate for at least one hour before serving.

name: Poppy Seed Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender.  Process on low speed for 30 seconds.
Cover and chill.  Stir well before serving.

name: Broccoli  Slaw
directions: Make dressing in covered container and shake well.
Pour over slaw.
Can be made ahead of time and refrigerated,
Stores well.

name: Citrus Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Whisk all together in a bowl or add to a blender and process 30 seconds. Pour into your favorite salad dressing container. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Keeps for 4-7 days.

name: Moonlight Family Restaurant Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients by hand.  DO NOT use mixer.
If too thick add vinegar.
Add to 3 to 4 cups romaine lettuce.

similar.name: Cooking Light's Creamy Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Italian Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients and let stand at least one hour in refrigerator (better if overnight). You will want to use container for salad dressing so the garlic does not fall out.
Sprinkle salads with garlic powder before using dressing.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Blend slowly in blender all items except oil and poppy seeds.
Add oil while blender is going.
Add poppy seeds.
Refrigerate until ready to use.

name: Smoked Yellow Pepper Vinaigrette
directions: In a blender, combine the peppers, chipotle, onion, and lime juice
and blend. While the blender is still running, slowly add the olive
oil until emulsified. Add the sugar and season with salt and pepper
to taste. This recipe yields about 1 cup of vinaigrette.

similar.name: Smoked Paprika and Red Pepper Dressing

name: Lobster and Caviar Salad With Green Peppercorn Dressing
directions: Steam or boil lobster (cook it!) for 15 minutes.
Cut lobster in half; place on a serving platter with caviar, arugula and red lettuce leaves.
Bruise dressing ingredients, ideally with a mortar. Drizzle onto salad.

similar.name: Warm Seafood Salad With Roasted Red Pepper Dressing
Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing
Leafy Green Salad With Honey-Dijon Vinaigrette for Two

name: Playboy Sesame Chicken Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Serve with salad.

name: Spicy Cilantro Lime Dressing
directions: Place lime juice, cilantro, garlic, salt, mustard, both peppers and cumin in small bowl and whisk to combine. Add olive oil and whisk again until well-blended.
Store in cruet or glass jar in the refrigerator. Spicy Cilantro Lime Dressing will keep 5 days.

similar.name: Like Cafe Rio/Coata Vida Cilantro Lime Dressing

name: Chile-Cilantro Dressing
directions: Place the first 8 ingredients in a blender; process 15 seconds.
Gradually add cilantro; process until smooth.
Combine 2 tablespoons water and yogurt in a small bowl, stirring with a whisk until smooth.
Add cilantro mixture; stir well.

name: Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Place the egg yolks, mayonnaise, and sour cream in a blender and blend until yolks are incorporated into the sour cream and mayonnaise. Add all other ingredients and blend on the lowest setting, stopping and scraping down the sides of the blender occasionally to make sure all the herbs are being chopped up. Puree the dressing in the blender until all the herbs are pureed and well incorporated into the dressing.

name: California Pizza Kitchen White Balsamic Provencal Salad
directions: Vinaigrette Preparation.
n a mixing bowl, use a hand held electric mixer on low speed or a whisk to blend together the vinegar, garlic, herbs, salt, lemon zest, and pepper.
Beating or whisking continuously, slowly pour in the oil, blending until it is thoroughly incorporated. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
Salad Preparation:.
In a large mixing bowl, use salad servers to toss together the romaine, arugula, cabbage and dressing.
Transfer the salad to chilled serving plates. Surround each serving with a ring of diced Roma tomatoes, then top with sun dried tomatoes, feta cheese and Kalamata olives.

name: Green Bean Chicken Salad
directions: In a large bowl, whisk the vinegar, basil, 1 tablespoon of oil, sugar and salt; set aside.  In a nonstick skillet, saute chicken in remaining oil over medium heat until juices run clear.  Add to vinaigrette and toss to coat.

Place green beans in a steamer basket in a saucepan over 1 inch of water.  Bring to a boil; cover and steam for 5-10 minutes or until crisp-tender.  Add to chicken mixture.  Add tomatoes and toss to coat.  Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.  Toss before serving.

similar.name: Cafe Green Salad by Melissa D'arabian
Black Bean and Wild Rice Salad

name: Asian Style Dressing- from Jamie Oliver's Winter Salad With Duck
directions: Juice two limes into a bowl big enough for you to whisk your dressing.
Add the olive oil, sesame oil, soy sauce, and sugar to the bowl.
Grate the small chili (red pepper flakes may be substituted here) and the garlic clove over the bowl into your dressing.
Whisk together.
Finely chop the scallion tops and cilantro and stir into dressing.

similar.name: Low-Cal, Fat-Free Asian Dressing -Similar to Islands China Coast

name: Sesame Miso Dressing
directions: Place all the ingredients together in a small screw top jar and shake well.

name: Honey, Mustard & Raspberry Vinaigrette
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge.

name: Red Wine Vinegar Salad Dressing/Marinade (Low Sodium)
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Refrigerate any leftovers.

similar.name: Red Wine Vinegar and Oil Dressing/ Marinade

name: Roasted Garlic Dressing
directions: Wisk together the buttermilk, roasted garlic (preferable still warm), vinegar and salt in a small bowl.
Add the cayenne peper or smoked paprika 1/4 teaspoon at a time, tasting after each addition.
Refrigerate in an airtight container until ready to use; shake before using.

Note: To roast garlic, Slice the top off 1 head of garlic so that the tops of the cloves inside are exposed. Lightly drizzle with olive oil and wrap tightly in aluminum foil. Bake in a preheated 425-degree oven for about 45 minutes or until garlic has softened and browned, set aside until cool enough to handle, then squeeze the softened garlic cloves out of there skins and discard their stem ends - dressing can be refrigerated up to two days in an airtight container.

similar.name: Roasted Garlic and Buttermilk Salad Dressing

name: Champagne Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar and shake until the dressing is well blended. Alternately, you can vigorously whisk the ingredients in a small bowl until they are completed incorporated together.

name: Low Carb Balsamic Dressing
directions: Blend together. Chill.
Let sit overnight.

name: Thousand Island Dressing (Homemade)
directions: Get out a pint size canning jar or clean peanut butter jar, with a good lid.  Measure all of the ingredients into the jar.  Put the lid on and shake it up until it is all smooth.  Store in the fridge, makes about 1-2/3 cup.  This is especially good on wedges of iceberg lettuce.  It is also nice on submarine sandwiches.

name: Moosewood Caesar Dressing
directions: In a small bowl or ramekin, cover the sun-dried tomatoes with 1/4 cup boiling water and set aside to soak for 10 minutes.  Drain and roughly chop the softened sun-dried tomatoes.
Place all ingredients in a blender and whirl until smooth.
Drizzle dressing on romaine lettuce and toss with optional croutons and Parmesan.
Note: I didn't have a lemon so used 3 tablespoons of the tomato water and a couple additional capers.

name: Cheesy Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Bake bread cubes 8 min 325 degrees and then fry lightly in some of the olive oil and garlic, as needed. Toss until crispy and golden.
Set aside to drain on paper towels.
Crush the garlic cloves and salt into a paste in a large wooden salad bowl, using the back of a wooden salad serving spoon.
Add the anchovies and do the same. Add the Worcestershire sauce, dry mustard, lemon juice and pepper. Whisk in the remaining oil until thoroughly combined.
Add the grated Parmesan cheese and whisk again until combined.
Add the coddled eggs and whisk until blended toss.
Add lettuce and toss until coated Top with shaved or shredded Parmesan cheese. Serve.

* To Make Coddled Eggs: Put egg (in the shell) in saucepan of room temperature water. Bring water to a simmer and simmer 1 minute. Drain and cool egg immediately by running cool water over it. It will be soft and the white still runny.
Adding the eggs is optional but oh so good.
The volumes have ranges to play with your own palette.
Reserve left over dressing as it will keep for weeks.

similar.name: Bold and Tasty Caesar Salad Dressing

name: Spinach, Avocado, Tomato Salad
directions: Combine dressing ingredients in a bowl and whip to combine. Pit, peel and slice avocado. Dice tomato. Combine salad ingredients and top with dressing.

name: Bow-Tie Salad With Tuna
directions: Cook pasta as package directs.  Drain; rinse under running cold water.
Dressing:  Drain oil from tuna into a large serving bowl; reserve tuna.  Whisk remaining dressing ingredients into the oil.
Add pasta to bowl, then remaining ingredients.  Add tuna and gently break up.  Toss well to mixand coat.  Serve salad at room temperature.

similar.name: Tuna and Cannellini Salad With Lemon

name: Aunt Nan's French Dressing
directions: Put all into blender jar, blend, and refrigerate.

name: Heavy on the Dill Salad Dressing
directions: Mix together and refrigerate for several hours allowing flavors to blend.

name: Tuna and Cannellini Salad With Lemon
directions: To make the dressing: combine the lemon juice, garlic, salt, and crushed red pepper in a small bowl.
Gradually whisk in the oil.
Season with pepper.
To make the salad: combine the beans, tuna, onion, rosemary, and lemon zest in a medium bowl.
Add 1/4 cup of the lemon garlic dressing (save remaining dressing for the arugula)
Toss well to coat.
Just before serving, place the arugula in a large bowl.
Add the reserved lemon garlic dressing and toss well.
Divide the arugula mixture among 4 plates.
Top with the tuna bean salad and garnish with cherry tomatoes.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, combine the vinegar, mustard, and garlic. Add the oil in a slow steady stream, whisking constantly. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Alternatively, you could put all ingredients in a jar with a tight lid and shake.

name: Low Cal Garlic Mustard Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients except oil.
Whisk well.
Add finely crushed garlic clove.
Stir well and microwave all ingredients for 40 to 50 seconds, depending on your microwave wattage.  Mine is 1200 watts and I set mine at 40 seconds).
May be served either warm, at room temperature, or chilled.  The cooking lets you enjoy the garlic taste without the odor.

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!

name: Homemade Ranch Dressing Recipe
directions: Combine the mayonnaise, sour cream, and buttermilk in a mixing bowl and stir until smooth. Add the bell pepper, onion, cilantro, garlic, pepper, and salt and mix well. Will keep for a week in a sealed container in the refrigerator.

name: Shelleys Best Ever Caesar Salad
directions: Put everything into a jar or container and shake the heck out of it.
Place in fridge for at least an hour to blend.
Pour over salad and let sit for at least 10 minutes.
Add bacon bits, sliced mushrooms and croutons.
top with fresh grated parmesan cheese -- .

name: Red Lobster Creamy Caesar Dressing (Copycat)
directions: Combine all ingredients. Refrigerate overnight before using.

name: Yogurt Cheese Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, mixing until well blended.
Refrigerate 'til ready to use.  Couldn't be easier!

name: Salade Nicoise With Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Mix dressing together by mixing all ingredients in jar.
Shake again, just before serving.
Mix potatoes and mayonnaise to coat thoroughly.
Toss tomatoes lightly in a small amount of vinaigrette.
In a separate bowl, toss green beans in a small amount of vinaigrette, let marinate while preparing the rest of the salad.
When ready to serve, toss lettuce lightly in a small amount of vinaigrette until leaves are barely coated.
Place around the edge and bottom on the bowl, using all the leaves.
Place tuna in the center of the bowl.
Arrange tomatoes, green beans, potatoes around the outside in an interesting pattern.
Decorate with olives, onion slices, green pepper rounds, celery and eggs.
Garnish with herbs and anchovies.
Serve immediately with additional vinaigrette dressing.

name: Spring Mix, Toasted Pecans, Cranberries and Stilton With Sherry
directions: In a saucepan, bring Ingredients 5-7 to a boil.
Add pecan halves and toss with wooden spoon for about 3-5 minutes. Be careful not to burn the pecans.
Take off fire and place on wax paper and cool. Use 1 cup for the salad and freeze the rest.
Make the dressing by whisking together the last 4 ingredients.
Assemble your salad, place pecans on top of salad and drizzle with dressing. ENJOY!

similar.name: Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad
Mixed Greens With Berry Dressing and Toasted Walnuts
Stilton and Cherry Salad With Cinnamon Dressing

name: French Dressing
directions: blend all ingredients until smooth and creamy.

name: Spicy Noodle Salad
directions: Whisk soy sauce with vinegar chili sauce, sugar, sesame oil, ginger, chili flakes and pepper.
Gradually whisk in vegetable oil.
Toss with cooked pasta, carrots and green onions.

similar.name: Cold Sesame Noodle Salad With Spicy Peanut Dressing

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk first 2 ingredients with salt and pepper in a small bowl.
Gradually whisk in oil, so the vinaigrette emulsifies.
Serve.

name: Almost-Empty Dijon Mustard Jar Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: In the almost-empty mustard jar (or other container), combine Dijon, vinegar, sugar, salt, and pepper; shake well.
Transfer to a bowl and, whisking constantly, slowly add olive oil.

name: Homemade Russian Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredient in a small bowl.

name: Sharese's Spaghetti Salad
directions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add spaghetti and cook 8-10 minutes or until al dente; drain.
In a large bowl combine spaghetti, bacon, celery, onion, bell pepper, olives, tomatoes, salad seasoning, salad dressing and parmesan cheese. Toss to coat.
Chill for 2 hours.

name: Momma Tony's Coleslaw
directions: Use only Best Foods or Kraft Mayonnaise.
In BIG bowl add cabbage,carrots,onion.

In smaller bowl mix vinegar,sugar,mayo,salt & pepper and wisk until sugar is dissolved. Add more salt and pepper if desired. Vinegar -1/4 cup at a time, if it needs more add it. Tony said 1 cup but I think this is to much.
Pour over cabbage, mix well chill and serve.This is Tonys above.
My notes.
Increase carrots to 4, use lots more fresh ground pepper,  and increase salt.
Green onion and use about 3/4 to 1 bunch 5-6 stems, cut white onion back to 1/2 an onion.
1 Green pepper finely diced/chopped in the same size as Green onions, Now I do not like green pepers as a general rule! But in this recipe it really taste GREAT!
Purple cabbage for color, about 1/8-1/4 of a head shredded, or fine chopped.
In dressing.
Yellow mustard about 1-2 tablespoons.
Horesradish 1/4 teaspoon, yes horseradish it cuts the mayo"slick" and adds a punch. You'll be supprised on this one.

name: Ginger Thyme Vinaigrette
directions: Place dressing ingredients in blender and purée for 1 minute.

name: Fred's Egg-Yolk Salad Dressing
directions: Mash the egg yolks (discard the egg whites - retain for a salad e.g. potato salad).
Mix egg yolk with sugar, mayo and mustard.
(If egg yolk is sticky rather than powdery and mixture becomes lumpy when mashed, push mixture through a sieve).
Mix with cream or milk to desired consistency and season with salt and pepper to taste.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Pulse all ingredients , except oil, in a food processor until combined.  With motor running, add oil in a slow stream and process until combined.  Season with salt if needed.  Transfer to an airtight container to store in refrigerator up to 2 weeks.

similar.name: Quick and Easy Gourmet Vinaigrette Dressing

name: Like Cafe Rio/Coata Vida Cilantro Lime Dressing
directions: Put all into a blender until slightly thickened.

name: Alternate Caesar Dressing
directions: add first 6 ingredients together in blender
blend for 2 minutes.
slowly add last two ingredients.
if after you blend recipe it starts to seperate, store in fridge and blend again once mixture is cold (to ensure it keeps thickness and smooth texture).

name: Strawberry Vinegar
directions: Pulse berries with sugar until finely chopped and juicy.
Transfer to bowl and add vinegar.
Let stand one hour.
Strain through fine mesh.

name: Bleu Cheese and Roasted Pecan Salad
directions: Place the greens on salad plates.
Add the apple slices, then sprinkle pecans and cheese over each.
Whisk the remaining ingredients together and sprinkle over salads.

similar.name: Spinach, Toffee Pecan and Goat Cheese Salad
Pecan Encrusted Goat Cheese Salad W/Maple Vinaigrette Dressing

name: Ham and Dill Salad
directions: Cook shells or ditalini according to package directions; drain well.
Cool.  (Rince with cold water to cool quickly; drain well.).
Combine soup, sour cream, vinegar, celery, onion, dill, salt and pepper; blend well.
Add cooled shells or ditalini and diced ham.
Chill.
Garnish with paprika and serve on lettuce leaves, if desired.

similar.name: Dilled Potato and Grilled Corn Salad

name: Fat Free Lemon Yogurt Vinaigrette
directions: In a mixing bowl, combine the lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, Dijon mustard, and Splenda.
Whisk in the yogurt.
Chill.

name: Lemon Lime Drizzle
directions: Combine all ingredients in a glass jar and shake well. Let stand for 15 minutes before using.
When ready drizzle over steamed veggies . Can be used as a marinade for meat dishes as well.
If you dont like capers leave them out.

name: Lighter Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well;chill.
Delicious over Romaine lettuce or spinach.

name: Orange-Oregano Dressing
directions: Combine ingredients and shake/whisk until combined.

name: Garden Court Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend till well mixed. Refrig leftovers.

name: Green Company Salad Dressing
directions: Place salt in bowl and rub hard with the fresh garlic until the garlic disappears.
Add mustard, pepper, and oil; mix well.
Add vinegar and lemon.
Pour over lettuce or place in fridge.

name: Minted Citrus Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in lidded jar in the refrigerator.

name: Lemon Fig Vinaigrette
directions: mix and pour on top of top favorite greens.

name: Avocado Ranch Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in food processor.
Pulse until blended, leaving some small chunks of Avocado.
Refrigerate until chilled.

name: Thai Slaw Dressing
directions: 1	 In a medium bowl, mix all ingredients together well.
2	 Pour over your favorite cabbage or broccoli slaw.

name: Herbed Vinaigrette
directions: In small bowl or a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine all ingredients and blend or shake until sugar is dissolved.
Add dressing to favorite salad combination.

name: Fat Free Asian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan over medium heat. Bring mixture to a rolling boil while whisking often. Cool. Put into a covered container and refrigerate.

name: Maple Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, mix all ingredients except oil with a wire whisk.
Beat in oil slowly until blended.

name: Ma's Catalina Dressing
directions: combine ingredients in jar, close and shake until sugar is dissolved.
chill.
shake again before serving each time.

name: Greek Salad Dressing - Lightened Up!
directions: Combine all ingredients expect oil in medium mixing bowl with wire whisk. Once ingredients are combined, slowly pour in olive oil while whisking to form an emulsion.

name: Bikini-Ready, Light Creamy Parmesan Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients in a small bowl. Chill before serving.

similar.name: Low-Fat Buttermilk and Parmesan Cheese Dressing

name: Honey Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk all together and drizzle over a salad.  Store refrigerated.

name: Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl combine first six ingredients.
Gradually whisk in olive oil until well blended.
Stir in shallots.
Drizzle over salad and toss to coat and serve immediately if serving salad.
I like to let the flavours marry overnight for a stronger flavour.

name: White Balsamic and Lemon Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients into a jar or tupperware container with a lid. Shake vigarously. Store in the refrigerator until ready to toss.

name: Catalina French Dressing
directions: Mix and store in fridge!

name: Blender Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and process until smooth.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Dijon Dressing
directions: In small bowl, whisk together all dressing ingredients until blended.

name: Maggiano's Little Italy Chop Salad
directions: Combine mustard, sugar, water, garlic, red and white vinegar and salt in food processor. Mix until smooth. Slowly drizzle in the oils, continue to blend. Add remaining ingredients and blend until well mixed. Recipe can be doubled with remaining stored in sealed container in refrigerator for up to a week.
Place lettuce in large mixing bowl Add all the ingredients except garnish and mix well. Add dressing and mix well.
Serve, topping each portion with garnish.

similar.name: Green Salad With Blue Cheese Dressing

name: Fresh Herb Dressing
directions: Process all ingredients in the processor.

name: Avocado-Orange Salsa
directions: Gently combine all ingredients in medium bowl.
Chill before serving, if desired.
Serve with grilled meat or on salad.

similar.name: Mixed Greens With Orange Juice Vinaigrette

name: Honey-Dijon Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: For the dressing; in a bowl whisk together garlic, salt, Dijon, honey and balsamic vinegar; add in olive oil in a slow steady stream until emulsified.
Adjust honey amount adding in more if desired for a sweeter taste, then season with more salt if needed, and black pepper.
Chill for a minimum of 2 hours before using.

name: Jack's Favorite Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Refrigerate and let set---overnight is best to allow flavors to develop.
Will keep for days in refrigerator.

similar.name: Jan's Beg for the Recipe Salad Dressing

name: Relish Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a medium size bowl, serve with chips or crackers or vegetables. Can also be used with french fries or poultry.

name: Creamy Caesar Dressing
directions: In a medium bowl, combine all ingrediants and stir well.
Add salt and pepper to taste (easy on the salt it may not need it). Cover and refrigerate for atleast 30 minutes. Serve chilled.

name: Strawberry Vinaigrette
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together preserves vinegar, mustard and pepper, until thoroughly emulsified.
gradually whisk in olive oil, then water.

name: Low-Cal Mustard Vinaigrette
directions: Shake all ingredients together in a covered jar and leave at room temperature a couple hours before using.

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!
Almost-Empty Dijon Mustard Jar Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Martha's Roasted Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: Make the vinaigrette: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Drizzle garlic with 1 teaspoon oil. Wrap in parchment, then in foil. Bake until soft, about 30 minutes. Squeeze garlic from skins. Mash until smooth.
Whisk together the remaining ingredients with the roasted garlic and remaining oil.

name: Chive Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together sour cream, buttermilk, chives, garlic, Worshestershire and vinegar.
Season to taste with salt, pepper and hot sauce.
Serve with a green salad or boiled and chilled vegetables.

name: Lime and Sesame Dressing
directions: In a bowl, combine lime juice, brown sugar, sesame oil and Fish Sauce.
Mix together well.
Serve with vegetable salad or cold noodle salad.

name: Peanut Dressing
directions: Whisk togetehr vinegar, peanut butter soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, canola oil and red pepper.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Pour over salad or cold cooked whole wheat noodles.
Add veggies of your choice.
I like edamame, snow peas, shredded carrots, broccoli slaw.

name: Chutney Garlic Dressing
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a food processor and blend.

name: Broccoli Salad With Coleslaw Dressing
directions: For the Dressing:
In a measuring cup, mix the lime juice and soy milk together and let it sit for about 10 minutes.
Transfer to a bigger container, and add the mayo, agave, mustard and salt and pepper.
Mix well.
For the Salad:
Cook your turkey bacon until just crispy.  Put it into the fridge for later.
Toss the broccoli, onion, dried cranberries, sunflower seeds and red pepper in a large bowl.
Mix in the 1/2 cup soy milk (if using) with the salad dressing and add to the salad until salad is slightly moist.
Refrigerate for about 1 hour to let the flavours soak into the veggies.
Sprinkle the bacon on top before serving.
Bon Appetit!

similar.name: Fruit Salad With Honey and Spice Dressing

name: Pendy's French Dressing
directions: Throw everything into a blender and blend until well combined.
Chill and enjoy!

name: Orange Poppy Seed Salad
directions: In a large bowl, mix all salad ingredients.
In  a small bowl, mix all salad dressing ingredients until well blended.
Pour over salad, toss to coat.
Serve immediately.

similar.name: Strawberry Spinach Salad W/Poppy Seed Dressing

name: Orange Vinaigrette
directions: Combine in a jar and shake.

name: Honey Mustard
directions: mix all ingredients together and wisk.
serve chilled.
its best to let settle in the fridge, covered, for a good 2 or 3 hours before serving.
it works great on salads, but I like it best as a dip for raw veggies.

name: Fruit Salad With Honey and Spice Dressing
directions: Peel papaya; cut in half lengthwise. Remove seeds; coarsely chop papaya. Measure 1 1/4 cups. In a food processor or blender combine the chopped papaya, honey, lime peel, lime juice, canola oil, chili powder, and salt. Cover; process or blend until smooth. Transfer to a covered container; chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes or up to 3 days.

To serve, in a very large bowl place spinach, strawberries, pineapple, peaches, grapes, blueberries, and pecans. Toss to combine. Drizzle with some of the dressing (about 1/2 cup). Toss to coat evenly. Pass remaining dressing.
Store any remaining dressing, covered, in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

similar.name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing

name: Chickpea and Couscous Salad (Weight Watcher Friendly)
directions: Cook couscous according to directions.
Whisk together all dressing ingredients in a small bowl and chill until ready to use.
In a large bowl, toss together cooked couscous and remaining ingredients.
Add salad dressing and mix well.
Cover and chill for at least 2 hours.
Toss before serving.

similar.name: Tomato, Artichoke and Feta Couscous Salad

name: Vini's French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender and blend well.

name: Sweet Red Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a shakable container.
Shake vigorously.
Chill, shaking occasionally.
"Cooking" time is Chill time.

name: My Lower-Fat Greek Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: Simple Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Beat the vinegar in a bowl with the optional sugar, garlic, salt and pepper until sugar and salt dissolves. Then beat in the oil by droplets, whisking constantly or place all the ingredients in a screw-top jar and shake to combine. Taste and adjust the seasonings.
If not using dressing right away, cover and refrigerate, whisking or shaking again before use.
Can be used as a dressing and a marinade.
*If using a good quality balsamic vinegar you should not need the sugar, but if using a lesser quality you might want the sugar to round out the dressing.

name: Tangy Dijon Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar or cruet.
Shake well and chill.

similar.name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients and stir or shake vigorously.

name: Polynesian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar & shake well to combine.

name: Tangy Mexican Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, keep in airtight container in the fridge.

name: Creamy Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and whisk until well combined. The flavor improves if you refrigerate for a while before using.

name: Turkey Salad
directions: Prepare pasta according to package directions, rinse, set aside.
Whisk together garlic, sour cream, broth, oil, vinegar, pepper, and basil in a small bowl. Set aside.
In a large bowl, combine pasta, lettuce, kidney beans, cheese, turkey, and onion.
Chill in refrigerator until serving time. Toss salad with dressing just before serving.

name: 1905 Salad Dressing Recipe
directions: Whisk together the garlic, oregano and Worcestershire sauce.
Gradually whisk in the olive oil, continually beating to form an emulsion. Stir in the vinegar and lemon juice
Season with salt and pepper.

name: Sweet Basil Dressing
directions: Blend all of the ingredients until smooth.  May be kept in an airtight container for up to two weeks in the fridge.

similar.name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing

name: Miso-Walnut Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients until smooth.

name: Rosemary Vinaigrette
directions: Process balsamic vinegar and next 7 ingredients in a blender or food processor 15 to 20 seconds, stopping to scrape down sides.
With blender or processor running, gradually add olive oil in a slow, steady stream; process until smooth.

name: Avocado Ranch Dip
directions: Mix together ranch dressing and sour cream.
Add avocado, lime juice and zest, cilantro and black pepper.
Chill 30 minutes,.
