Serve as a dip or thin with milk to use as a dressing.

name: French Dressing
directions: Mix together mustard, honey, wine vinegar & seasoning.Whisk in olive oil.

name: Sammy's Charcoal Grill Salad Dressing
directions: Chop celery, bell pepper and cheese.
Put in a blender with garlic & buttermilk and blend for 12-15 seconds.
Add eggs and blend for 3 more seconds.
Add Miracle Whip, catsup & sugar and blend until well mixed.
Refrigerate.
Serve over wedge of iceberg lettuce.

similar.name: Garlic and Blue Cheese Salad Dressing

name: Specialty of the House Dressing
directions: Mix well and chill.
Makes 10 (2Tbsp) servings.

name: Maple Cider Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together first 5 ingredients.
Gradually whisk in oil until completely blended. Cover and refrigerate up to 3 days.

name: Tangy Oil and Vinegar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all the ingredients in a small bowl.  Pour over salad and enjoy!

name: Lemon Maple Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients, stirring well with a whisk.
Cover and chill until ready to serve.

name: Spinach Salad Dressing
directions: Cook bacon, reserve 2 tsp of fab in skillet. Add shallot. Cook about 2 minutes. Whisk in vinegar and mustard. Season with salt and pepper. Pour over spinach salad.

name: Special Salad Dressing  (Frances Virginia Tea Room)
directions: Mix well and chill until ready to use.
Cook time is chill time.

name: Cucina! Cucina! Chopped Salad (Copycat)
directions: To prepare vinaigrette: Combine egg yolk and water in a small bowl; whisk well. Cover the bowl with a small plate and microwave on HIGH for 10 seconds or until the mixture starts to expand. Continue to cook 5 seconds longer. Whisk with a clean whisk. Cover, then cook on high for 5 seconds. Remove from the microwave, whisk with a clean whisk, cover and let sit 1 minute. (This process heats the egg enough to kill any harmful bacteria.).

In a food processor, combine egg mixture, Dijon, garlic, salt, pepper, dry mustard, oregano, sugar, vinegar and lemon juice; process to blend. Blend in grated parmesan if you choose to add it. Slowly add olive oil, processing until emulsified. Set aside.

To prepare salad: Combine chickpeas, iceberg lettuce, basil, mozzarella, chicken, salami, half of the tomatoes, half of the provolone and half of the green onions. Toss with dressing. (You may not need all of the dressing. Refrigerate any that remains.).
Divide evenly among 8 lettuce-lined plates. Garnish with remaining tomatoes, provolone and green onions.

similar.name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing

name: Orange Sesame Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl with a wire whisk.

name: Scallops W/ Spinach Prosciutto Salad-Warm Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Pick stems off the spinach leves.  Lay scallops on a kitchen towel and pat dry.  Cut prosciutto in thin strips.  Cut shallot into thin rings.  Mince garlic.  Put spinach and prosciutto in a large bowl.

Heat 2 T olive oil in a large pan over medium high heat, place scallops in hot pan but do not crowd pan.  (do in two batches if necesssary)  Sear and allow to caramelize for about 2 minutes.  Flip scallops and sear on the other side.

Remove from pan and set aside, keep warm.  Keep pan on the heat and add another 1 tsp of olive oil if necessary,  Add garlic, cook for 5 minutes, deglaze with chicken stock, add balsamic vinegar, honey and shallot.  Allow to reduce by half.

Pour hot vinaigrette over spinach and toss to coat all the spinach leaves.  This will wilt the leaves slightly.  Add a dash of olive oil and season with salt and pepper.  Put three scallops in each plate.  Place half the spinach salad on each plate.
Sprinkle the top of each salat with tomato and parmesan cheese.
Serve.

similar.name: Seafood Salad With Lemon Dill Vinaigrette
Lobster and Caviar Salad With Green Peppercorn Dressing
Spinach Salad With Gorgonzola & Pepper Jelly Vinaigrette

name: Apple Spinach Salad With Warm Maple Dressing

name: Roasted Garlic Vinaigrette
directions: Preheat the oven to 350°F
Slice off the top of the garlic bulb, just enough to barely expose the garlic inside each clove.
Place the bulb in an ovenproof dish and drizzle with olive oil.
Place the dish, uncovered, in the oven and cook the garlic until it is golden brown and soft, about 15 to 20 minutes.
Let cool.

When the roasted garlic is cool enough to handle, squeeze the pulp from 6 of the cloves and mash with a mortar and pestle, or with a fork. (Store the remaining cloves in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.)
Put the garlic in a food processor and add the mustard, vinegar, salt and pepper and puree.
Slowly add the olive oil and stock through the feed tube, and puree until the vinaigrette is creamy.
If it is too thick add a teaspoon of stock until the consistency is correct.  Add the shallots and tarragon.
Adjust the salt and pepper to taste.

name: Seafood Salad With Lemon Dill Vinaigrette

Combine the lemon zest and juice, garlic, oil, sugar, and dill in a bowl.
Gently toss in the seafood, bell pepper, tomatoes, red onion, salt and pepper.  Cover and marinate in the fridge for 30 minutes.
Divide and arrange salad greens on four chilled plates.  Arrange seafood mixture on top.
Enjoy!

name: Tee Pee Restaurant Salad Dressing
directions: To make mayonnaise: Stir together dry mustard, nutmeg, ginger,paprika and white pepper and set aside.
Break eggs into mixing bowl and beat well. With mixer on high speed slowly add 2 gallons salad oil.
Bring 3 quarts of water to boil. Mix each pound of corn starch with 1/2 qt of cold water. Add cornstarch mixture to boiling water to the mayonnaise mixture and stir well.
Add dry mixture and vinegar and mix.
To make Garlic oil:
Peel garlic and add to garlic to a gallon of salad oil and let soak overnight.
To make dressing:

Stir paprika, season salt, sugar and salt.  Stir this into 1 cup of the mayonnaise mixture along with the anchovy paste.  Add wine vinegar, water and 10 oz of garlic oil, stir thoroughly.  Save remaining oil for other recipes.

For the Salad:  Toss zucchini, eggplant, and bell peppers with 3 tablespoons oil in bowl and season with salt and pepper.  Brush onion and baguette with remaining 5 tablespoons oil and season with salt and pepper.  Grill vegetables and bread until charred on both sides, just 1 or 2 minutes for bread, and up to 12 minutes for onions.
Chop vegetables roughly and toss with pine nuts and 2 tablespoons dressing in bowl.  Toss salad greens with 2 tablespoons dressing in second bowl.
Place 3 bread slices on each large plate.  Top bread with salad, then vegetable.  Drizzle with remaining dressing; garnish with Parmesan.  Serve.

Combine shallot, mustard, vinegar, and 1/2 teaspoons salt in a bowl.  Pour in remaining 2/3 cup oil in a slow steady stream until emulsified.  Season with salt and pepper.

Arrange lettuce on a large platter, and pile tabbouleh on top.  Toss tomatoes with 1/4 cup of the dressing, and arrange on top of the tabbouleh.  Drizzle 1/4 cup dressing over tomatoes, and season with pepper.  Serve with remaining dressing on the side.

dressing.

name: Super Spinach Salad
directions: Mix greens; divide among salad plates.

Top each serving with bacon bits, red onion, grated cheese and mushrooms, if desired.  (You can combine it all in a large bowl if necessary.   But the 'goodies' get distributed a lot better if you treat each serving separately.).
Combine all dressing ingredients in a jar with a tight-fitting lid.  Shake well to combine.
Taste and adjust amount of sugar if needed.
Pour a small amount of dressing over each individual salad, serving additional dressing on the side.
Stir dressing well before each use, as the sugar tends to sink to the bottom.

name: Simple Balsamic Vinaigrette

Taste and adjust the seasonings.

name: Roasted Pecan Salad
directions: I have a small rocket blender so I just throw all the ingredients for the dressing in here and let it mince my shallots along with it.  Stick in to the frig until ready to serve.
The Pecans are the most time consuming but I like to double this and my family will just snack on them too.
Put all the ingredients into a pot except the pecans.
Once everything is melted and mixed together pour in to a big bowl with the pecans. Place the coated place on a piece of wax paper on a cookie sheet.  Roast pecans at 250 for 1 hours
Let them cool, but make sure you don't let them completly cool before breaking up.  They will be sticky untill completly cool and will clump together.
Just throw on top of your mixed greens and blue/fetta cheese.  Add your dressing and enjoy.
This is a great salad for parties.  I never know what veggies people like and the pecans are always a hit.  I will throw them on any salad to add a spicey sweet and crunch.
I am also going to make the pecans as gifts for my neighbors b/c the liked them so much.

name: Chef's Salad
directions: FOR THE CROUTONS:
Adjust oven to middle position and heat oven to 350°F  Combine garlic, salt and oil in a small bowl; let stand 20 minutes, then pour through fine mesh strainer into medium bowl.

Add bread cubes and toss to coat.  Spread bread cubes in even layer on rimmed baking sheet and bake, stirring occasionally, until golden, about 15 minutes.  Cool on baking sheet to room temperature.  (Can be covered and stored at room temperature for up to 24 hours).
FOR THE VINAIGRETTE:
Whisk ingredients together in medium bowl until combined.  Add cucumber and toss; let stand for 20 minutes.
FOR THE SALAD:

Toss lettuce, arugula, and radishes in large, wide serving bowl.  Add cucumbers and all but 1 remaining Tablespoon dressing and toss to combine.  Season to taste with salt and pepper.  Toss tomatoes in remaining dressing in bowl; arrange tomatoes around perimeter of greens.  Arrange egg wedges in ring inside tomatoes and drizzle with any dressing in bowl.  Arrange ham, turkey, and cheese over center of greens; sprinkle with croutons and serve imnmediately.

name: Poppy & Sesame Seed Vinaigrette

Add the wet ingredients and shake.

directions: Add  water, vinegar, garlic, oregano, sugar, pepper, and salt ; whisk to combine.
Whisking constantly, drizzle in oil in slow steady stream until combined.

name: Ken Bauder's Mint Salad Dressing
directions: Place all of the ingredients in your blender and blend until well-mixed.

name: Mediterranean Dressing
directions: Press garlic and let it sit for 5 minutes to get the goodness out of it.
Put the lemon juice in a bowl and slowly add the olive oil, whisking all the time; the slower you add it and the faster you whisk it, the creamier and thicker it will get.
Add the garlic, salt, and pepper and whisk again.
Put in a glass jar and refrigerater.
You will have to bring it to room temperature before adding to a salad because olive oil gets thick when cold.

directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, stirring with a wire whisk until smooth.  Cover & chill.

Whisk in remaining ingredients.
I like it to chill and meld several hours before serving, but that is just me.
NOTES: you may want to add half the amount of salt and sugar the first time you make it and adjust it to your tastes.

name: Fresh Basil Vinaigrette
directions: Heat 1/4 cup oil with 1 cup basil in medium saucepan over medium heat until basil turns bright green and small bubbles appear, 2-3 minutes.  Turn off heat and steep 5 minutes.

Process shallot, garlic, vinegar, water, salt, pepper, and mustard in blender until garlic and shallot are finely chopped, about 15 seconds.  With blender running, slowly add remaining oil and steeped basil oil and continue to process until dressing is smooth and emulsified, about 15 seconds.  Pack remaining basil into blender and process until dressing is smooth, about 15 seconds. (Dressing can be refrigerated in airtight container for up to 3 days).

name: Creamy Caesar Dressing
directions: Place all dressing ingrediants except oil and mayonnaise in food processor; blend until smooth.
Slowly add oil, and continue blending to keep mixture smooth.
Put mayonnaise in large bowl and add mixture; stir until well blended.
Cover and chill 30-60 minutes to thicken.

name: Pizza Salad
directions: Heat oven to 450.
For the dressing:  Mix tomato, oil, and garlic in a small skillet or saucepan.
Cook over medium-high heat for 5 minutes, breaking apart the tomatoes with a spoon.
Transfer to a blender and add vinegar, salt, pepper, sugar, and basil.
Pulse to blend and set dressing aside.
Place prepared pizza shell on rack in 450 degree oven.
Bake for 6 to 8 minutes or until shell is crisp and lightly browned.
For the salad, combine the tomatoes, mozzarella, basil, lettuce, and pepperoni in a large bowl.
Remove pizza shell from the oven and slice into bite-sized pieces.
Add pizza pieces to the bowl and toss with dressing.

similar.name: California Pizza Kitchen White Balsamic Provencal Salad

name: Thousand Island Dressing (Cooked)
directions: Mix together in saucepan eggs, sugar, flour, dry mustard, water, vinegar,salt and butter. Cook until thick. Chop hard boiled eggs, green pepper, pimientos and onion. Add to cooked portion. Stir in chili sauce and whipping cream.

name: Low-Fat Asian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and serve.

name: Russian Dressing
directions: Mince onion very fine, or use food processor.
Add tomato soup, sugar, vinegar, oil, salt and pepper and mix.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Fruit Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a jar and shake vigorously. Use with fresh fruits.

name: French Dressing
directions: Combine and shake well. Dressing keeps well.

name: Blue Cheese and Chive Dressing
directions: Blend buttermilk, mayonnaise, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, and salt in a food processor until smooth.
Add parsley and pulse until chopped. Add blue cheese and pulse until cheese is incorporated but dressing is still slightly chunky.
Transfer to a bowl and stir in chives and pepper.
Dressing keeps, covered and chilled, 1 week.

name: Sweet 'n' Sassy Salad Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine all ingredients.  Cover and shake well, then refrigerate (will keep for 1 week).

name: Lime & Poppy Seed Salad Dressing
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine all ingredients.  Cover and shake well, then refrigerate (will keep for 1 week).

name: Creole French Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl which has been rubbed with a cut garlic clove and mix well.
Serve with any green or meat salad.

name: Wheat Berry Salad With Fruit & Feta
directions: Starting with about 7 Cups of water, boil wheat for 1 hour, keeping the water level about an inch above the wheat.  (You will need to add water periodically.).
Roast Almonds by baking on a cookie sheet or pan at 400* for 10 minutes, stirring/flipping after 5 minutes.  Set aside to cool, then chop to desired size.
Peel and cut apples, finely grate orange zest, juice orange (chunks in the juice are okay.).
When wheat is done boiling (it should have a tender, rubbery consistency), drain and rinse with cold water.  Drain well.
My feta was herb-soaked in a garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, rosemary, dill mix.  It adds a lot to the salad.
Mix everything together.  You can serve it right away, but it gets even better the next day.

similar.name: Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing

name: Pear Salad With Lemon Dill Dressing
directions: Salad:.
Place spinach in a large salad bowl. Top with chopped pears. Sprinkle shredded cheese on top. Spread toasted pecans on the top of the salad last.
Dressing:.
Mix mayo and lemon juice well. Add dill and Splenda mixing well. Lastly slowly incorporate the heavy cream. Refridgerate at least 4 hours. Serve dressing with salad.

similar.name: Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing
Pear & Blue Cheese Walnut Salad W/ Pear Vinaigrette
Spinach and Pear Salad With Pomegranate Dressing

name: Super Easy Taco Salad Dressing
directions: In a mixing bowl put the ranch dressing.
Add 1/4 cup salsa and stir.  Add 1 Tble. of salsa at a time until you have the desired kick to your dressing.  Add as much or as little as you wish.
Stir in the minced cilantro.  This is optional but I love it myself.
Check for salt and pepper.
Serve with taco salad.  I especially like this dressing on a chicken taco salad.

similar.name: Taco Salad Dressing - Fast and Easy

name: Zesty Citrus Salad Dressing
directions: Mixt together equal parts lemon, orange and lime juice.
Add fresh minced garlic, chopped fresh dill and celery salt.
Chill to blend flavors.
Serve over tossed salad.

name: Cheater's Caesar Dressing
directions: Wisk mayonnaise,lemon juice,garlic,anchovy paste until they are incorperated well.
Add parmesean and pepper
Let sit for at least an hour in the refridgerator.

name: Esperanza Il Forno Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Let stand in fridge for at least 24 hours before intended use.

name: French Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients. Allow to stand for 24 hours before serving; serve over salad greens.

name: Celery Seed Dressing
directions: Using electric mixer or blender, combine first 4 ingredients; add onion. Gradually add oil and vinegar, beating constantly. Garnish with thyme. Add to fruit salad just before serving. Note: Constant beating to blend ingredients is the secret of success!

name: Dijon Mustard Dressing
directions: Combine mustard, vinegar and lemon juice in a bowl large enough for stirring with whisk. Slowly drizzle the olive oil into the bowl whisking the contents so that the olive oil is thoroughly incorporated. Add the rest of the ingredients.

name: Delicious Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all together and serve over salad greens. Season with salt, pepper and garlic salt to your taste.

name: Herbed Vinaigrette
directions: In a bowl, combine mustard, salt, pepper, garlic, vinegar, oregano, and cumin.  Whisk to combine.  Gradually add olive oil, continuing to whisk briskly.
Serve over chilled salad greens.

name: Caterer's Basic French Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk ingredients together in a bowl. Makes 1 3/4 cups dressing.

name: Caper Vinaigrette
directions: Put capers with liquid, minced roasted red pepper, and minced parsley in food processor with steel blade and pulse 20-30 seconds, until ingredients are coarsely chopped, then place ingredients in small plastic bowl.
Stir in Dijon and red wine vinegar, then whisk in olive oil a little at a time, until vinaigrette is emulsified.
(Make dressing a day ahead if desired. Bring to room temperature before using.).

name: Healthier Creamy Parmesan Salad Dressing
directions: Blend in food processor until thick but fairly smooth.

name: Quick and Easy Gourmet Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Blend in food processor until smooth.
Whisk ingredients together in bowl.

name: Cranberry Salad Dressing
directions: Mix first 5 ingredients together. Plate salad greens, add remaining ingredients and pour dressing over.

name: Lemon Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender.  Blend until combined.

name: Homemade Ranch Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl.
Mix well.
This dressing will keep about a week in the refrigerator.

name: Garlic and Herb Vinaigrette
directions: Combine everything in a medium bowl.
Whisk until well blended.
Shake well before serving.
This keeps indefinitely in the refrigerator.

name: Basil Vinaigrette (W/ Oregano & Tarragon Variations)
directions: In a blender, combine the oil, vinegar, basil, mustard and garlic.  Process for 1 minute.  Store in a tightly covered glass jar in the refrigerator.
VARIATIONS:  Oregano Vinaigrette - Substitute 1-1/2 - 2 teaspoons dried oregano for the basil; Tarragon Vinaigrette - Substitute 3/4 to 1 teaspoon dried tarragon for the basil.

name: Herbed Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl. Adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.

name: Couscous Salad With Avocado & Prawns
directions: Place the couscous in a heatproof bowl. Place the stock and water in a saucepan over medium heat. Bring to the boil. Stir in the oil. Pour the stock mixture over the couscous. Stir to combine. Set aside, covered, for 3 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed. Use a fork to separate grains. Set aside for 5 minutes to cool.
Meanwhile, to make the dressing, whisk together the lemon rind, lemon juice and oil in a small bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
Add the cucumber, carrot, avocado, prawns and mint to the couscous, and toss to combine.
Add the dressing and toss to combine. Divide among serving bowls and serve.

similar.name: Chickpea and Couscous Salad (Weight Watcher Friendly)
Tomato, Artichoke and Feta Couscous Salad

name: Green Mayonnaise Dressing (Green Goddess)
directions: Put everything into a blender and blend until smooth.
Stores in refridgerator for up to 3 weeks.

name: Light Balsamic Vinaigrette Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl or food processor combine garlic and mustard until well mixed.
Whisk in balsamic vinegar, hot water and sugar.
Slowly add in olive oil mixing until emulsified.
Add salt and pepper to taste.  Use immediately or cover and refrigerate.
Feel free to substitute different vinegars and seasoning to your own taste.

name: Thousand Island Dressing Ala Slim
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Serve immediately.

name: French Dressing
directions: Add all ingredients in a mason jar except olive oil. Shake these ingredients, adding olive oil slowly, and shaking often.
Let sit in fridge for an hour or so, and serve on any salad greens.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in a blender or with a hand blender.

name: New Orleans Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix together.

name: Jg House Vinaigrette
directions: Place the bouillon cube in a bowl with 2 tbsp water.  Microwave on high for 30 seconds to dissolve cube.
Place sugar in a bowl and add pepper, garlic powder, and vinegar. Stir and let set 5 minutes.
Whisk in the dissolved bouillon and gradually add oil in a slow steady stream while whisking vigorously.
Whisk again before serving.

name: Aunt Lou's Balsamic Dressing
directions: Pour all ingredients into container and shake to combine. No need to refrigerate.

name: Roasted Fingerlings, Tomato Salad With Green Beans and Arugula
directions: MAKE THE SALAD:

Preheat oven to 375°F  Purée garlic and oil in a blender until smooth.  Toss with potatoes, tomatoes, and thyme on a rimmed baking sheet.  Season with salt and pepper.  Bake until potatoes are golden and tender, about 30 minutes.  Let cool slightly.
Prepare an ice-water bath.  Cook green beans in a large pot of boiling, salted water until bright green and just tender.  Transfer to  ice-water bath.  When cool, drain beans in a colander.
MAKE THE DRESSING:
Combine vinegar, mustard, thyme and shallot in a small bowl.  Add oil in a slow steady stream, whisking until emulsified.
Toss potato mixture, beans, and arugula with dressing. Season with salt and pepper.  Serve immediately or at room temperature.

similar.name: Dilled Potato and Grilled Corn Salad

name: Easy, Creamy, Low-Fat Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Place a colander in a 2-quart glass bowl. Line colander with 4 layers of cheesecloth, allowing cheesecloth to extend over sides. Spoon yogurt into colander. Cover loosely with plastic wrap; refrigerate 12 hours.
Spoon drained yogurt into bowl; discard liquid. Add 1 1/2 T water, mustard, vinegar and garlic. Stir well.
Drizzle dressing over salad and toss to coat.

similar.name: Caesar Salad from Salt Fat Acid Heat

name: Mayonnaise
directions: Beat egg yolks, together with the vinegar, seasoning and mustard for about 30-40 seconds. While beating add the olive oil in a constant stream until all ingredients are combined.
Serve chilled.

name: Leafy Green Salad With Honey-Dijon Vinaigrette for Two
directions: Combine vinegar, mustard, honey, poppy seed, garlic, salt and pepper in bowl with a fork.  Add oil, then whisk or mix with fork until smooth, about 30 seconds.  The dressing will separate after 5 - 10 minutes, so use immediately or mix again just before tossing with greens.
Place greens in large salad bowl.  Drizzle with dressing and toss until greens are evenly coated.  Serve immediately.

similar.name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing
Fresh Apple and Parsley Dressing With Greens

name: French Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients together in a processor and serve.

name: Pink Cloud Salad Dressing
directions: Blend the sour cream and raspberry jam together.

name: Delicious Fat Free Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix cottage cheese, ranch dressing mix, and water in a blender.  Puree until smooth.

similar.name: Fat-Free Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing (Mix  It Yourself )

name: Vinegar & Oil Sunset Dressing
directions: Cover seasoning with water and let sit for few minutes. Add oil and vinegar and whisk to combine. add mustard and honey and whisk to blend.

name: Good and Healthy Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar (I use a mason jar) and shake well. store in refrigerator.  Keeps indefinitely.

name: Pear, Beet and Red Onion Salad
directions: Vinaigrette:  Whisk together pear juice, vinegar, mustard, salt and pepper.  Whisking constantly, gradually whisk in oil until dressing is thickened and smooth.
Salad:  In large bowl, gently toss together spinach, onions, and pear.  Drizzle dressing over, toss gently until well coated.  Gently stir in beets.  Sprinkle with nuts.

similar.name: Pear & Blue Cheese Walnut Salad W/ Pear Vinaigrette
Spinach and Pear Salad With Pomegranate Dressing

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Whisk together first 7 ingredients until blended. Gradually whisk in olive oil, blending well.

name: Victoria's Oil & Vinegar Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: Gloria Pitzer's Olive Garden-Style Salad Dressing
directions: Blend first 5 ingredients (not oil) in blender on high speed 1/2 minute or until smooth. Pour this mixture into the top of a double boiler and add 1/4 cup oil. Stir gently with a whisk over gently boiling water until it begins to thicken and egg is completely cooked. Chill several hours or overnight before using. If the dressing is too thick, add more Italian dressing as needed.

Mix together equal amounts of fresh spinach, iceberg and romaine lettuce. Allow 2 cups for each salad. Moisten leaves in dressing, do not saturate; let stand 5 minutes. Add onion rings, radishes, etc.

name: Feta Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together in either a blender or food processor. This make one quart of dressing.
I prefer to use fresh basil and oregano, *remember it is necessary to double the amount when using fresh herbs.
The number of servings is merely an estimate, it depends on how much dressing you like on your salad.

similar.name: Feta and Olive Oil Salad Dressing

name: Abby's Grilled Prawn Mango Avocado Salad
directions: Dressing:  Place all the ingredients for the dressing in a jar with a lid.  Shake the ingredients until combined.  Set aside.
Salad:  Wash and clean salad greens.  Shread into bit size pieces and place on 2 bowls or decorative plates.  Arrange rest of the ingredients on the plate.
Preheat grill to medium high.

Prawns:  Place 6 prawns on the wooden bamboo stick.   Remove about 2 T of the salad dressing to a small bowl, and brush on both sides of the prawns.  Place on the grill and grill for 3-1/2 - 4 minutes.  Turn the prawns over and brush with any remaining dressing.  Grill until cooked, about 3-1/2 - 4 minutes more.  Remove from grill when just slightly pink.  Cut into one prawn to check if cooked.  You have overcooked when the prawns when it becomes bright pink.
Remove the prawns from the bamboo and let sit for 1 minute.
Just before serving, sprinkle the remaining dressing on the salad and add the prawns on top.
Serve.

similar.name: Warm Seafood Salad With Roasted Red Pepper Dressing

name: Chili Lime Marinade
directions: mx together.

name: Creamy Buttermilk Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in blender or food processor, cover and process until smooth.  Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.

name: Pineapple-Jalapeno Coleslaw
directions: Combine first 7 ingredients in a small bowl, stir with a whisk.
Combine cabbage and remaining ingredients on a large bowl.  Pour dressing over cabbage mixtuer; toss gently to coat.  Serve immediately.

name: Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Mix all in a bowl and stir until blended.

name: Sweet Onion Vinaigrette
directions: In food processor puree everything but the onion. Pour into a bowl and let stand at room temperature for 1 hour so that flavors blend.
In mean time grill onion slices.
Add onion slices to vinaigrette and serve at room temperature over grilled veggies.

name: **copycat** Costa Vida Ranch Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in the blender and blend well. If you like it a little more mild, use less cayenne pepper and less of the jalepeno seeds. I usually only use 5-6 seeds & a tiny piece of the core. My mom uses the whole pepper seeds and all.
"Cooking" time does NOT include chilling time - chill in fridge for at least 2 hours to let the flavors meld.

similar.name: Cafe Rio Copycat Cilantro Ranch Dressing

directions: In a bowl, combine ingredients except for olive oil. Slowly whisk inches.
olive oil until thickened. Great with field greens. Makes about 2/3.
cup dressing.

name: Marion's Sweet Basil Dressing
directions: Combine the ingredients in a small jar with a lid and shake until well combined.

similar.name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing

name: Sesame Ginger Dressing
directions: Whisk together the garlic, ginger, hot pepper sauce, mustard, honey, lemon juice, vinegar, soy sauce, orange juice, sesame oil and olive oil.
Stir in cilantro and chives.  Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary.

name: Romaine Salad With Miso Dressing
directions: Purée vinegar, miso, garlic, sugar and ginger in blender. With machine running, gradually pour in 1/2 cup olive oil; blend until mixture is creamy. Season dressing to taste with salt and pepper.
Toss with enough dressing to coat the lettuce leaves.  Place on plates.  Add chopped tomatoes and mushrooms.

name: Romaine Salad With Balsamic Dressing
directions: Whisk first 4 ingredients in medium bowl to blend. Gradually whisk in oil.
Season dressing to taste with salt and pepper. (Can be prepared 8 hours ahead. Cover and chill.
Let stand at room temperature 15 minutes; rewhisk before using.).

similar.name: Fresh Basil and Balsamic Low Fat Salad Dressing

name: Basic Vinaigrette
directions: Assemble ingredients in any kind of jar.
Shake before use.

name: Tarragon Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk the lemon juice, shallot, tarragon, mustard and garlic together in a small bowl.  Whisk in the olive oil and season with salt and pepper to taste.

similar.name: Simple Market Salad With Tarragon Vinaigrette

name: Gay's Dressing
directions: Beat eggs in a bowl. Mix oil & vinegar in a jar until blended well. Add all other ingredients & mix well. Refrigerate.

name: Spinach, Mushroom and Red Onion Salad
directions: For Dressing:.
Whisk all ingredients in small bowl.
(Can be prepared 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate.).
For Salad:.
Combine all ingredients.
Whisk dressing to blend, add to salad and toss to coat; serve immediately.

name: Pazzo's Best Caesar Salad
directions: Place garlic, mustard, egg beaters, parmesan, lemon juice and anchovy into beaker.
Place the hand blender into the beaker and blend.
Slowly add the  olive oil until smooth.  Add the pepper to taste.
In a salad bowl or on a plate place the lettuce.  Add dressing and toss.
Top with parmesan cheese and croutons and chicken or shrimp if using.

name: Smoked Almond Salad With Honey and Oil Vinaigrette
directions: Place all salad ingredients in a bowl.  Put dressing ingredients into non-matalic bowl and whisk well (ingredients may be put into a mason jar and shaken wel).  Pour dressing over salad and gently toss.  Serve.

similar.name: Salad With Walnut Vinaigrette and Warm Chevre

name: Maple Grilled Corn, Blueberry and Apple Salad
directions: Assemble all dressing ingredients in a non-metal bowl, whisk until combined (ingredients may also be put into a mason jar and shaken well).  Chill.

Rub ears of corn with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.  Place on grill, turing until grill marks are evenly achieved.  Remove.  When corn is cooled, cut kernels away from ear and place in a large bowl,.
Core and dice apples, leaving the peel on, add to the corn.  Add green onions, and blueberries. Add lemon juice and toss.  Pour dressing over salad and serve.

similar.name: Dilled Potato and Grilled Corn Salad
Apple Spinach Salad With Warm Maple Dressing
Apple and Spinach Salad With Sweet Seed Butter Dressing
Mixed Greens With Orange Juice Vinaigrette

name: Mangia Mangia Balsamic Vinaigrette on Baby Greens
directions: UP TO 3 DAYS AHEAD:
Place vinegars, water, oregano, basil, pepper and garlic in a large glass jar.
Shake to mix well.
Slowly add olive oils, stirring constantly to mix well.
Refrigerate until needed - bring to room temperature before serving and shake to mix.
TO SERVE:
Place desired amount of mixed baby greens in a large bowl.
Add dressing to coat and toss to mix well.
NOTE:
You can use this tangy dressing on any mixed green salad. The recipe makes enough dressing for several occasions.
Try drizzling the vinaigrette on steamed asparagus or toss with julienne zucchini.

name: Smoked Salmon and Blueberry Kahlua  Salad
directions: Combine all the dressing ingredients in a medium saucepan, mash with a fork and bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for five minutes.  Remove from heat and purée until dressing has a smooth consistency, then cool.

To assemble the salads, divide the spinach between 4 salad plates.  Place 3 oz. of smoked salmon on top of spinach.  Top each salad with 1 oz of crumbled goat cheese. top each salad with an equal amount of the red onion and walnuts.  Drizzle each salad with the cooled blueberry dressing.  ENJOY!

similar.name: Maple Grilled Corn, Blueberry and Apple Salad
Green Salad With Blue Cheese Dressing
Smoked Almond Salad With Honey and Oil Vinaigrette
Pear & Blue Cheese Walnut Salad W/ Pear Vinaigrette

name: Chico Inn Spinach Salad With Creamy Italian Dressing
directions: Cook bacon in medium skillet until crisp; transfer to paper towels, drain and crumble.
Blend mayonnaise, Italian seasoning,garlic and sugar in blender.
With machine running, gradually add oil alternately with vinegar in slow steady stream.
Gradually add half and half.
Toss spinach and onion in large bowl with enough dressing, to taste.
Divide between plates, sprinkle with bacon and egg and serve.

similar.name: Spinach Salad Dressing - Spicy and Fabulous
Cafe Green Salad by Melissa D'arabian

name: Strawberry Spring Salad
directions: DRESSING:.

Combine jam, vinegar, mustard, and pepper, in a small bowl, wehisking until blended.  Gradually whisk n oil m then add 2/4 cup water.  Continue whisking until smoothly blended.  Add additional water if a thinner consistency is desired.  Refrigerate until ready to use.
SALAD:.

Place salad greens, green onion, tangerine pieces,  strawberries, and sunflower seeds in al large salad bowl.  Add desired amount of vinaigrette.  Toss lightly.  Crumble blue cheese over top.  Serve immediately.
TIP:
Store remaining vinaigrette in refrigerator and use for nay salad.
Replace the blue cheese with cheese of your cheese, such as brie or goat cheese,.
Toss in your favorite nuts.

name: Chef-Boy-I-Be Illinois' Creamy Chive Salad Dressing
directions: In a bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, mayonnaise, chives, parsley, lemon juice and mustard.
Season with salt and pepper.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Blend sugar, vinegar, salt and mustard powder in small food processor.
Slowly add oil, blend until smooth.
Stir in poppy seeds.

name: Sun-Dried Tomato Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients except oil in a blender or food processor.  Pulse until all tomatoes are finely chopped.
Add oil, a little at a time.  Pulse until thick and well blended.

name: Homemade Ranch Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and whisk well.

name: Nicoise "petite"  Potato Salad
directions: IN a saucepan of salted boiling water, cook potatoes until tender, about 10 minutes; drain.  In a large bowl, combine potatoes, green beans, olives , onions, and tomatoes.
Whisk together vinegar and olive oil.  Pour over salad and toss gently.  Transfer to a platter.  Sprinkle cheese crumbles and tuna over the top.

similar.name: Greens W/ Beets, Haricots Verts & Goat Cheese Croutons
Roasted Fingerlings, Tomato Salad With Green Beans and Arugula

name: Won Ton Salad
directions: For Dressing:.
Mince onion and garlic in processor.
Blend in vinegar, soy sauce,sugar, mustard,ginger and worcestershire.
With machine running, gradually add peanut oil and sesame oil.
Stir in sesame seeds.
For Won Ton Strips:.
Heat oil in fryer or deep skillet to 375 degrees.
Add won ton strips in batches and cook until golden brown, about 1 minute; drain on paper towels.
(Can be prepared 1 day ahead. Store in airtight container at room temperature.).
For Salad:.
Combine chicken, ham, water chestnuts lettuce and spinach in large bowl.
Toss with enough dressing to coat.
Top with bean sprouts then won ton strips.
Serve, passing remaining dressing separately.

similar.name: Spinach, Mushroom and Red Onion Salad

name: Nif's Creamy Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Make a garlic paste. To make the paste, place the clove on a cutting board and chop or mince. Sprinkle the salt on the garlic; it will help break down the garlic. Put your knife on the garlic at a 40 degree angle, almost completely flat. Scrape the garlic with the side of your blade until you end up with a paste.
Mix all the ingredients in a bowl and chill in the fridge for about an hour.

similar.name: Creamy Low Fat Garlic Salad Dressing

name: Honey Mustard Vinaigrette for a Crowd
directions: In a mixing bowl (or your food processor), whisk together the vinegar and mustard.  You can adjust the vinegar as desired -- ie, if you prefer less tart dressing, slightly reduce the amount of vinegar and increase the mustard.
If you're using creamed honey, you'll have to warm it up to liquify it -- then allow it to cool a bit.  In a slow drizzle, whisk in the honey to the vinegar/mustard mixture.
Whisk in the oil, also in a slow drizzle.  You can adjust the oil at this point as well, adding about 3 1/2 cups then tasting and adjusting to your preference.
Add salt to taste and freshly ground pepper.

name: Irish Mist Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk mustard, liqueur, vinegar and honey in small bowl.
Whisk in oil in thin stream.
Season with salt and pepper, if desired.
Refrigerate in airtight container.

name: Light Salad Dressing (Fit Back Into Your Skinny Jeans)
directions: Mix all ingredients and enjoy.

name: Roasted Pear and Beet Salad
directions: Adjust oven rack to lower-middle position; heat oven to 350°F  Wrap each beet in foil and roast until fork can be inserted and removed eith little resistence, 1 to 1 1/2 hours; unwrap beets.  When cool enough to handle, peel and cut beets lengthwise into quarters; cut each quarter n half into wedges.
Peel and core pears.  Wrap in foil.  Roast in oven for 1/2 - 3/4 hour, or until fork tender.  When cool enough to handle, cut in slices.
Whisk togehter oil, vinegar, salt and pepper ot taste in small bowl.
Conbine beets, blue cheese, and watercress in large serving bowl.  Add pears and vinaigrette; toss gently to combine.  Serve immediately.

similar.name: Pear, Beet and Red Onion Salad
Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing
Spinach and Pear Salad With Pomegranate Dressing

name: Caesar's Salad Dressing
directions: When making leave out 1 cup oil until the end.  Blend for 2 minutes, then add the cup of oil, tabasco and Worcestershire.

name: Simple Market Salad With Tarragon Vinaigrette
directions: Combine shallot, vinegar, mustard, 1 teaspoons salt, and 1/4 teaspoons pepper, and let stand until shallot is soft, about 10 minutes.  Add oil in a slow, steady, stream, whisking until emulsified.
Whisk in 1 tablespoons tarragon. Season with additional salt and pepper.
Toss lettuce with remaining 3 tablespoons tarragon and the vinaigrette.  Season with salt and pepper if desired.

name: Heart-Friendly Vinaigrette
directions: Place garlic in pan and cover with water (about 1/2 c.) Bring to boil, reduce heat and simmer until garlic is tender - about 15 minute.
Reduce liquid to 2 T and increase heat for 3 minute
Pour through sieve into bowl and press garlic through, mashing, with wooden spoon.
Whisk in vinegar and add oil.

name: Poppy Seed Dressing
directions: Whisk together all ingredients.

name: Crazy Awesome Caesar Salad
directions: mix all together, including bacon, and set in the fridge for atleast 30 minutes to let the flavours combine.
Cut and clean salad, and mix all. Serve with croutons. You can then shred more parmesan cheese on top if you like.
To make croutons cut up a baguette of your choice into cubes, drizzle with olive oil, oregano, basil. salt and pepper and put in oven at 375 for about 6-8 minutes depending on your oven. Bon Apettit!

name: Buttermilk Dressing
directions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the ingredients.  Chill for 1 hour.  May be stored in refrigerator for up to 1 week.

name: "zippy" Italian Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar with a lid; cover and shake to mix thoroughly.
Let stand at least 2 hours to blend seasonings.
Shake well before using.
Great for lettuce salad, antipasti and for a chicken marinade.

name: Lindas Creole Vinaigrette
directions: Combine all the ingredients except the oil in a small bowl.
Gradually whisk in the oil until the mixture thickens.
It will keep in a covered jar in the fridge for up to ten days.

name: Marmie's " Better Than Catalina" Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a tightly sealed jar or container and shake like crazy for 2 - 3 minutes until smooth and blended.
Can also blend with  a hand held  blender instead if desired.

name: Hot Cajun Potato Salad
directions: Cook un-peeled potatoes in boiling water in saucepan until tender, almost 40 minute.
Fry bacon in skillet until crisp, about 10 minutes.
Remove, drain on paper toweling, crumble & reserve.
Cook onion in bacon drippings until partially tender, about 5 minutes.
Blend together sugar, flour, salt, pepper, water and vinegar, add to onion mixture.
Cook stirring constantly, until mixture thickens and bubbles, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Peel & slice potatoes, add potatoes to onion mixture, heat well, about 5 minutes.
Add radishes and toss.
Serve hot, topped with bacon.

name: Creole Vinaigrette
directions: Combine everything BUT the oil in a small bowl. Gradually whisk in the oil until the mixture thickens. It will keep in a covered jar in the fridge for up to ten days.

name: Salade Nicoise
directions: MAKE THE SALAD:  Place potatoes in a medium saucepan, and add enough cold water to cover potatoes by about 1 inch.  Add 1 tablespoon salt.  Bring to a boil.  Reduce heat, and simmer until tender, about 15 minutes, drain.  (cooked potatoes can be refrigerated in an airtight container for up to 1 day).  When cool enough to handle, halve lengthwise.

Place eggs in a small saucepan.  Add enough cold water to cover by about an inch.  Bring to a boil. Cover and remove from heat.  let stand 13 minutes.  Drain, and cool under cool running water.  (Boiled eggs can be refrigerated in an airtight container for up to 1 day).  Peel eggs, and cut into quarters.

Prepare ice-water bath.  Bring a pot of water to a boil.  Add remaining tablespoons of salt.  Add haricots verts, and cook until bright reen and just tender, about 1 minute.  Plunge beans into ice-water bath.  Drain.  (Beans can be refrigerated in an airtight container for up to 1 day).
MAKE THE DRESSING:
Whisk together vinegar, honey, salt and pepper.  Add oil in a slow, steady stream, whisking until emulsified.  Whisk in thyme.
ASSEMBLE THE SALAD:.
Arrange 2 lettuce leaves on each plate to form a cup.  Arrange potatoes, eggs, haricots verts, tuna, olives and anchovies in lettuce cups.  Drizzle with dressing and serve.

name: Cucumber, Cantaloupe, and Squash Salad
directions: Make the dressing:  Combvine the yogurt, lime zest and juice, salt, cumin and pepper.  Cover and refrigerste 20 minutes.
Make the salad:  Using a vegetable peeler or a mandolin, shave squashes and cucumber into wide ribbons, and refrigerate.
Just before serving, drain cucumbers, and squash ribbons and toss with cantaloupes - drizzle with dressing.

similar.name: Fruit Salad With Honey and Spice Dressing

name: Ajeen Grilled Tomato Vinaigrette
directions: Brush the tomatoes with some of the olive oil and grill until lightly charred and softened.
Cool, peel and finely dice.
Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend for 7 - 10 seconds and refrigerate overnight.

name: V8 V-Fusion Mango & Avocado Salad
directions: Beat the V8 V-Fusion, oil, vinegar, water, mustard, garlic and black pepper in a medium bowl with a whisk or a fork.
Divide the lettuce among 4 salad bowls.  Top with mango and avocodo slices.  Spoon 2 tablespoons vinaigrette over each salad.   GArnish with fresh raspberries.   Serve immediately.

similar.name: Abby's Grilled Prawn Mango Avocado Salad
Apple, Avocado Salad With Mandarin Dressing

name: Homemade Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix dry ingredients into wet ingredients.
Refrigerate.

name: Caesar Salad
directions: Crush garlic with a fork against the sides of the salad bowl.
Remove from the bowl.
Prepare Romaine lettice, tearing into bite sized pieces. Assemble the, onions, crutons and Parmesan.
Pour the vinegar, lemon juice, Worcestershire, mustard and seasons into the salad bowl.
Pour the oil into the mixture, in a slow stream, whisking constantly.
Add the lettuce and onions to the dressing, topssing gently just until the lettuce is coated. Sprinkle over the cheese and croutons. Serve at once.

similar.name: Caesar Salad from Salt Fat Acid Heat

name: Low Fat Buttermilk Peppercorn Dressing
directions: Place ALL ingredients in a blender, cover and process until smooth.
Pour in a container, cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.
Enjoy!

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Press garlis into a jar with a resealable lid.  Add remaining ingredients and 1 tbsp cold water.  Cover tightly and shake well until combined.  Taste and adjust salt and pepper if needed.  Can be refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.

name: Ajeen Beckford Basic Blender Vinaigrette
directions: Place the garlic in the blender and set on low. Pulse to chop the garlic. Add the mustard, vinegar, salt and pepper.  Blend for 15 to 20 seconds.  With the blender running, add the oils in a slow steady stream taking about 20 seconds.  Then blend for 20 seconds longer until emulsified.
Change the basic vinaigrette by adding different herbs, sun-dried tomatoes or pesto.
Try using a little lime juice and honey for a honey-mustard vinaigrette.
Ajeen Beckford Distinguished Jamaican Chef.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk ingredients together.  Pour on 1 head of chopped romaine lettuce.

name: Sweet Garlic Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and stir together thoroughly or mix in a blender.

name: Barbecue Ranch Dressing
directions: Whisk the garlic, mayonnaise, buttermilk, and 2 tablespoons barbecue sauce together in a medium bowl.  Add the remaining ingredients and whisk until smooth.  Taste and add more barbecue sauce if desired and season with salt and pepper.  Cover and chill for at least 1 hour.

name: Herb Salad Dressing Claudio's
directions: In a blender or food processor fitted with the steel blade blend in all ingredients except oil.
With motor running add 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons each of olive oil and the safflower oil in a stream.
Blend until mixture is smooth and thick.
If the dressing separates, beat it gradually into a large egg yolk. Makes about 3 1/4 cups.

name: French Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a large bottle, or blender.  Add oil, then soup, then vinegar,  Shake well before using,
Keep in refrigerator.

name: Better Homes and Gardens Honey Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: In a screw top jar, combine, mustard, oil, lemon juice, honey and garlic. Cover and shake well.
Serve immediately or cover and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Shake well before serving.

name: Buttermilk Garlic Dressing or Dip
directions: Mash garlic with the salt until its paste-like. You can use a mortar and pestel or just place your knife parallel to the cutting board and mash.

Combine all ingredients and mix well. If the dressing is too thick, add more buttermilk 1 tablespoon at a time until desired consistency. Refrigerate at least one hour for flavors to combine. You'll never buy store dressing again.

name: Phyllis's Blue Cheese Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients by hand.
Store this dressing in a Hellmann's mayonnaise jar and it will keep fresh for weeks.

name: Sour Cream Dressing for Coleslaw
directions: Mix together the first five ingredients.
Mix with the cabbage mix just before serving.

name: Green Goddess Dressing- Chez Panisse
directions: Blend first 7 ingredients in processor until coarse puree forms.
With machine running, gradually add oil through feed tube; blend well. Transfer mixture to bowl; whisk in cream.
Add parsley, tarragon, cilantro, basil, and shallot; whisk to combine.
Season dressing to taste with salt and pepper.
Cover and chill at least 3 hours (dressing will separate if not chilled). Let stand at room temperature 20 minutes and rewhisk before serving.

name: Sam Choy's Garlic Ranch Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and whisk until thoroughly blended.
Chill.

name: Sam Choy's Island French Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients except oil in a blender on puree setting for about 1 minute, then begin adding oil slowly.
Readjust seasonings and blend for about 2 minutes after adding all the oil.
Total blending time will be 3 to 4 minutes.
Refrigerate.

similar.name: Sam Choy's Garlic Ranch Salad Dressing

name: "even People Who Don't Like Blue Cheese Will Like This&quot
directions: Add all ingredients and mix until blended, I like to use a food processor for this.
Pour into a sealed container and keep refrigerated.

similar.name: The Best Blue Cheese Dressing You'll Ever Make

name: Chapel Hill Green Goddess Dressing
directions: Put onion, garlic, parsley leaves, anchovy fillets, tarragon, tarragon vinegar, and mayonnaise into blender and blend for one minute.  If not salty enough add another anchovy fillet.
Serve over lettuce, as a dip for raw vegetables, or over a tossed salad.  Also good over cold seafood.

name: Scrumptious Fresh Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Dump ingredients into a jar or container with a tight-fitting lid. Shake until mixed. Pour on green salad.

name: Maple Ginger Vinaigrette
directions: In a blender or food processor, combine the garlic, shallot, ginger, mustard, soy sauce, maple syrup, and balsamic vinegar until liquified.
With motor running on low, pour the olive and canola oils gradually through the hole in the blender lid or food processor feed tube and blend until emulsified.
Store refrigerated or use immediately.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing (Cook Yourself Thin)
directions: Combine all ingredients into a small mixing bowl and whisk together.
Taste and season (with salt and pepper).

name: Maple Mustard Salad Dressing
directions: In a 4 cup measure mix mustard with hot water.
Wisk in syrup, vinegar salt and pepper.
Wisk in olive oil.
Serve on your favorite salad.

name: Soya Dressing for Mum's Rice Salad
directions: Combine all ingredients.

name: Ranch Salad Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, ix mayonnaise, sour cream, buttermilk, green onions, onion powder, parsley, garlic, paprika, cayenne, salt and pepper.
refrigerate 6 hours or overnight.

name: Russian Tomato Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a container and shake before using.

name: Dill.....dill Sauce
directions: In a small bowl mix the sour cream,mayonnaise,dill,tarragon, and salt.Cover and refrigerate, (overnight if possible) until needed.

similar.name: Cucumber Dill Dip or Salad Dressing

name: Horseradish..... Horseradish Sauce
directions: .In a small bowl, mix all ingredients well. Cover and refrigerate over night if possible.

name: Greek Dressing
directions: Mix and serve. Mmmmmmm.

name: Ginger-Sesame Vinaigrette
directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in a medium bowl, stirring with a whisk until smooth. Stir in green onions and the remaining ingredients.

name: Ranch Dressing and Dip Spice Mix
directions: measure all ingredients carefully,,,,,not “rounded” teaspoons etc.
Dump all ingredients into a blender or food processor ( I use the Vita-Mix) and blend until fine powdered.)  You will get about 1 ½ to 2 cups of mix.
Store mix in freezer, with a note on it about how to use, as below:.
TO USE: The day before serving….
Mix:.
1  level tablespoon measure of the spice mix.
1 cup of mayo
1 cup of buttermilk. ***** NOTE********(use sour cream instead of buttermilk if you are making dip)
Whisk all thoroughly and store in frig.

name: Perfect Creamy Coleslaw Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in Cuisinart, and blend.
Or in a bowl, and beat….
Store in frig, leave overnight to blend flavors and soften onions before using.
Mix desired amount into shredded cabbage with sliced green onions.

similar.name: Tastier-Than- Heck Oil and Vinegar Coleslaw Dressing

name: Fruit Salad Dressing
directions: mix together and pour over cut friut.
Stir to coat.

name: Miso-Mustard Dressing
directions: Mix miso and mustard together until smooth.
Add remaining ingredients and beat with a fork until lump-free.

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!

name: Ginger Lime Vinaigrette
directions: Process all ingredients in a blender; be sure to use plenty of black pepper.
Add 1 tablespoon, or so, warm water to reach desired consistency.
Let rest in refrigerator 1 day before using so flavors meld.

name: Basic Vinaigrette
directions: Combine oil, vinegar and salt and pepper in a blender and blend about 30 seconds until emulified.
Taste and add more vinegar, by teaspoons, if desired.
Add shallot and pulse until shallot is minced.

name: Basic Buttermilk Salad Dressing
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar with a tight fitting lid and shake vigorously.
Taste for seasonings; serve.

name: Basic Creamy Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, except shallot, in a blender and process until emulsified, about 30 seconds.
Taste and add more vinegar by teaspoons, if desired.
Add shallot and process, by turning on and off, until minced.
Taste and adjust seasoning.

name: Western Salad Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Laura's Family Secret Creamy Italian Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in a blender and blend well.  Serve with a chopped salad with your choice of ingredients (toss the salad with the dressing and don't just pour over the salad).

Suggestions for chopped salad:  chopped mixed lettuces & baby spinach, carrots, tomatoes, artichoke hearts or garbanzo beans, cucumbers, red/green/yellow bell peppers, jicama, fresh basil or herbs of choice.  Chop all ingredients in a uniform size (I use kitchen shears to chop the lettuces) and toss.  You can do this ahead and assemble the salad at the last minute, having stored the ingredients in ziplock bags in the fridge.  Toss with dressing immediately before serving.

name: Homemade Sour Cream Ranch Dressing
directions: Whisk buttermilk into the mayonnaise and sour cream, whisking until smooth.
Add all other ingredients and whisk until smooth.
Season to taste. I don't add salt because of the mayonnaise.
Serve immediately.
Keep unused portion refrigerated.

name: Pear & Blue Cheese Walnut Salad W/ Pear Vinaigrette
directions: Halve and core the pears. Slice thin, brush with lemon juice to prevent browning. This may be done up to 60 minutes ahead.
To arrange the salads: line plates with salad greens.
Fan pear slices atop each serving.
Sprinkle with some blue cheese and candied nuts.
Drizzle with dressing. Serve extra on the side.
CANDIED WALNUTS / PECANS.
Line a baking sheet with foil. Butter or spray with a nonstick oil.
In a heavy skillet, over medium heat, combine: 1 C walnuts, 1/2 C sugar and 2 T butter.
Cook until sugar starts to melt, DO NOT  stir. Reduce heat to low and cook 'til sugar is golden brown. Stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and pour onto prepared baking sheet. Cool completely. Break into clusters.
PEAR NECTAR VINAIGRETTE.
In a screw top jar, combine:
white wine vinegar,  oil,  pear nectar, Dijon mustard, salt, pepper.
Cover and shake well.
Store in refrigerator for up to one week.
Shake before serving.
Makes about 1°C.

similar.name: Pear, Beet and Red Onion Salad
Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing
Mandarin Orange and Pear Salad With Toasted Pecan Vinaigrette
Spinach and Pear Salad With Pomegranate Dressing

name: Val's Vinaigrette
directions: Emulsify with an immersion blender.

name: Iceberg Lettuce Wedges With Creamy Garlic Dressing
directions: Remove any tough outer leaves from lettuce heads and cut each into quarters. Remove tough stem end and core. Put quarters on chilled plates.

Combine sour cream, mayonnaise, vinegar and garlic in a food processor or blender. Add 2 tablespoons cold water and process until blended and smooth. Add parsley, salt and pepper; pulse briefly to mix. Cover and chill until ready to serve (can be made up to 2 days in advance).
To serve, spoon some dressing on each wedge and sprinkle with scallions, if desired. Serve remaining dressing on the side.

name: Cafe Green Salad by Melissa D'arabian
directions: Rub the inside of a large wooden bowl with the garlic clove and then either discard it or save it for another use.
Put the mustard in the bowl and whisk in the balsamic vinegar and soy sauce (a French secret!) vigorously for about 10 seconds to get a creamy consistency.
Season with salt and pepper, to taste.
Drizzle in the olive oil as slowly as possible with 1 hand while whisking as quickly as possible with the other hand to emulsify.
Lay the salad greens on top of the dressing and toss just before serving.

similar.name: Mixed Greens With Orange Juice Vinaigrette

name: Date & Fig Salad Dressing
directions: Slightly roast or soften garlic first, otherwise too bitter.
Whirl all the ingredients using a hand held electric blender; mine is a Cuisinart 200 watts Smart Stick that comes with a cup for the ingredients.
Very thick; experiment with different vinegars (red wine is also good).

similar.name: Cranberry Date Salad With Citrus-Honey Vinaigrette

name: Salsa Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix together well.
Let set at least 1 hour for flavors to blend.

similar.name: Taco Salad Dressing - Fast and Easy

name: Petersons Corners Patricks Ruby Red Salad Dressing (Copycat)
directions: Whisk all ingredients togeather until sugar is dissolved and oil well emulsified with other ingredients.
Put into 2 pint jars.
Refrigerate overnight allowing ingredients to marry.
Take jar of dressing out of refrigerator and allow it to sit for at least 15 to  30 minutes.
Shake jar well prior to serving over a nice green salad.

similar.name: Diane's Easy Thousand Island Salad Dressing

name: Ginger Lime Salad Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients in a glass jar or plastic container.
Shake well until dressing is combined.
Refrigerate until ready to use.
Shake well before serving.
Keeps well in refrigerator.

name: Seasoned  Salad Dressing
directions: 1. Combine all ingredients in a bowl and whisk.
2. Store extra dressing in the refrigerator, covered.

name: Copycat Recipe of Bob Evans Colonial Dressing
directions: Directions:.
Process water and cornstarch in a blender on high speed for 1 minute or until smooth. Transfer to a 2 1/2-quart saucepan.

Cook mixture over medium-high heat, stirring constantly. Add each of the remaining ingredients, stirring well after each addition. Cook, stirring, until mixture begins to thicken and become quite smooth.
Let cool completely before pouring into a refrigerator container. Cap tightly. Keeps for about 6 weeks. Do not freeze.

Creamy Italian Colonial: After preparing the previous recipe (let cool first), add 1 tablespoon pre pared mustard, 1 tablespoon light corn syrup, 1 tablespoon dried oregano leaves, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder and 1/2 cup mayonnaise. Stir to combine well. Refrigerate. Do not freeze.
Catalina Colonial: To prepared recipe, add 1 cup ketchup and 1/2 cup sweet pickle relish.
Creamy Onion Dressing: To prepared recipe, stir in 1 carton (8 ounces) onion chip dip. Do not freeze.
Slaw Dressing: To prepared recipe, stir in 1/2 cup sour cream and 1/2 cup mayonnaise. Use within 1 week. Do not freeze.

name: Salad With Walnut Vinaigrette and Warm Chevre
directions: For the vinaigrette:
Combine the vinegar, mustard and honey in a small mixing bowl; whisk to combine thoroughly.
Add the oils in a slow, steady stream, whisking continuously, until the vinaigrette is creamy and emulsified.
Add the parsley and season to taste with salt and pepper; set aside until needed.
For the Chevre:.
Preheat oven to 400 degrees Farenheit.
Finely chop parsley, chives and walnuts.
Season chevre lightly with salt and pepper.
Spread parsley/chive/walnut mixture on a plate and roll chevre in it, pressing down lightly to coat it completely.
Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 10 minutes.
Slice chevre into 6 evenly sliced rounds and place on a parchment-lined baking sheet and place in oven until cheese is heated through -- about 5 minutes.
For the salad:.
Combine the salad greens, onion, walnuts and berries and toss gently to mix.
Add just enough dressing to lightly coat each leaf.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Mound salad on plates and place one or two rounds of warm chevre on top of each salad and serve immediately.

name: Lemon-Herb Dressing With Mint and Tarragon
directions: Whisk together all ingredients until blended.
Lemon-Herb Dressing With Chives and Tarragon: Substitute chopped fresh chives for mint. Proceed with recipe as directed.
Lemon-Herb Dressing With Basil: Substitute 1/3 cup chopped fresh basil for mint and tarragon. Proceed with recipe as directed. Season with salt to taste.

name: Caesar Salad Dressing
directions: Dump all ingredients in food processor, blender, or Magic Bullet.
Process until well blended.  Serve over salad right before serving.
Enjoy.

name: Heather’s Miso Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together or blend in blender to emulsify.  One serving is 2T.

name: Red Lobster's Caesar Dressing
directions: Combine the mayonnaise and 2 dressings with the white wine and vinegar.  Whisk until smooth and creamy.
Add the sour cream.  Whisk to blend.
Cover tightly and refrigerate.  Dressing will keep one month.

name: Ramen Cabbage Salad
directions: Break uncooked Ramen noodles into small pieces. Save chicken flavoring packet for dressing.
Pour cole slaw mix, broken Ramen noodle pieces, almonds, sunflower seeds, and green onion into a big bowl.
Combine vinegar, olive oil, sugar, and chicken flavoring together and pour over cole salw mix. Toss to mix and serve.

name: Lemony Fruit Salad
directions: For dressing, combine the sugar, lemon juice, lemon peel and poppy seeds in a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat for 5-6 minutes or until sugar is dissolved, stirring occasionally. Remove from the heat; cool.
In a large bowl, combine the apples, oranges, carrot and raisins. Add dressing and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for 4 hours. Serve in a lettuce-lined bowl if desired.

similar.name: Apple Pear Salad W/ Poppy Seed Dressing

name: Whale's Rib Raw Bar Whale Juice (Honey Mustard)
directions: Combine the first five ingredients and whisk together.
Add water to adjust the consistency.  The whale juice should be thinned out a bit to the point that it can easily coat the food which it is being served with.
Refrigerate overnight for best results.
Enjoy.

name: Mayonnaise Nicoise
directions: Mix all ingredients together in food processor until smooth.
Stir in additional capers to taste.

name: Artichoke Parmesan Dressing
directions: Combine all of the ingredients in a blender or food processor.
Process until well combined.

name: Msg Free Ranch Dressing
directions: Mix all ingredients together in  medium sized bowl with a whisk.  Taste and adjust spices to your tastes.
For a thicker dip, use less cream and more sour cream.

name: Gold Salad Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients in the blender except for the oil and begin to blend.
Slowly add the oil until the dressing is completely blended.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: Mellow Mushroom Esperanza Dressing
directions: Put all ingredients except oil and parsley in the bowl of a food processor and process until smooth.
With motor running, drizzle in oil through feed tube.
Stir in parsley by hand.

name: Asian Dressing
directions: In a jar mix shake or whisk all ingredients together.
Store in refrigerator.
Serve over salad greens.

name: Sweet & Tangy Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: Measure EVOO into a blender.
Clean garlic and crush with the flat blade of you knife. Put into EVOO and allow to stand for 20 minute.
Add remaining ingredients.
Blend, pour into a jar and refrigerate.
Allow to come to room temperature before serving.

name: Creamy Parmesan Ranch Dressing
directions: In a large bowl, whisk together all of the ingredients. Make sure everything is well distributed. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving. It's best when made the day before so the ingredients have time to blend.

name: Best Homemade Thousand Island Dressing
directions: Mix every thing together. Can be served immediately although is better if it chills for an hour or more.

name: Nut Tree French Dressing
directions: Mix salt, pepper, and garlic granules or juice. Stir in lemon juice and red and white vinegars. Add salad oil and slightly beaten egg white, and beat throughly. Store in covered jar in refrigerator. Shake or beat well before using.
If a milder dressing is desired, add 1/4 to 1/3 cup more oil to this dressing.

name: Apricot Vinaigrette
directions: In blender, combine first 5 ingredients; blend until smooth. With blender running, slowly add loive oill; blend until smooth.

name: Oriental Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients, except green onions, in food processor until well blended a minute or two.  Best served hot poured over spinach salad with bacon, green onions to garnish and hard boiled eggs (opp). Can be used cold too.

name: Colorful Vegetarian Spinach Salad
directions: Thoroughly blend all dressing ingredients, store in fridge until salad is ready.
Gently wash, drain, dry, tear spinach leaves.
(Drying leaves in a salad spinner works best).
Arrange spinach in individual serving dishes.
Nicely arrange remaining salad ingredients on top of spinach.
Drizzle dressing on each salad, and toss to gently coat leaves.

name: Gluten Free Mustard Marmalade Salad Dressing
directions: 1. Combine vinegar, orange fruit spread, mustard and agave in a vita-mix and blend on high for 20 seconds.
2. With motor on low speed, drizzle in olive oil.
3. This will (hopefully) create an emulsion, you can see in photo of dressing above that it is thick.
4. Toss over salad greens.
5. Serve.

similar.name: Low-Cal, Low-Fat Creamy Mustard Dressing!  Delicious!
Almost-Empty Dijon Mustard Jar Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

name: Creamy Ginger Lime Dressing
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well with wisk.
Refrigerate 1 hour before use.

name: Caesar Dressing (From Carnival Cruise Lines)
directions: Place egg yolks in a bowl and mix at high speed until creamy.
Add oil slowly while beating and an emulsion starts to form.
Continue adding oil in a slow stream until you have all the oil blended in and mixture looks like mayonnaise.
Add rest of the ingredients and adjust seasoning to taste.
Chill dressing until ready to use.

name: Napa Salad
directions: Toss together lettuce, cabbage and sunflower seeds.
In skillet, brown and strain: margarine, rice noodles, almonds.  Stir constantly until crisp.  This only takes a few minutes.
Place noodle mixture on the bottom of salad bowl.  Add greens.  Top with dressing.  Top with strips of grilled or fried chicken breast.

name: Creamy Sweet Onion & Bacon Salad Dressing (Low-Fat)
directions: The first step is to caramelize your onions. Once done, combine with the first seven ingredients and blend with a immersion blender of food processor.  Quickly warm up your bacon in a pan right before serving and mix together.  Tastes wonderful with a spinach salad & crumbled light goat cheese.

name: Honey Garlic Balsamic Vinaigrette
directions: In a small bowl, stir together garlic, honey and salt to taste until well mixed.
Add vinegar, then oil, whisking vigorously till blended.
Let stand 10 minutes to allow flavours to blend.
Remove garlic slices.

name: Citrus Dressing (Claim Jumper's)
directions: Combine ingredients well and refrigerate to meld flavors.
Before serving, whisk well to combine again.

similar.name: My Favorite Oil and Vinegar Dressing

name: "hold the Mayo" Blue Cheese Salad Dressing
directions: Whisk together first 4 ingredients (garlic, oregano, sugar, vinegar).
Very slowly add the olive oil while still whisking.
Carefully stir in the blue cheese, then give it a taste test.  Add salt & pepper if choose to do so.
Pour dressing over some nice, crispy lettuce, like romaine hearts or an iceberg wedge  -- enjoy!

name: Carole's Coleslaw
directions: Mix the dressing ingredients together well in a bowl, using a whisk.
Pour into a jar with a lid and refrigerate for several hours to blend the flavors.

In a large bowl add the coleslaw mix, dressing and raisins and mix gentley until the coleslaw mix is coated evenly with the dressing. Let stand in the refrigerator for about 30 minutes and then serve.

name: Honky Tonk Salad Dressing
directions: Blend all ingredients in blender. Adjust seasoning to taste.

name: Dill & Parmesan Salad Dressing
directions: Blend mayonnaise and sour cream together in a medium sized bowl.  Mix in remaining ingredients till well incorporated.  Cover and chill 6 hours before serving.
Serve atop any green salad.  A fantastic serving suggestion is topping off your salad with baby cooked & chilled shrimp.  Your family will greatly be impressed.

name: Asian Style Sesame Dressing
directions: Mix all and let sit for 2 hours.

name: Oriental Three Lettuce Salad
directions: Add vinegar, sugar, and soy sauce into a jar and shake until sugar is dissolved. Add oil and shake. Sometimes I cut back on the amount of oil and sugar and use 1/2 cup of each. Just a matter of taste.
Saute ramen noodles and walnuts in margarine until slightly golden. Drain and cool.

Combine lettuce, green onion, and topping in a large bowl. When ready to serve, shake dressing and pour on salad, then serve. Do not put dressing on until ready to serve. Dressing will being breaking down the lettuce.

name: Creamy Horseradish Dressing
directions: In a small bowl, stir together all ingredients. Cover and refrigerate until needed.
Use for Layered Dungeness Crab Salad.

name: Soy Ginger Vinaigrette
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar.  Cover and shake until combined.
Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

name: Blueberry Vinaigrette
directions: In a mixing bowl, add mustard, salt, pepper and garlic.  Crush the garlic while mixing the ingredients.
Add balsamic vinegar, maple syrup, blueberry juice and oil.  Whisk until it emulsifies.  Refrigerate for 30 minutes.  For best results, make the previous day.

name: Potato Salad Dressing
directions: Melt the butter in a small saucepan.
Add the celery seed, salt and pepper and mix well.
In a bowl, combine the vinegar, sour cream, mayonnaise, green onion and parsley.
Add the butter mixture and mix well.
Chill and add to2 - 3 pounds of cooked cooled potatoes.
Chill until ready to serve.

name: Cranberry Date Salad With Citrus-Honey Vinaigrette
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting cover, combine all vinaigrette ingredients; cover adn shake well.
PREPARE SALAD:  In a small bowl, toss cranberries with sugar; let it for 15 minutes.  In another bowl, toss apple slices in 1/4 cup vinaigrette.

In a large bowl, combine romaine, apple slices, cranberries, dates and walnuts.  Refrigerate.  When ready to serve, give the remaining vinaigrette another shake and drizzle over salad.  Season with pepper, toss well, and transfer to a salad bowl.

similar.name: Mixed Green Salad With Honey Orange Dressing

name: Macaroni Tuna Salad - Sherri Griffin
directions: Cook shell macaroni following package directions.  Drain thoroughly.
Drain tuna & break into pieces in a large bowl.
Combine tuna with macaroni & pickle relish.
In another bowl, blend maynnaise, russion dressing, lemon juice, salt, pepper, & onions.
Add to tuna mixture & toss lightly.

similar.name: Tuna and Cannellini Salad With Lemon

name: Crisp Goat Cheese Salad
directions: Heat the broiler.  Brush a baking sheet with oil.  Set aside.

Place the potatoes in a medium saucepan, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil.  Simmer until fork-tender, 20-25 minutes.  Rinse the potatoes under cold water until cool enough to handle, and cut into  1-inch chunks.

In a shallow bowl, whisk the egg with 1/4 teaspoons each salt and pepper.  Place breadcrumbs in another shallow bowl.  Slice goat cheese into 8 rounds, and pat each disk into a disk about 1/2-inch thick.  Dip disks in the egg and then in the breadcrumbs, coating evenly.  Place on prepared baking sheet.
In a large bowl, whisk together vinegar, mustard, and 1/4 teaspoons each salt and pepper.  Slowly add 1/4 cup oil, whisking to emulsify.  Set aside.
Brush disks lightly with remaining 1/4 cup oil, and broil until golden and crisp, about 5 minutes.
Toss mesculin and potatoes in the dressing.  Spoon salads onto plates, and top each with two cheese disks.

To make the dressing, purée all of the dressing ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth. Transfer to a skillet.

name: Cobb Salad With Chipotle Black Bean Vinaigrette
directions: Preparation:
To make the dressing, purée all of the dressing ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth.
Toss the corn and red pepper in a small bowl; set aside.
Toss the tomato, cucumber and onion in a separate bowl; set aside.
Make a bed of arugula or watercress on a large platter. Top 1/3 of the greens with the corn mixture.
Place the tomato and cucumber next to the corn, covering another 1/3 of the greens.

Cover the remaining greens with the sliced chicken; top the chicken with crumbled bacon. Drizzle half the dressing over top, and scatter the diced avocado overall. Serve the remaining dressing on the side.

name: French Dressing
directions: Put 3/4 cup of good tarragon or basil vinegar into a quart mason jar. With needle, string a garlic clove on a long white thread. Drop garlic into vinegar so that the ends of thread dangle outside the jar. Add 2 Tblsp. salt, 1 Tblsp. dry mustard, 1/2 Tblsp. pepper. Cover jar and let stand several days, shaking frequently. After a few days, discard garlic and fill jar with good olive oil. You will need almost 3 cups of oil. Shake well. Add more salt and vinegar if you like a sharp taste. Vary French Dressing by adding to taste; worcestershire sauce, chili or tabasco sauce, herbs and spices of all kinds. Make a quart of this dressing at a time and store in a cool place. It will keep for months and improve as it stands.

name: Fruit or Coleslaw Boiled Dressing
directions: Heat vinegar in a double boiler; add salt and pepper.
Beat egg yolks in a bowl; pour the warm vinegar over the egg yolks while stirring.
Return to double boiler; cook and stir until creamy.
Remove from heat and add the cream; stir well.
Refrigerate until serving.
Can be used with raw fruit or coleslaw.

name: Pecan Encrusted Goat Cheese Salad W/Maple Vinaigrette Dressing
directions: Preheat oven to 350ºF.
Spread pecans on a baking sheet and oven toast 10-15 minutes.
Let cool and chop fine.
Roll the log of goat cheese in toasted pecans.
Slice into 8 slices and set aside.
Whisk together the maple syrup, balsamic vinegar, olive oil, salt and pepper.
Dress greens 'lightly' with vinaigrette and toss; remaining dressing will be drizzled on each serving.
Transfer greens to a serving plate.
Add 2 slices of the pecan-encrusted goat cheese to each serving.
Top salad with the Granny Smith matchsticks.
Drizzle each serving with the remaining vinaigrette and serve; drizzle some around the plate for presentation.

name: Elegant  Almond  Rice  Salad
directions: In a large saucepan, heat water and rice to boiling. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes. Drain excess liquid from rice.
Drain artichoke hearts, reserving marinade. Halve artichoke hearts and add to rice with peas, green pepper, green onions, tomatoes, reserved marinade, and only HALF the dressing.
Toss well. Cover and chill. Just before serving, toss again and taste. Add some of remaining dressing, if desired. Sprinkle with almonds and serve.
DRESSING PREPARATION:
In a blender, puree mustard, olive oil, lemon juice garlic and herbs. With motor running, drizzle in oil to create an emulsion. Pour in a lidded jar and shake well. Refrigerate until ready to use.
